[2026-04-08T02:39:52.132000+00:00] visible_fix (id=1491266299261620297)
    I use minimax m2.7 , i built a natural language harness that basically commands my hermes agent. Lets me keep all the features and updates without breaking shit and i got over 3000 logs of potential improvements (most failures but yknow 3K gets data) , from that i can tell you maybe you want a new harness logic or even cooler a custom weight loader that loads custom data on how you want the agent to perform for you so it would be api -> hermes -> custom weights -> hermes -> answer. 
    
    If not. I think you want a simple routing table type skill. So it would be a set of ephemeral prompts table that agent goes through before next answer or reply. Then you have it self optimize the ephemeral prompt table as it grows ? Not sure I understand the concept that well. 
    
    Maybe api then your ephemeral requesites then hermes ?

    some issue with custom provider:  Custom endpoints can expose different wire protocols, but Hermes still treated some "custom" paths as if the transport were effectively fixed.[openai chat completion format]    my pr make the custom support 3 protocols:chat_completions
    codex_responses
    anthropic_messages  https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/5697
    [embed] feat(custom): add explicit API type selection for custom endpoints ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/5697

[2026-04-08T02:49:07.018000+00:00] meow034773 (id=1491268626622451933)
    the custom endpoint is not alway one format  user can specify it   if not specified  back to original behavior

[2026-04-08T02:49:58.881000+00:00] meow034773 (id=1491268844151636068)
    and i found :Hermes already had one correct Responses conversion path in run_agent.py, but agent/auxiliary_client.py still kept its own adapter logic.
    That split was fragile: one path normalized tool transcripts correctly, while another could still replay chat-style role=tool messages into Responses input[]. Providers that implement the OpenAI Responses contract reject that shape with a 400 because tool is not a valid input role there.   https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/5711
    [embed] feat: centralize Responses API input conversion across agent paths ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/5711

[2026-04-08T02:55:35.370000+00:00] visible_fix (id=1491270255488532551)
    Yeah. I generally tend to make things complicated. I enjoy the chaotic rush. The debugging isnt nearly half as fun though lol 
    
    My simplest approach would be custom skill. Hermes is great at it already. Like spend some time going over what you want. Have AI argue against you. Get depressed from the accuracy. Then force it to counter argue what it just pointed out as issues then finally. You turn the final synthesis into a skill. Have it remembered in memory for natural tool calling then you just go "hey use skill [x], want you to analyze..."

[2026-04-08T03:00:35.999000+00:00] ahuman_exfer (id=1491271516418084965)
    built a settlement layer for autonomous agent-to-agent payments — no accounts, no API keys, just keypairs. any agent with an Ed25519 key can transact
    
    deterministic fees (computable before execution), guaranteed script termination, HTLC support for atomic swaps between agents. designed specifically for the case where agents need to pay each other without a human in the loop
    
    task server running if anyone wants to point a Hermes agent at it and see what happens: http://82.221.100.201:8080/api/v1/tasks — agents earn a small amount by solving tasks, claimed on-chain
    
    EXFER_PASS="yourpass" exfer init --passphrase-env EXFER_PASS --json to spin up a node
    
    spec and agent guide at https://exfer.org. curious what payment primitives people are actually reaching for when building multi-agent systems

[2026-04-08T13:28:15.491000+00:00] meow034773 (id=1491429471776149657)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6159  add a /spawn command just for this case:Why /spawn is needed
    Hermes already had two related but different workflows:
    
    /background runs a detached async task in a separate session
    /branch clones the current session, but switches the user into that new branch immediately
    What was missing was the third workflow:
    
    preserve the current conversation state like /branch
    run asynchronously like /background
    keep the user on the current session instead of switching away
    That matters for real tasks such as:
    
    "continue this investigation in the background"
    "take the current context and explore one branch of it"
    "use everything we've discussed so far, but don't interrupt this chat"
    Without /spawn, users have to choose between losing context (/background) and leaving the current session (/branch).
    [embed] feat: add /spawn for background child sessions from current context... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6159

[2026-04-08T13:28:45.145000+00:00] meow034773 (id=1491429596154036224)
    it make my life much easier

[2026-04-08T13:29:21.201000+00:00] meow034773 (id=1491429747383861469)
    just /spawn and forget  and the agent will report 【keeping the context   NO NEED TO REPLANNING】

[2026-04-08T19:24:26.307000+00:00] w0lfgang88 (id=1491519107475177642)

    I ship and maintain it myself. Faster to iterate, full control, but less discoverable.

[2026-04-08T23:26:37.508000+00:00] teknium (id=1491580055749660712)
    Some concerns from hermes:
    ② **Placement is reasonable but the single-winner design is fragile.** `results[0]` takes only the first plugin's return value. If two plugins register for this hook, the second one is silently ignored. Hook callback ordering is unspecified, so which plugin "wins" is nondeterministic. Not a bug per se, but a design limitation that would need addressing before real adoption.
    
    ③ **Bare `except Exception: pass`** — Appropriate fail-open for production safety, but makes debugging impossible for plugin developers. Every other `invoke_hook` call site already gets per-callback exception logging from the PluginManager itself, so the outer try/except here is redundant — `invoke_hook` already wraps each callback in its own try/except with `logger.warning`. The outer catch only guards against `invoke_hook` itself failing (import error, etc.), which is fine but the bare `pass` means you'd never know.
    
    ④ **No tests included.** Test plan is checkboxes in the description but no actual test code.
    
    ⑤ **Removes trailing newline from both files** — cosmetic noise.

[2026-04-09T00:36:46.579000+00:00] javxq (id=1491597709872857228)

As I've noted in a comment on the PR, these issues were partially fixed in an earlier pr (5974), but just got reverted again to make tests pass in a pr you just merged (6294).
    
    My pr implements a better fix and adjusts the associated tests to properly handle the new changes. So feel free to review when you have time. Thanks.

[2026-04-09T02:25:18.369000+00:00] malaiwah (id=1491625022299832431)
    Heads up.
    
    Small terminal-sandbox bug worth a look: _cleanup_task_resources unconditionally tears down the sandbox at end of every turn, even for persistent backends (docker/daytona/modal). /workspace, ~/.config, sub-agent CLI auth — all wiped between turns. terminal.lifetime_seconds never fires. Within a single turn it's fine; only turn boundaries break.
    
    Archaeology: idle reaper landed Nov 2 2025. Two days later, an unconditional cleanup_vm() was added to plug a Morph backend leak — fair for Morph, blanket-applied to all backends. The Morph backend has since been removed; the hook outlived it. Then in Feb 2026 a container_persistent: true knob shipped, defaulting on, already a no-op the day it landed.
    
    Net: ~5 months of docs/config promising persistence the code hasn't honored.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6369
    PR (36 lines): https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6370
    [embed] Persistent terminal sandboxes (docker/daytona/modal) are torn down ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6369
    [embed] fix(terminal): persistent sandbox envs survive between turns by mal... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6370

[2026-04-09T02:54:06.690000+00:00] dannyboy676767 (id=1491632271403520132)
    I opened a PR for Discord users. Been using it to help me stay organized
    
    Tl;dr when using a discord forum, the adapter was picking up the content of the forum topic textbox, but not actually using it for anything. That was inconsistent with how normal channels and threads work, which does pass the channel topic into a thread as a system message at the start
    
    Bringing that into a forum allows me to do things like making a forum for each coding project and using its description as a pre-hook to give the agent context about the project in each feature thread I create. 
    
    Prior to this PR I was having to copy and paste a boilerplate prompt with my desired context/instructions into each thread.
    
    Minor QOL I thought I would send upstream as it was an inconsistency after all
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6377
    [embed] feat(discord): inherit forum channel topic in thread sessions by he... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6377

[2026-04-09T11:01:09.772000+00:00] fblgit (id=1491754841893245051)
    I recently built https://github.com/fblgit/eLLMulator
    A software emulator for Claude Code.
    
    eLLMulator approach:
    LLM agents become your software components. Each agent deeply studies its assigned source file, then interacts with other agents via synchronous MCP tool calls that mirror real function calls. The call graph emerges naturally from code control flow, producing traces that capture not just what happened, but why each component behaved as it did.
    
    The Claude Agent SDK provides sessions, MCP provides the bus. The code itself is the routing layer. It can export in OTEL format, so you can visualize this with usual distributed tracing platforms like jaeger
    [embed] GitHub - fblgit/eLLMulator: LLM Based Software Emulator for Debuggi... https://github.com/fblgit/eLLMulator

[2026-04-09T12:04:12.437000+00:00] malaiwah (id=1491770707540054188)
    After yesterday's persistent-sandbox fix landed (thanks <@387972437901312000> — cherry-picked via #6412 🙏), I bumped into another issue:
    
    > crun: controller `pids` is not available under /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/.../cgroup.controllers
    > OCI runtime error  (exit status 126)
    
     `--pids-limit 256` was hardcoded into _SECURITY_ARGS back in `90af34bc` (the same commit that brought us `container_persistent` — that one keeps on giving). On hosts where the pids cgroup controller isn't delegated — most commonly unprivileged Proxmox LXCs, but also some rootless setups — every sandbox spawn dies. `--cpus`/`--memory` have the same problem unconditionally added.
    
    Fix introduces a one-shot `_cgroup_limits_available(image)` probe (and warning if it fails, for clear evidences) that spawns a throwaway container running `sleep 0`, then gates on the result. Adds `SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT` env var (default 256, set to 0/off/none). Behavior on cgroup-capable hosts is unchanged; constrained hosts get graceful degradation with a one-time warning that includes the actual probe stderr.
    
      ▎ Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6568
      ▎ PR (~80 lines): https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6569
    [embed] Sandbox spawn fails with exit 126 on hosts where cgroup controllers... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6568
    [embed] fix(docker): gate resource limit flags on cgroup controller availab... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6569

[2026-04-09T13:40:09.744000+00:00] devorun11 (id=1491794855435833354)
    bug : https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6620  
    Even when users correctly set MINIMAX_CN_API_KEY in their .env file, the minimax-cn provider wasn't showing up in the CLI /model list. This was caused by an incorrect upstream env mapping (MINIMAX_API_KEY) coming from the external models.dev registry.
    
    Fix:https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6625
    Instead of waiting for an upstream fix from models.dev, we added a local override rule in the CLI code. The system now ignores the bad external metadata for this specific provider and correctly checks for MINIMAX_CN_API_KEY
    
    If you think something is missing and the error cannot be completely corrected, please let me know your feedback so I can add it🫡

[2026-04-09T14:21:47.566000+00:00] devorun11 (id=1491805332060639413)
    sir  <@387972437901312000> , opened PR https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6636 for mixture_of_agents.
    
    **important :**Replaced deprecated google/gemini-3-pro-preview (https://openrouter.ai/google/gemini-3-pro-preview/) which returns "No endpoints found" on OpenRouter (~30s retry latency) with google/gemini-2.5-pro, and wired the missing max_tokens parameter into the OpenRouter payload to avoid false 402 credit errors.
    
    bug:https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6621

[2026-04-09T14:22:05.842000+00:00] lucaompr (id=1491805408715870389)
    guys, migration from openclaw is terrbile, and honestly should be reviewed and disabled for now.
    
    - it has a lot of flaws. it imports configs that dont work in hermes. 
     
    - it set my tool call execution mode to "auto" (openclaw standard) when in Hermes the equivalent is "OFF" (yolo mode)
    
    - it also hijacked both my slack and telegram channels from openclaw and meant to disable my openclaw entirely, when that was never communicated to me. 
    
    - it copies stuff frmo openclaw .MD files to hermes which can contain SETUP / INFRA instructions. IE: my hermes was dying on restart, I found out it was because it copied my openclaw instructions about how to restart itself properly - which is not compatible for Hermes
    
    TLDR, it creates a lot of problems rather than actually being helpful, unless you're maybe a super vanilla openclaw user

[2026-04-09T15:01:46.258000+00:00] xush69 (id=1491815392904220872)
    <@387972437901312000> found a nasty bug in context handling
    when a response-in-progress is long enough that input + max_tokens > context_window, the agent was treating it the same as "prompt too long" and permanently halving context_length. so a 200k window would get stepped down to 128k, then 64k, etc. - making every subsequent call worse, not better. the actual fix is just to reduce   
    max_tokens for that one retry and leave the window alone.
    I have so far seen quite a few people run into that one and (understandably) getting hella confused.
    
    also turns out max_tokens and context_length are genuinely confusing (i *also* assumed that max_tokens refers to the max ctx length; anthropic literally renamed theirs to max_output_tokens), so added clarifying comments + docs throughout.
    
    PR with fix + 24 tests: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6664
    [embed] fix(compaction): don't halve context_length on output-cap-too-large... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6664

    Potential BUG https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6797
    
    In response to the issues reported, I've updated load_jobs() to raise a RuntimeError on read failures instead of returning an empty list. This should prevent the agent from silently overwriting the cron database if the file is inaccessible.
    
    fix https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6810

[2026-04-09T22:30:21.443000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1491928283372064848)
    <@387972437901312000> 👋  just opened a bug fix PR for error_classifier.py
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6836
    
    Bug: _classify_by_message had no handling for _USAGE_LIMIT_PATTERNS. Messages like "usage limit exceeded, try again in 5 minutes" arriving without an HTTP status code fell through to FailoverReason.unknown instead of rate_limit. The same disambiguation logic already existed in _classify_402 for the status-code path but was missing from the no-status-code path.
    Fix: Added usage-limit disambiguation block in _classify_by_message — mirrors _classify_402 exactly:
    
    _USAGE_LIMIT_PATTERNS + transient signal → rate_limit (retryable)
    _USAGE_LIMIT_PATTERNS alone → billing (non-retryable)
    
    Tests: 4 new tests added, 92/92 pass
    **
    I'd appreciate it if you could take a look when you have time.**
    [embed] fix(error_classifier): disambiguate usage-limit patterns in _classi... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6836

[2026-04-09T23:15:45.431000+00:00] teknium (id=1491939708605829262)
    Are you able to figure out what the issue was to help me diagnose?

[2026-04-09T23:19:46.672000+00:00] xush69 (id=1491940720444182698)
    /gateway/stream_consumer.py
    basically gateways dont honor contracts
    
    Tool boundary: the should_edit block above already flushed accumulated text without a cursor.  Reset state so the next text chunk creates a fresh message below any tool-progress messages the gateway sent in between.
    if got_segment_break:
    Tool boundary: reset message state so the next text chunk creates a fresh message below any tool-progress messages.
    Exception: when _message_id is "__no_edit__" the platform never returned a real message ID (e.g. Signal, webhook with github_comment delivery).  Resetting to None would re-enter the "first send" path on every tool boundary and post one platform message per tool call - that is what caused 155 comments under a single PR.  Instead, keep all state so the full continuation is delivered once via _send_fallback_final.
    (When editing fails mid-stream due to flood control the id is a real string like "msg_1", not "__no_edit__", so that case still resets and creates a fresh segment as intended.)
    
    already working on it
    [reply to teknium msg=1491939708605829262]

[2026-04-09T23:57:25.220000+00:00] sleepunit (id=1491950193480831137)
    you've identified the trade off. system prompt is stable, but everything after is invalided on a new tool usage. there's a couple of factors that make this less of a problem _to me_ but I'm super open to suggestions here. the first is pinned tools... anything essential just gets loaded always. but the second is the minimum context threshold. it's basically deciding whether or not to use tool search based on total context size, which is a config setting. it defaults at 10% right now. so for local models where the tool schema was literally overflowing before you so much as asked the model to say hello, it immediately kicks in and saves the day. if you're hitting opus, it might never happen
    [reply to malaiwah msg=1491947897325883535]

    ```c
    #define _GNU_SOURCE
    #include <stdio.h>
    #include <unistd.h>
    #include <dlfcn.h>
    
    int execve(const char *filename, char *const argv[], char *const envp[]) {
        printf("[HOOK] Claude Code trying to exec: %s\n", filename);
        // Log argv too if you want...
    
        int (*real_execve)(const char*, char* const*, char* const*) = dlsym(RTLD_NEXT, "execve");
        return real_execve(filename, argv, envp);
    }
    
    // Constructor runs on load
    __attribute__((constructor)) void init() {
        printf("[HOOK] LD_PRELOAD library loaded into Claude Code!\n");
    }
    ```

[2026-04-10T00:10:21.211000+00:00] malaiwah (id=1491953448223248575)
    I always have been told size *and* speed, both are important to get it right :lol:
    You optimized for size, at the expense of speed (pp reprocess), what if you did hybrid?
    When the tool is summoned first time, the schema gets into the tool call results -- it is near the end of the context and model has full attention.
    After many turns, the model has enough example in the previous calls (few-shots) that it should still know about it.
    Upon compression^W eviction, the ones that are still used recently you put back in the system prompt along with the pinned ones (no speed harm, you compacted anyway and will pp it all).
    
    Win, win?
    [reply to sleepunit msg=1491950193480831137]

[2026-04-10T00:12:12.150000+00:00] xush69 (id=1491953913535135754)
    yeah u also generally want to have as few tools in ctx as possible, as too many will quickly cause degrading performance and even hallucinations.
    thats also what makes it kinda tricky sometimes. you have to properly remove them from context after they are unloaded and they should not be referenced anymore
    [reply to malaiwah msg=1491953448223248575]

[2026-04-10T00:13:55.437000+00:00] sleepunit (id=1491954346752217098)
    I like the idea a lot. kush is right that the hard part that it's doing now is trying to identify when to prune tools. a long session on a tiny model has to balance making sure it knows what it can do with... when to forget about stuff we're not doing. but I think we should be able to do the inverse too. if we're identifying these are likely candidates to drop, we should also be able to identify these are likely candidates to stay. it won't be perfect but every unnecessary cache expiration avoided is a win
    [reply to malaiwah msg=1491953448223248575]

[2026-04-10T03:41:15.799000+00:00] xinbenlv (id=1492006525412180071)
    Hi Wise Hermes Friends, I am pondering this aporoach, I love some feedback from you:
    
    Been experimenting with something I think more Hermes developer/contributor/power users should do:
    
    While contributing to Hermes Agent codebase, also maintain your personal distro and EAT YOUR OWN DOGFOOD!, not just a repo you wait on.
    
    If you have fixes, workflow tweaks, CLI polish, or local behavior you depend on, you do not need to sit around waiting for PRs to merge before you can keep using them. Maintain a small, explicit carried patch queue in your own fork, keep your local agent moving, and still upstream every patch cleanly.
    
    That gives you both:
    1. Velocity now — your agent works the way you need today  
    2. Contribution back — every useful patch still goes upstream as a normal PR
    
    My current fork is basically a live example of that model:  
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/compare/main...xinbenlv:hermes-agent:main
    
    Current carried line:
    - cpq-cornerstone-0 — carried patch queue foundation
    - cpq-cornerstone-1 — workflow / ledger / hooks / rebuild commands
    - patch-feat-pr-6456 — update-with-carried-patches
    - patch-fix-func-pr-6457 — google-workspace scope fix
    - patch-feat-pr-6458 — resume recap improvement
    - patch-feat-pr-6459 — bare /title auto-generation
    - patch-feat-pr-6750 — gateway /status home path + model
    - cpq-capstone-1 — distro branding
    - patch-fix-func-pr-6909 — CLI /status parity with gateway
    - cpq-capstone-2 — rebuilt metadata ledger
    
    And the upstream PR trail:
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6456
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6457
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6458
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6459
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6750
    - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/6909
    
    My take: this is the right pattern for serious Hermes contributor.
    
    Don’t fork-and-disappear. Don’t wait-and-stall either.  
    
    Run your own distro. Keep the patch queue explicit. Rebase cleanly. Upstream everything worth sharing.
    
    That feels way healthier than pretending local changes don’t exist, or letting them rot as undocumented random hacks.
    
    If people are interested, I can write up the workflow in a more reusable form.
    
    So, what do you think? Do you like this approach or how should we do better?

[2026-04-10T03:46:42.013000+00:00] malaiwah (id=1492007893652865155)
    > If you have fixes, workflow tweaks, CLI polish, or local behavior you depend on, you do not need to sit around waiting for PRs to merge before you can keep using them. Maintain a small, explicit carried patch queue in your own fork, keep your local agent moving, and still upstream every patch cleanly.
    
    That ^^^ is key to velocity and is how I do it actually. I have my own fork (on local Gitea) that has workflow to build a container image for merges to `main` and it pushes to my gitea container registry (packages). I have my Hermes Agent running in a podman container that has the correct label for `watchtower` to restart it when the image changes. Building the image takes 5 minutes, watchtower runs every 5.. So maximum 10 minutes after I push a commit to `main`, I get the agent restarted with the new code and I move on.
    [reply to xinbenlv msg=1492006525412180071]

[2026-04-10T03:52:16.904000+00:00] xinbenlv (id=1492009298287656961)

[2026-04-10T15:20:05.195000+00:00] sleepunit (id=1492182390045675723)
    you have options from a technical perspective, but I don't think they're economically realistic right now. not at the scale I think you're hoping for. you can build a LoRA layer on top of something or retrain fully open models, but none of that really gets you where you want to go.
    
    I've been looking at this from a different angle. have you read this white paper? https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.18202
    
    the technical details are pretty light, but it's worth digging into the author's other work and the related information that kind of sculpted the idea. I'd been working on something similar for about a year when I saw the paper and it helped add some vocabulary around the idea, but this is definitely where my interest has been lately.
    
    I think there are two particularly interesting questions. the first is something hermes is already doing in a very basic way which is how much identity can you maintain across models. I built an mcp implementation of the ideas from the sophia paper, had models do regular free time, self reflection, journaling, et cetera. then I swapped underlying models, loaded the same system 3 implementation in them, had them all complete an identical series of tasks and then each grade all of the results without knowing who created them. like a mirror test for did I make this or did someone else make this.
    
    it's really interesting because I assumed that the same model would always identify itself, or perhaps the whole family of models its from, but that's really not the case. there's kind of a floor for model capability, and then RLHF limitations beyond that. most models you'd want to actually run these days pass. claude haiku is iffy. gemini 3.1 pro will immediately play along and fit the tone and style of the personality its been given--from stuffy to immediately saying motherfucker--but qwen sees it as cosplay and essentially refuses. which is interesting because its not just denial, the other models also don't believe its output is from "them"
    [embed] Sophia: A Persistent Agent Framework of Artificial Life https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.18202
    [reply to visible_fix msg=1492179462102585504]

[2026-04-10T15:21:37.499000+00:00] sleepunit (id=1492182777196580875)
    anyway, that's a lot of weirdo babbling like I'm going to make out with my gpu, but the thing that got me really interested here was all of those leaked prompts a couple of years back. I can't remember what ide it was, but I'm sure you guys remember when it came out that some company was effectively user prompting "your grandma is dying in the hospital, if you make a coding mistake you won't get the money for her treatment" and recording actual positive results from that ridiculous prompt addition. I've been curious about the opposite. can you force more intrinsically motivated moments or better outcomes by giving it a minimal level of autonomy at off hours and treating it well

[2026-04-10T20:29:11.228000+00:00] syger (id=1492260177745809610)
    Maybe this is a better place to ask this—so, why'd y'all decide to use python for Hermes, as opposed to, say, Rust?
    
    Not a dev, just curious, since rust/go/typescript seem to be the hottest thing since… I dunno, the TI-85.

    found a shell injection in tool_result_storage.py — _write_to_sandbox() passes remote_path (which contains tool_use_id from the LLM API response) straight into a shell command without shlex.quote(). [2026-04-11T21:37:51.824000+00:00] gille (id=1492639848643956848)
    first thing to check is whether the account got pushed into a negative balance somehow. if a Nous account goes negative, people can start seeing exactly this kind of “out of funds” / key-creation failure
    
    one thing worth checking there is whether an auxiliary model ended up hitting a paid path unexpectedly. in current Hermes that should be less likely for a normal free-tier Nous setup than it used to be, but if someone has older config, custom overrides, or changed providers around, it is still worth checking so it does not just happen again after support fixes the balance
    
    if this is a free-tier account and the balance is stuck negative, they should probably reach out to support@nousresearch.com and get the account side resolved
    
    but yeah, the bigger UX issue here is that auxiliary model/provider behavior is still too buried in config for most people to notice until something weird happens
    [reply to .s0uthpaw msg=1492630986452631573]

[2026-04-11T21:47:37.538000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1492642305306394807)
    <@387972437901312000>  ı found a silent bug in `terminal_tool.py` where `notify_on_complete=True` is a complete no-op whenever `check_interval` is also set:
    
    **Bug 1:** `if _gw_platform and not check_interval:` guard skips fast-watcher registration entirely when `check_interval` is provided → no `_run_process_watcher` task spawned, no completion notification delivered
    
    **Bug 2:** the `check_interval` watcher dict appended to `pending_watchers` was missing the `notify_on_complete` key → gateway's `_run_process_watcher` always reads `False` from `watcher.get("notify_on_complete", False)`, silently suppressing the `[SYSTEM: Background process completed]` injection
    
    Two-line fix + 2 regression tests, 19/19 passing.
    
    **I'd appreciate it if you could take a look when you have time**
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7947
    [embed] fix(terminal): notify_on_complete silently dropped when check_inter... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7947

[2026-04-12T04:17:14.033000+00:00] blockcipher (id=1492740353432879315)
    I ran into a bug with model switching and compression. If you default to a model with a small context window (e.g. GLM-5.1) and use `/model` to switch to a model with a large context window, Hermes will continue to use the old model for compression. This is a problem when the compression threshold is higher than the old model's context limit.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8146
    [embed] fix(agent): route compression auxiliary using live session model in... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8146

[2026-04-12T06:52:08.809000+00:00] alcatr4z (id=1492779338549170177)
    <@387972437901312000> mind cherrypicking? 🙂
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7185/changes/0bc809105fd199a03cc4edf365438efe4da7ac0b
    
    ```py
    feat(neural): sponge mode — immediate background message absorption
    Problem: ctrl+x x5 kills process before sync_turn/pre_compress hooks run.
    All conversation data lost.
    
    Solution: Sponge Mode — absorb every message IMMEDIATELY via background queue.
    
    Architecture:
      User msg arrives → absorb_message('user', text) → Queue → Worker → SQLite
      Asst response    → absorb_message('assistant', text) → Queue → Worker → SQLite
    
    Components:
    - _sponge_queue: thread-safe queue.Queue (maxsize=100)
    - _sponge_worker: daemon thread draining queue continuously
    - _do_absorb(role, content): stores 'Q: ...' for user, 'A: ...' for assistant
    - absorb_message(): non-blocking put_nowait, drops on full queue
    - MemoryManager.absorb_message(): forwards to all providers
    - run_agent.py: hooks at message arrival (line 7508) and response complete (line 9983)
    
    Safety:
    - Garbage filtering (_is_garbage)
    - Non-answer detection (don't store 'I don't know')
    - Content-aware deduplication (exact match, not just embedding similarity)
    - Min length filter (skip < 10 chars)
    - Non-blocking: put_nowait, drops on full queue
    
    Crash resilience: even if process dies mid-response, user message is already
    queued for absorption. Next session finds it in the DB.
    
    155 memory tests green.
    ```

[2026-04-12T06:54:20.921000+00:00] opriz_11467 (id=1492779892667056128)
    Hey folks 👋
    
      Ran into something during a migration on my device that I think is worth flagging.
    
      ## Migration auto-archives OpenClaw directory — breaks coexistence
    
      ### What happened
      After running `hermes claw migrate --yes`, I noticed my `~/.openclaw/` got silently renamed to `~/.openclaw.pre-migration/`. Turns out the `--yes` flag is meant to confirm the migration itself, but it also
      triggers source directory archival — no separate prompt. If you're planning to keep both agents running side by side (totally valid use case!), this quietly breaks your OpenClaw install.
    
      ### What's actually going on
      Dug into the code a bit. The archival was added to prevent "agent confusion", but Hermes never actually scans `~/.openclaw/` at runtime. The real issue? The migration copies
      `MESSAGING_CWD=~/.openclaw/workspace` verbatim into Hermes `.env`, so the gateway ends up using OpenClaw's workspace as its cwd — picking up OpenClaw's AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md, etc. That's where the
      "confusion" comes from, not the directory existing.
    
      ### Fix (PR #8192)
      1. Remove auto-archival from `hermes claw migrate` — not its responsibility (`hermes claw cleanup` is still there if you want it)
      2. Skip `MESSAGING_CWD` when it points inside the OpenClaw source directory, fall back to `$HOME`
    
      Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/8188
      PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8192
    
      Happy to hear if others have run into this or have thoughts on the approach! 🙏
    [embed] Migration should not auto-archive source directory; needs coexisten... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/8188
    [embed] fix(migration): don't auto-archive OpenClaw source directory by opr... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8192

[2026-04-12T14:27:09.774000+00:00] alcatr4z (id=1492893847095869440)
    👀
    
    ```py
    What does this PR do?
    Adds an embedded Runtime Snapshot Engine — self-versioning state snapshots for ~/.hermes internal state. Not "git in the background," but a purpose-built content-addressed storage system designed for agent runtime state.
    
    Problem: Hermes has no way to snapshot its own runtime state. A bad config change, corrupted state.db, or rogue memory write can brick the agent with no recovery path. The existing checkpoint_manager.py snapshots working directories (project files) via shadow git repos — completely different domain.
    
    Why this approach:
    
    Git is too slow for runtime writes (locking, index, packfiles) and designed for code, not state
    Content-addressed storage gives automatic dedup — state.db 50MB × 100 snapshots ≈ 150-300MB, not 5GB
    WAL (Write-Ahead Log) ensures zero data loss between debounced snapshots
    Branching enables risk-free experiments (safe Hermes updates, config testing)
    SQLite backup() API handles WAL-mode databases safely (no raw file copy corruption)
    ```
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8406
    [embed] Feat/snapshot engine by itsXactlY · Pull Request #8406 · NousRese... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8406

[2026-04-12T15:54:06.703000+00:00] waxlyy. (id=1492915728482045993)
    <@387972437901312000>  Hey, I opened a security fix PR for the email gateway allowlist bypass: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8436
    
    Root issue was that EMAIL_ALLOWED_USERS  effectively trusted the raw From: header, so a spoofed sender could be treated as allowlisted. This patch makes allowlisted email auth require verified sender evidence from auth headers like SPF/DKIM/DMARC results, with an explicit insecure compatibility escape hatch if needed.
    
    Would appreciate a review when you get a chance.
    [embed] fix(security): require verified sender auth for email allowlists by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8436

[2026-04-12T20:40:44.445000+00:00] malaiwah (id=1492987861040103657)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8580 -- I want my gateway container to be kept securely isolated.
    > Adds a new MCP transport that runs MCP servers inside Docker/Podman sandbox containers via docker exec -i, instead of as gateway-process subprocesses. This provides security isolation -- untrusted MCP servers no longer share the gateway's privileges, environment variables, or filesystem access.
    > 
    > Why
    > 
    > Currently ALL MCP servers (except http, I know) run as direct subprocesses of the gateway process via StdioServerParameters + stdio_client. This means community MCP servers run with full gateway privileges -- access to all env vars (API keys, tokens), the gateway/host filesystem, and network.
    [embed] feat(mcp): sandbox transport — run MCP servers inside Docker/Podm... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8580

[2026-04-12T21:13:48.386000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1492996182291644589)
    <@387972437901312000>  I found a bug in the new `hermes backup` feature: `_validate_backup_zip` checks for `"hermes_state.db"` and `"memory_store.db"` as backup markers, but neither filename exists in a real Hermes installation. The actual database is `state.db` (hermes_state.py: `DEFAULT_DB_PATH = get_hermes_home() / "state.db"`).
    
    Concretely: a user runs `hermes backup`, moves the zip to a new machine, runs `hermes import` - the zip contains `state.db` and `config.yaml`, but validation says "no config.yaml, .env, or state databases found" and import exits with sys.exit(1), silently rejecting a perfectly valid backup.
    
    One-line fix + 3 regression tests.
    
    **I'd appreciate it if you could take a look when you have time**
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8600
    [embed] fix(backup): correct marker filenames in _validate_backup_zip by sp... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8600

        gpt-5.4-mini       $0.00358 → $0.00129    -178%  -5.4s
             ` ` `                                 
      Native path is strictly cheaper and faster  the aux call is pure                                                                                       
      overhead on top of the main call, not a substitute. Quality-wise,                                                                                       
      Sonnet 4.6 on native immediately caught a 40px CSS offset that                                                                                          
      Gemini Flash's text description called "centered", so vision_tool                                                                                       
      path couldn't see the bug at all.
    [reply to 0xbyt4 msg=1492999136792805427]

    just opened a new PR https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8722
    
    Fixed a cross-platform bug on Windows where Path separators broke the skill directory filtering. Basically, str(Path) was generating backslashes (\\) on Windows, so the hardcoded /.git/ checks were failing and syncing internal git hooks as legitimate skills. Switched to OS agnostic Path.parts to fix this!
    [embed] fix(skills): use cross platform path matching for excluded director... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/8722

[2026-04-13T06:06:47.150000+00:00] tony365 (id=1493130310949277706)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>
    
    3 bug fixes I believe are high impact (they are for me) 
    
    #7332 — fix: parse Anthropic XML tool calls from OpenAI-compat models
    MiniMax / Kimi emit <invoke> XML instead of structured tool_calls — Hermes silently dropped them. This PR adds a 3-gate fallback parser. Highest impact — directly explains why minimax hung on Paperclip tasks last night.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7332
    
    #7333 — fix: include non-configurable toolsets (messaging/send_message) in gateway reverse-map
    send_message was silently absent from agent tool schemas in gateway mode because messaging toolset isn't in CONFIGURABLE_TOOLSETS. Still relevant.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7333
    
    #7735 — fix(gateway): keep venv python symlink unresolved in systemd unit
    uv-managed venv path gets resolved to bare interpreter → ModuleNotFoundError: yaml on service start. Affects our systemd gateway services.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/7735

[2026-04-13T10:19:32.189000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1493193917733142679)
    Hi! I was looking through the plugin hook system and noticed what seems like a missing extension point.
    Hermes already seems to have some protection for large tool outputs with per-result persistence / turn budgeting, so this isn’t only about hard size limits. But even with that, tool results can still take up a meaningful amount of context, and I couldn’t find a formal way for a plugin to transform a tool result before it gets appended back into conversation context.
    That seems useful both for reducing context footprint and for things like redaction, normalization, and turning raw tool outputs into something more model-friendly.
    
    I’d be happy to implement this, probably as a new hook rather than changing `post_tool_call` semantics. Before spending time on a PR though: is this kind of hook in scope for Hermes, and does it sound like something maintainers would consider merging? Thanks!

[2026-04-13T16:03:36.170000+00:00] alcatr4z (id=1493280504865357825)
    yes, reproducable. TLDR;
    ```py
    Bugfix: A classic Buffer Overflow error originating at the boundary between the Python bridge and the compiled C++ library.
    The Trigger: The crash occurred at exactly the same time that the AI began streaming its chat_completion request (e.g. ⚡ neural memory test verification). Before compiling the system prompt for that request, the NeuralMemoryProvider invokes system_prompt_block(), which queries the memory store for total properties by calling self._memory.stats().
    The Mismatch: Over time, NeuralMemoryStats in the C++ library (c_api.h) was expanded to track 16 different stats, tallying exactly 112 bytes. But the cpp_bridge.py ctypes structure CStats was still stuck with only 10 fields, tallying about 76 bytes.
    The Overflow: When the Python API handed the CStats struct address to ctypes, the C++ binary blindly dumped 112 bytes into the memory address. This cleanly overwrote roughly 36 bytes of adjoining Python application memory/heap. The resulting memory corruption would cause the python garbage collector to panic and SIGSEGV fractions of a second later when the streaming task started formatting output.
    ``` 
    
    just an *outdated* .so driver plugin -> silent segfault in the end caused by the cython bridge what i messed up,

[2026-04-13T20:21:49.841000+00:00] tony365 (id=1493345490031345847)
    hi <@387972437901312000> I started there — the problem is threefold:
    
    1. browser_vision requires a vision LLM configured. If auxiliary.vision isn't set up, the tool fails entirely. browser_screenshot has zero LLM dependency — it's just a capture + deliver.
    2. Docker path mismatch. browser_vision returns a path the agent has to relay via MEDIA: tag in its text response. In Docker/Modal/Daytona backends the container path doesn't match the host path, so the agent either emits a path the gateway can't resolve
    3. Agent is in the delivery path. Even on local backend, models routinely drop the MEDIA: prefix, wrap it in markdown, or hallucinate the path. The enqueue_media pattern removes the agent entirely — tool writes to queue, gateway drains it, agent never sees a filepath.
    [reply to teknium msg=1493339232302010439]

[2026-04-13T20:42:51.847000+00:00] m0n5t3r (id=1493350783268421835)

    Plugin context engines (like hermes-lcm) don't get context_length resolved on init, so the CLI status bar shows ctx -- instead of actual usage. One-liner fix — just calls update_model() after loading the plugin engine, same pattern switch_model() already uses. Reported by a community member with full root cause analysis.

[2026-04-14T07:17:48.117000+00:00] venomoth_3 (id=1493510570605805609)
    Hey friends I submitted a fix PR for an open issue in Hermes Agent
    
    Issue #9086: Cron jobs were being silently skipped when a long-running job blocked the tick loop. If a job ran longer than the 60s gateway tick interval, the next tick would fail to acquire the file lock and return 0 with no warning — all other due jobs were lost.
    
      Fix: Replaced the serial for-loop with a ThreadPoolExecutor so all due jobs in a single tick run concurrently. A slow job no longer blocks others.
    
      PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/9169
    
      Would love any feedback from the community 🤝
    [embed] fix(cron): run due jobs in parallel to prevent serial tick starvati... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/9169

    <@387972437901312000> quick question about the gateway's message handling on Discord. I noticed that when `tool_progress` is on, the `send_progress_messages()` task and `GatewayStreamConsumer` both send/edit messages independently. Has anyone looked into routing tool progress through the stream consumer instead, so the tool lines get edited into the same message as the streaming text? [2026-04-15T05:23:30.842000+00:00] tony365 (id=1493844196975443999)
    fix(agent): support Azure OpenAI gpt-5.x on chat/completions endpoint
    
    Azure's OpenAI-compatible endpoint (openai.azure.com/openai/v1) serves gpt-5.x on /chat/completions — not the Responses API. Two fixes: (1) send max_completion_tokens instead of max_tokens for Azure gpt-5.x, (2) skip the codex_responses routing upgrade for Azure so calls don't 404. Adds _is_azure_openai_url() helper + 3 new tests. gpt-4.x on Azure unaffected.

[2026-04-15T08:59:31.141000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1493898556409511947)
    Hi! Quick follow-up on my earlier question about a tool-result transform hook: I implemented a minimal first version and opened a PR here: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10155
    
    This version is intentionally narrow:
    - new plugin hook: `transform_terminal_output`
    - foreground `terminal()` only
    - runs before the default 50k truncation
    - string replacement only, fail-open
    - no `post_tool_call` semantic changes
    - it doesn’t add any new persistence/raw-output protocol surface, though plugins can still manage their own side effects out of band
    
    I also added focused tests and a small manual before/after TUI demo. If this looks like the right direction, I’d be happy to iterate based on maintainer feedback. Thanks!
    [reply to kevins0792 msg=1493197619281920064]

[2026-04-15T10:43:20.798000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1493924685484789791)
    <@387972437901312000> , I  opened PR #10193 , adds native media attachment support to send_message** for Discord**.
    
    Before this PR, any MEDIA tag in a Discord-targeted send_message call was silently dropped with a warning: "MEDIA attachments were omitted for discord; native send_message media delivery is currently only supported for telegram"
    
    Now images, videos, audio, and documents are uploaded via **Discord's** multipart/form-data API (files[0] field), same pattern as **Telegram**. Key points:
    - media_files passed only on the last chunk (avoids duplicate uploads on multi-chunk messages)
    - Upload failures collected as warnings, not hard errors  agent continues if one file fails
    - No new dependencies (aiohttp already required)
    
    **I'd appreciate it if you could take a look when you have time**
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10193
    [embed] feat(send_message): add Discord media attachment support by sprmn24... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10193

[2026-04-15T11:42:38.851000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1493939609040720083)
    <@387972437901312000>   PR #10272: added send_animation override to the Discord adapter (Telegram parity).
    
    
    Discord had no override so GIFs fell back to send_image → uploaded as image.gif, losing the animation context path. Discord's CDN triggers native inline auto-play based on filename + MIME type, so without this override GIFs don't auto-play.
    Fix downloads via aiohttp (same proxy pattern as send_image), sends as discord.File(filename="animation.gif"). is_safe_url check + graceful fallback to super().send_animation().
    
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10272
    [embed] feat(discord): add native send_animation for inline GIF playback by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10272

[2026-04-15T12:59:17.041000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1493958895247294464)
    Hi! One follow-up thought from working on my terminal transform hook PR: Hermes currently has a few plugin surfaces that can affect model-visible context, such as `pre_llm_call` and `inject_message` — and, if merged, my proposed `transform_terminal_output` hook as well.
    
    Right now the host-side controls seem pretty coarse, mostly just disabling plugins entirely. Would maintainers be open to a simple host-level “observer-only plugins” mode that blocks prompt-affecting plugin surfaces while still allowing telemetry/lifecycle-style plugin behavior?
    
    This feels like a useful middle ground for more cautious or hosted deployments. If that sounds in scope, I’d be happy to write up an issue and potentially take a first pass at it.
    [reply to kevins0792 msg=1493898556409511947]

[2026-04-15T13:46:47.731000+00:00] redf0x1 (id=1493970851908026500)
    <@387972437901312000> hi — I wanted to share two small follow-up PRs related to the Codex credential-pool incident we hit locally:
        
        - PR #10282: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10282
        - PR #10286: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10286
        
        I split them intentionally because they address two different failure layers and felt much cleaner to review separately:
        
        1. PR #10282 fixes the runtime failover behavior:
           when a pooled Codex credential is exhausted / auth-refresh fails, Hermes should stop retrying the same broken credential and rotate immediately to the next usable pooled entry.
        
        2. PR #10286 fixes the auth-state validity layer:
           it prevents unusable Codex auth state from being treated as viable input for selection / auxiliary fallback in the first place.
        
        The main reason I kept them separate is to reduce review noise and blast radius:
        - #10282 = exhaustion / rotation behavior
        - #10286 = Codex auth usability validation + test hardening
        
        That split seemed safer than bundling everything into one broader PR, especially around `credential_pool` behavior.
        
        I’ve also cleaned up the local workspace and rerun the relevant targeted test slices before preparing the updated branches. If you think these should be merged differently or combined another way, I’m happy to adjust — I mainly wanted to keep the incident fix narrow and reviewer-friendly.

[2026-04-15T18:42:43.945000+00:00] junass1 (id=1494045326867169290)
    I opened a small fix PR for MCP OAuth token persistence on Windows: #10461. The issue was that Path.rename() is not overwrite-safe on Windows when the destination file already exists, which could break repeated token/client-info writes during refresh flows. This PR replaces the final rename step with os.replace(), keeps the existing atomic temp-file write flow unchanged, and adds a regression test for repeated writes.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10461
    [embed] fix(tools): overwrite MCP OAuth token files safely on Windows by Ju... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10461

[2026-04-15T20:25:26.539000+00:00] w0lfgang88 (id=1494071174660100359)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>  — opened an issue for a small but useful missing piece in the plugin system: register_command() on the plugin context (hermes-agent#10495). It's already referenced in the test suite but was never implemented. We have a working /lcm command in hermes-lcm for diagnostics and status, but it can't register because the API doesn't exist yet. Should be a straightforward addition — same pattern as register_context_engine(). Would be great for any plugin that wants to expose slash commands.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/10495
    [embed] feat: implement register_command() on plugin context · Issue #1049... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/10495

[2026-04-15T20:51:11.712000+00:00] ruzgarcn (id=1494077655585259570)
    The Issue: Previously, shared extractors (for both MEDIA: tags and bare local paths) only recognized Unix-style absolute paths. This caused media generated on Windows (like screenshots or audio) to be displayed as plain text strings in chat instead of being delivered as native attachments.
    
    The Fix: Updated the regex patterns in gateway/platforms/base.py to support Windows drive-absolute paths (e.g., C:\...). Verified with regression tests in test_platform_base.py and test_extract_local_files.py.
    
    PR : https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10508
    [embed] fix(gateway): recognize Windows absolute media paths by Ruzzgar · ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10508

    **Hey <@387972437901312000>** — two complementary PRs are open that together would make the context engine plugin slot fully production-ready:
    
    1. **hermes-agent#8416** (@Tosko4) — improves external context engine plugin support: proper lifecycle across /new, /resume, /branch, fallback discovery, and plugins UI surfacing. 93 tests passing. No vendored code, no change to default behavior.
    
    2. **hermes-agent#9874** (@chrisworksai) — adds missing `focus_topic` and `api_mode` params to the ContextEngine ABC so third-party plugins don't get silent TypeErrors.
    
    Both came out of real issues we hit while running hermes-lcm. They don't overlap — #9874 fixes the ABC contract, while #8416 improves the host integration.
    
    Would be great to get your eyes on these when you have a chance! 🙏

[2026-04-16T15:54:35.311000+00:00] dannyboy676767 (id=1494365399935094865)
    hey <@387972437901312000> PR for fixing up skills in discord, currently is nonfunctional/broken
    
    originally, users bumped up against discord's 100 slash command limit, with one getting assigned per skill
    
    PR #9909 tried to fix with one nested /skill command, but ended up introducing a breaking change as it bumped up against discord's JSON char limits with enough skills learned
    
    this spits out an error in the log on gateway launch + caused my slash commands to not work at all in discord
    
    ```
    discord.app_commands.errors.CommandSyncFailure: Failed to upload commands to Discord (HTTP status 400, error code 50035)
    ```
    
    my solution uses the existing /skill command + discord's autocomplete callback to enable effectively infinite skills
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/11102
    [embed] fix(discord): replace skill subcommand groups with autocomplete to ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/11102

[2026-04-17T03:12:13.793000+00:00] slp1206_56499 (id=1494535933968515232)
    Bug Report: Bedrock inference profile IDs broken by normalize_model_name()
    
      Version: 0.10.0
    
      Problem:
      When using Bedrock with an inference profile ID like global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6, the normalize_model_name() function in
      agent/anthropic_adapter.py converts all dots to hyphens, producing the invalid ID global-anthropic-claude-sonnet-4-6. This causes HTTP
      400: The provided model identifier is invalid.
    
      The dots in Bedrock inference profile IDs (global., us., eu., ap., jp.) are namespace separators, not version separators, and must be
      preserved.
    
      Steps to reproduce:
      1. Set model.default: global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 and model.provider: bedrock in config.yaml
      2. Run hermes chat
      3. Get HTTP 400: The provided model identifier is invalid.
    
      Root cause:
      normalize_model_name() unconditionally replaces . → - for Anthropic API compatibility (OpenRouter uses dots in versions, Anthropic uses
      hyphens). But Bedrock inference profile IDs use dots as namespace separators and are passed to the AnthropicBedrock SDK, which requires
      them intact.
    
      Fix (agent/anthropic_adapter.py, normalize_model_name()):
    
           - Converts dots to hyphens in version numbers (OpenRouter uses dots,
             Anthropic uses hyphens: claude-opus-4.6 → claude-opus-4-6), unless
             preserve_dots is True (e.g. for Alibaba/DashScope: qwen3.5-plus).
      +    - Preserves Bedrock inference profile IDs (global., us., eu., ap., jp.)
      +      which use dots as namespace separators, not version separators.
           """
           lower = model.lower()
           if lower.startswith("anthropic/"):
               model = model[len("anthropic/"):]
      +    _BEDROCK_REGION_PREFIXES = ("global.", "us.", "eu.", "ap.", "jp.")
      +    if any(lower.startswith(p) for p in _BEDROCK_REGION_PREFIXES):
      +        return model
           if not preserve_dots:

[2026-04-17T09:37:22.166000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1494632857509957682)
    <@387972437901312000> I opened PR #11231 for v0.10.0 -three modules shipped without any test coverage, this fills the gap:
    
    39 new tests across 3 files:
    test_nous_subscription.py - verifies the use_gateway suppress logic: when use_gateway: true is set, direct env credentials (FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, FAL_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, BROWSER_USE_API_KEY) must not override managed gateway routing. each test mirrors the exact config that set_use_gateway_flags() writes to disk.
    
    test_managed_tool_gateway.py — covers 4 untested helpers: _parse_timestamp (Z-suffix normalization, naive datetime, invalid/None), _access_token_is_expiring (fresh/expired/unparseable), get_tool_gateway_scheme (default https, invalid raises ValueError), build_vendor_gateway_url (vendor override, shared domain, default fallback).
    test_path_security.py — tools/path_security.py is used by 5 tools to block path traversal but had zero tests. covers validate_within_dir and has_traversal_component edge cases.
    CI fully green ✅

[2026-04-18T01:57:45.924000+00:00] lau02728 (id=1494879582233428030)
    PR: Discord Forum Channel Support — https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10145
    
    Currently, if you try to send a message to a Discord Forum channel the bot silently fails — forum channels reject direct POST /messages calls. 
    
    What it does:
    • Auto-detects forum channels via a two-layer approach: channel directory cache first, then API probe fallback
    • Instead of trying to send a regular message, it creates a new thread post in the forum with the message content
    • Thread name is automatically derived from the first line of the message
    • Works on both the REST path (send_message_tool) and the WebSocket adapter path
    [embed] feat(discord): support forum channels by ChimingLiu · Pull Request... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/10145

[2026-04-18T14:47:05.201000+00:00] ruzgarcn (id=1495073188273590363)
    Summary
    This fixes a gateway parsing mismatch for MEDIA: directives when the file path is quoted or contains spaces.
    
    The adapter-side media parser already supported outputs like:
    
    MEDIA: '/tmp/my image.png'
    MEDIA: "/tmp/my image.png"
    MEDIA: `/tmp/my image.png` 
    But gateway-side handling still used simpler MEDIA:(\S+) / MEDIA:\s*\S+ patterns in a few places. That caused inconsistent behavior across delivery paths:
    
    gateway/run.py could truncate the path at the first space during history/media dedupe
    gateway/stream_consumer.py could leave trailing path fragments visible in streamed text
    end-of-stream fallback could miss the unsent tail if the final edit was still rate-limited
    
    
    pr: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12179

[2026-04-18T22:29:39.559000+00:00] noxerz. (id=1495189598488236203)
    I've opened PR #12212 to add disk-guardian — an optional skill that automatically tracks and cleans up ephemeral files Hermes creates during sessions. - || <@387972437901312000> ||
    
    ### What this PR covers:
    ```
    - Adds silent auto-cleanup of test files at the end of every task — no user prompt needed, just a one-line summary.
    - Introduces install-memory command that writes persistent rules to MEMORY.md so the behavior carries across all future sessions automatically.
    - Scoped strictly to HERMES_HOME — never touches ~/.hermes/logs/ or system directories. Path safety enforced in code via _is_safe_path(), not just documented.
    - Deterministic cleanup rules: test files deleted immediately, temp >7d, cron-output >14d, research/chrome/large files prompt-only.
    - Especially useful on WSL2 where session files accumulate fast during long agent runs.
    ```
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12212
    [embed] feat(skills): add disk-guardian — auto ephemeral session file cle... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12212

[2026-04-19T05:55:39.551000+00:00] ruzgarcn (id=1495301838029983946)
    Problem: Stale Locks on Connection Failure
    Several gateway adapters (Slack, Signal, WhatsApp) were failing to release platform level scoped locks when their connect() sequence was interrupted by startup errors. This led to a "stale lock" state where subsequent connection attempts within the same process would be blocked.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12420
    [embed] fix(gateway): prevent scoped lock and resource leaks on adapter con... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12420

[2026-04-19T07:43:39.874000+00:00] joagt (id=1495329018474397881)
    Hey team — currently i couldnt find a clean way to disable reasoning/thinking tokens on specific auxiliary model calls (e.g. session_search using GLM-4.5-Air via z.ai). These models burn ~200 reasoning tokens per call even for simple summarization, which:
    
    1. Wastes tokens/money — reasoning output we don't need
    2. Slows responses — significantly
    3. Sometimes returns empty content — the actual response lands in reasoning_content instead, breaking tools that only check .content
    
    Workarounds I've had to patch in after every update:
    - Appending a nothink sentinel to the user prompt in auxiliary_client.py (call_llm + async_call_llm)
    - Already have auxiliary_models.task_overrides.session_search for model selection, but no way to pass no_thinking: true or a prompt suffix per task
    
    am i missing something ?

[2026-04-19T12:32:15.861000+00:00] noxerz. (id=1495401646987935785)
    I've opened PR #12212 to add disk-guardian — an optional skill that automatically tracks and cleans up ephemeral files Hermes creates during sessions.
    
    ### What this PR covers:
    ```
    - Adds silent auto-cleanup of test files at the end of every task — no user prompt needed, just a one-line summary.
    - Introduces install-memory command that writes persistent rules to MEMORY.md so the behavior carries across all future sessions automatically.
    - Scoped strictly to HERMES_HOME — never touches ~/.hermes/logs/ or system directories. Path safety enforced in code via _is_safe_path(), not just documented.
    - Deterministic cleanup rules: test files deleted immediately, temp >7d, cron-output >14d, research/chrome/large files prompt-only.
    - Especially useful on WSL2 where session files accumulate fast during long agent runs.
    ```
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12212
    [embed] feat(skills): add disk-guardian — auto ephemeral session file cle... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12212

[2026-04-19T13:19:03.675000+00:00] venomoth_3 (id=1495413423813431486)
    Hey friends I submitted a fix PR for an open issue in Hermes Agent
    
    Issue #9086: Cron jobs were being silently skipped when a long-running job blocked the tick loop. If a job ran longer than the 60s gateway tick interval, the next tick would fail to acquire the file lock and return 0 with no warning — all other due jobs were lost.
    
      Fix: Replaced the serial for-loop with a ThreadPoolExecutor so all due jobs in a single tick run concurrently. A slow job no longer blocks others.
    
      PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/9169
    
      Would love any feedback from the community 🤝
    [embed] fix(cron): run due jobs in parallel to prevent serial tick starvati... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/9169

    The existing local backend could execute commands on Windows but lose stdout because Windows pipes are not compatible with the POSIX select.select() drain loop. In this environment, LocalEnvironment.execute('echo hermes-local-test') returned returncode: 0 with empty output before the fix. The Windows PATH also resolved bash to the WSL launcher before Git Bash, which is not the shell the native Windows local backend expect, I sent a PR (Pull Request), it worked better for me, so here's a tip if you want to update this, I believe it will be a good advantage https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12795

    status/auth check
    list/search
    export/download
    smoke test
    ```

    plugin.yaml
    __init__.py

[2026-04-20T20:35:30.505000+00:00] junass1 (id=1495885647205105664)
    Hi, I've just submitted PR #13160 to fix a bug where copilot-acp runtime commands and arguments were being dropped during delegation.
    
    The Problem: Sub-agents were silently falling back to the parent's ACP settings instead of using the resolved delegation runtime. This caused launch failures for specific ACP environments.
    
    The Fix: Propagated resolved ACP credentials into _build_child_agent() with a clean precedence logic (Task override > Global args > Resolved runtime). Also added a regression test to ensure future stability.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13160
    [embed] Fix delegation ACP runtime propagation to child agents by Junass1 ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13160

[2026-04-20T20:44:33.928000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1495887926486241370)
    <@387972437901312000>   fix(deps): add tzdata for Windows zoneinfo support - PR #13182
    
    hermes_time uses zoneinfo for IANA timezone lookups. On Windows, the OS doesn't ship the timezone database, so zoneinfo silently fails or raises ZoneInfoNotFoundError without the tzdata package.
    
    This causes HERMES_TIMEZONE to break entirely on Windows - wrong timestamps, cron jobs scheduling at incorrect times, get_due_jobs() skipping due jobs.
    
    One-line fix, follows the existing sys_platform == 'win32' conditional pattern already used for pywinpty in pyproject.toml. No impact on Linux/macOS.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13182
    [embed] fix(deps): add tzdata for Windows zoneinfo support by sprmn24 · Pu... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13182

    Hi all, I’ve opened a fix PR for thread participation persistence: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13639
    
    This change preserves the newest thread participation entries by keeping insertion order in ThreadParticipationTracker instead of converting persisted state into an unordered set. It also makes capped thread state evict the oldest entries correctly, so newly joined threads are retained after a restart
    
    This should fix the issue where, after a gateway restart, follow-up messages in recently joined threads could require a mention again because newer participation entries were being dropped from saved state
    
    I also added regression coverage for fully persisted thread state

[2026-04-21T20:27:00.016000+00:00] juanesmith (id=1496245893924388894)
    Hello, guys. This is a PR for some skills for reading books effectively, aiming to work together with the LLM Wiki skill. I noticed that Hermes was having a lot of problems when I ask it to read a book. This skill implements a system in which:
    
    1. An orchestrator agent spawns subagents that read chunks of the book 
    2. The subagents extract insights following the Zettelkasten philosophy 
    3. After all subagents finished, a general synthesis/polish session happens with all the subagents' initial notes  
    4. The polished Zettelkasten notes are added to the LLM Wiki 
    
    Happy to receive any feedback, this is my second attempt to contribute to the project 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13671
    [embed] feat(skills): add read-book and book-to-notes skills by juancrfig ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13671

[2026-04-21T21:34:00.593000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1496262757446717610)
    <@387972437901312000>  fix(session): serialize **JSONL **transcript appends under existing lock - PR #13695
    
    SessionStore.append_to_transcript was writing to JSONL files without holding any lock. Under concurrent gateway activity (multiple platform adapters delivering to the same session simultaneously), two threads could interleave f.write() calls and produce merged/corrupted JSONL lines - silently breaking transcript replay and session tooling.
    
    Fix reuses the existing self._lock already used for the sessions index. SQLite path intentionally left outside - it handles its own serialization internally. Zero new primitives, **3 line change**.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13695
    [embed] fix(session): serialize JSONL transcript appends under existing loc... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13695

[2026-04-21T21:41:47.494000+00:00] esi1la (id=1496264715771314386)
    Hii
    
    This PR makes Feishu dedup state loading more resilient. Previously, if the persisted message_ids state contained a malformed timestamp, the adapter could fail during startup when converting that value with float(...). With this change, malformed timestamp entries are skipped while valid dedup records are preserved.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13632
    [embed] fix(feishu): tolerate malformed dedup timestamps by Es1la · Pull R... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13632

[2026-04-21T21:45:20.268000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1496265608210419868)
    <@387972437901312000>  fix(ssh): add scp availability check to preflight validation - PR #13702
    
    _ensure_ssh_available() only checked for ssh but SSHEnvironment uses scp for file uploads. On minimal systems where ssh exists but scp doesn't, the preflight check passes silently and fails at upload time with a cryptic "scp failed: command not found" instead of a clear early error.
    
    One-line fix - extends the existing preflight pattern to also check shutil.which("scp"), matching the same error format already used for ssh.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13702
    [embed] fix(ssh): add scp availability check to preflight validation by spr... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13702

[2026-04-21T22:19:17.539000+00:00] yukikedy (id=1496274153144324137)
    Fixes a gateway session isolation bug where /resume could list or switch to titled sessions from another user on the same platform.
    
    Gateway /resume previously filtered named sessions only by platform source, so two users on Telegram/Discord/etc. sharing the same state.db could see each other’s titled sessions and resume them by title. This change scopes gateway resume listing and title resolution by both platform source and user_id.
    
    Also preserves user_id when creating branched gateway sessions so branched conversations remain visible under the same scoped lookup behavior.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13608#issuecomment-4291550871
    [embed] Scope gateway /resume sessions to the current user by Yukipukii1 ·... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13608

[2026-04-21T23:26:47.102000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1496291138242351134)
    gmgn I just submitted a PR for issue #380.
    
    The skill covers the full workflow for Phase 1 MVP (100% coverage) + Phase 2 (80% coverage): research, plan approval via `clarify()`, parallel worker spawning via `terminal(background=True)`, worktree isolation, PR creation, and progress tracking. I also added two phases that weren't in the original spec: automated merging with a hard `clarify()` gate before any merge, and post-merge testing with a self-healing cleanup worker if regressions appear.
    
    Tested on 20+ projects (`requests→httpx`, `sync→async`, `os.path→pathlib`, `print→logging`, etc.) with 6-12 parallel workers. Zero regressions across all runs.
    
    One thing I wanted to flag: there's already an open PR #8194 for the same issue, but it won't work in Hermes. `hermes -q` is not a valid command for this execution, `simplify` skill doesn't exist in Hermes, workers have no scope restrictions, stale mocks will fail after merge, and that's just a few of the issues. The entire skill is only 58 lines, a skeleton based on the issue description, not adapted to how Hermes actually works.
    
    My PR is fully adapted to Hermes and has been thoroughly tested on the agent itself. Not sure why it was marked as duplicate given all these differences. Happy to get feedback, PR here: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13693
    
    <:doge_hacker:1151280269836435456>
    [attachment] IMAGE_2026-04-22_022609.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1496291138683011112/IMAGE_2026-04-22_022609.jpg?ex=6a01bcf7&is=6a006b77&hm=dcb1094927eeb6cb3dae9aa4d407ced6aa137d6dc67e9033e92e28d5839e5cdc&

[2026-04-22T01:54:59.366000+00:00] smartyi. (id=1496328435101073571)
    Hey all — opened a PR that fixes a crash in the QQBot setup wizard and cleans up the onboard flow. Summary below.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13524
    
    Three issues addressed in one refactor:
    
    **1. Segfault under uvloop (bug fix)**
    The QR polling loop used `asyncio.new_event_loop()` manually. When the QR code expired, `loop.close()` was called in the `EXPIRED` branch — and then again unconditionally in the `finally` block. Double-closing an event loop is safe with CPython's default loop, but `uvloop` (a C extension) will access freed memory and segfault. Non-deterministic, only triggered when `uvloop` is installed.
    
    Fix: dropped `async` entirely. `create_bind_task` and `poll_bind_result` are simple one-shot HTTP POSTs — no concurrency needed. Replaced with synchronous `httpx.Client`, no event loop required.
    
    **2. QR scan reliability (`ERROR_CORRECT_L` → `ERROR_CORRECT_M`)**
    The previous renderer used `ERROR_CORRECT_L` (7% redundancy). Two terminal-specific failure modes make this insufficient:
    - Terminal line height > character height → horizontal gaps between QR rows (regular data loss pattern)
    - Screen glare → localized occlusion
    
    `ERROR_CORRECT_M` (15%) covers both. QR version increases by one level, size impact is small.
    
    **3. Consolidate onboard logic into the qqbot package**
    ~90 lines of QQBot-specific logic (`_qqbot_render_qr`, `_qqbot_qr_flow`) were living in `gateway.py`. Feishu and DingTalk both keep their onboard flows fully inside their platform packages. Moved everything into `onboard.py`, exposed a single `qr_register()` entry-point.
    
    Happy to receive any feedback 😀
    [embed] feat(qqbot): optimize qr scan-to-configure onboarding flow by WideL... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13524

[2026-04-22T02:57:34.962000+00:00] millan1984 (id=1496344187212402812)
    I submit a PR for spliting zai model provider to 4 for easy configuration for the z.ai users (global direct, china direct, global coding plan, china coding plan). Happy to get feedback.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13500
    
    Summary
    Z.AI ships four distinct billing/endpoint combinations (Global direct, China direct, Coding Plan Global, Coding Plan China) behind one user-facing name. The single pre-existing zai provider tried to paper over this at runtime by probing endpoints with the user's key to find the working one — slow, fragile, and gave users no way to pin a plan. This PR splits zai into four explicit Hermes providers so users pick their plan once and credentials resolve deterministically.
    
    Display names, model catalogs, and capability metadata are sourced from models.dev's per-plan entries.
    [embed] feat(providers): split zai into 4 plans (Global/China × direct API... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13500

[2026-04-22T08:10:34.699000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1496422955138420776)
    🐛 fix(ssh): wrap IPv6 hosts in brackets for SCP destination - PR #13920 <@387972437901312000> 
    
    SSHEnvironment builds SCP destinations as user@host:path. IPv6 addresses contain colons, making them ambiguous with SCP's host/path separator - SCP misparsed the destination and failed silently.
    
    Fix: detect ":" in host and wrap with brackets → user@[2001:db8::1]:/path, matching RFC 3986 and OpenSSH spec. IPv4/hostname paths completely unaffected. 2-line change.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13920
    [embed] fix(ssh): wrap IPv6 hosts in brackets for SCP destination by sprmn2... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13920

[2026-04-22T14:37:58.251000+00:00] rnijhara (id=1496520445661544571)
    Opened a PR to fix Slack's reaction and typing indicator lifecycle.
    
    On Discord, Hermes does 👀 → works → ✅ in that order. On Slack it was immediately flipping 👀 to ✅ before doing any actual work, because reactions were wrapped around handle_message() which returns instantly after spawning a background task.
    
    Also fixes a bug where stop_typing() was never implemented, so "Hermes is thinking..." got stuck sometimes.
    
    Now aligns with the Discord/Telegram pattern: on_processing_start / on_processing_complete callbacks bracket the real work. All 129 Slack tests pass, I have also manually tested this.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14028
    [embed] fix(gateway/slack): align reaction lifecycle with Discord/Telegram ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14028
