[2026-04-22T19:48:54.750000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1496598696689664051)
    It gives plugins earlier + deeper access to the agent pipeline (before auth and during runtime) instead of only late, limited hooks.
    Before: plugins only saw events after session/agent start and got limited context (IDs, not the agent itself).
    Now: plugins can
    intercept raw inbound messages (even unauthorized ones)
    access live agent state during execution
    optionally modify behavior or stop handling early
    Net effect for plugin builders:
    from “observe late, limited control” → “hook early + full control / introspection”

[2026-04-22T21:17:21.319000+00:00] bobashopcashier (id=1496620954053251072)
    Issue #14158:
    validator has no stop_reason awareness
    Trigger pattern described precisely (text + trivial tool call in same turn → empty end_turn next turn)
    Real-world repro with the exact Discord session ID (20260420_113639_77768db5)
    Correct callout that downstream code (normalizer + stop_reason_map) already handles empty content safely — so no additional changes needed
    Proposed fix is return getattr(response, "stop_reason", None) == "end_turn"
    
    PR #14159:
    One-line fix (literally: if empty, valid iff end_turn) — exactly the right scope
    Regression test guards the other stop_reason cases (tool_use, etc.) still get rejected
    22 tests pass on targeted suite, 158 pass on adjacent regression suites

[2026-04-23T03:46:26.847000+00:00] aslaen.eth (id=1496718872294719569)
    What this fixes
    hermes profile import was reconciling the explicit target name too late.
    
    Before this change, the import flow extracted the archive directly into the live
    profiles/ root and only then renamed the extracted directory if --name was
    different from the archive’s top-level folder.
    
    That created a bad edge case:
    
    an archive rooted at victim/
    imported as --name renamed
    while a real victim profile already exists
    
    In that case, the import could touch the live victim profile before the rename
    step, which breaks profile isolation and can lead to unintended overwrite/move
    behavior.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14325
    [embed] fix(profiles): stage profile imports before target name reconciliat... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14325

[2026-04-23T19:14:15.476000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1496952363448143984)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    Amazing, it worked like a charm for the local plugins.
    While refining and creating new plugins, under heavy gateway restarting while I was repeatedly rebuilding slash commands , suddenly hit discord 429 responses (discord rate limit of 200 native command registrations per discord server from PUT requests) and spotted a problem that exposed an issue in Hermes’ startup command registration flow for the built-in commands like `/new`, `/reset`, `/model`, `/reasoning`, `/personality`, `/retry`, `/undo`, `/status`, `/sethome`, `/stop`, `/compress`, `/title`, `/resume`, `/usage`, `/provider`, `/help`, `/insights`, `/reload-mcp`, `/voice`, `/update`, `/restart`, `/approve`, `/deny`, `/thread`, `/queue`, `/background`, `/btw`, and `/skill`.
    
    Before this PR:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14648
    Hermes was calling `tree.sync()` during post-connect initialization. That means startup reconciliation was coarse-grained: even if the remote global commands were already correct, reconnects could still trigger unnecessary command writes.
    
    In practice, that becomes noticeable in restart-heavy workflows. Once commands are already registered, everything appears to keep working, but repeated restarts can still burn write budget and surface intermittent 429s from Discord’s command registration API.
    
    What this PR changes is the right direction technically:
    - fetch existing global commands
    - compare desired vs existing payloads
    - skip unchanged commands
    - `PATCH` changed commands
    - `POST` only missing commands
    - delete stale commands one by one
    
    So the important improvement is not just “sync works,” but that startup sync becomes diff-based instead of blindly reapplying the whole command set every time.
    [embed] fix(gateway/discord): add safe startup slash sync policy by Magaav ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14648
    [reply to teknium msg=1496672433485250620]

[2026-04-23T21:30:35.129000+00:00] harryy1_1_81973 (id=1496986671399309485)
    Summary
    Narrow the --replace scoped-lock cleanup so it only removes lock records that belong to the gateway process being replaced.
    
    Before this change, the --replace path called release_all_scoped_locks() with no ownership filter, which could delete unrelated live scoped locks in the shared machine-local lock directory. That weakened the per-resource exclusivity guard for other active gateways running on the same machine.
    
    Pr: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14750
    [embed] fix(gateway): only clear scoped locks for the replaced process by h... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14750

[2026-04-23T21:35:51.349000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1496987997722251356)
    <@387972437901312000>, I opened one more - PR #14751, small but nasty: GATEWAY_HEALTH_TIMEOUT env var is parsed with bare float() at module level in web_server.py. 
    
    Any non-numeric value crashes the entire web server at import with no useful error. Wrapped it in try/except with a warning log and 3.0s fallback.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14751
    [embed] fix(web_server): guard GATEWAY_HEALTH_TIMEOUT against invalid env v... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14751

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14308
    
    This intends to fix the Hermes auxiliary routing side of the summary/compression model issue we hit while testing hermes-lcm. The specific problem is that auxiliary.compression.provider: main was not inheriting the live session model cleanly, which can cause compression calls to route through the wrong provider/model shape.
    
    The downstream LCM-side companion is hermes-lcm #71 / #72, which handles explicit LCM summary/expansion overrides like LCM_SUMMARY_MODEL=cerebras/gpt-oss-120b. That PR is green now. #14308 is the upstream Hermes Agent piece for the shared auxiliary compression lane.

[2026-04-24T00:16:49.611000+00:00] 0xbyt4 (id=1497028507409055864)
    <@387972437901312000> Now I need to sleep but some notes : The ceiling is the vision model, not the driver. Anthropic's and Codex's native paths work because the model is the reasoning engine  trained end-to-end on screenshot , coordinate. Giving a hallucination prone open model "correct" SOM element IDs doesn't fix that  , blind clicking    stays blind. SOM genuinely helps on AX-rich native apps (semantic press/show_menu beats pixel guessing), but Chromium web content and canvas surfaces (Blender, Figma, DAWs, games) have empty AX trees by design, so the model is back to raw pixel targeting  exactly where open models underperform.

[2026-04-24T04:22:55.411000+00:00] harryy1_1_81973 (id=1497090439663325264)
    Just pushed #14893 to fix remote environment sync issues for Windows users
    
    The Problem: On Windows hosts, remote POSIX paths (like /root/.hermes/...) were often getting mangled into backslash paths, breaking mkdir and sync-back operations on SSH, Modal, and Daytona backends.
    
    The Fix:
    POSIX Enforcement: Used PurePosixPath to ensure remote paths stay POSIX, regardless of the host OS.
    Symlink Fallback: Fixed SSH upload failures on Windows when admin privileges for symlinks are missing.
    Hardened Extraction: Replaced Windows-sensitive temp file logic with a more robust, traversal-resistant tar extractor for sync-back.
    Tests: Added 57 passing tests covering these Windows-specific edge cases.
    
    pr: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14893
    [embed] fix(environments): handle remote POSIX paths on Windows hosts by hh... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14893

    this is a complex question. suggest asking your own hermes with a project plan on how to use something like subagent driven development with the coding plans that you have
    [reply to tyrael08 msg=1497326011895779449]

[2026-04-24T20:46:00.836000+00:00] minddragon (id=1497337842467340461)
    this isn't a "button" type of application. this is a way of taking something ... call it "app a" and using an autonomous loop to go from a known working percentage or duration or something measurable and forcibly improving it using autonomous variations ... the agent is forced to look at it using different prompts over a prolonged duration. it usually produces results. it's really good at improving models but it can also improve...well...anything that is measurable
    [reply to imwithpedro msg=1497337246318067713]

[2026-04-24T21:20:26.119000+00:00] yukikedy (id=1497346504891961347)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, here are two PRs ready for your review
    
    1. fix(env): safely quote ~/ subpaths in wrapped cd commands (#15098)
    This PR addresses an issue where ~/... paths with spaces were incorrectly parsed by cd commands. It ensures these paths are safely quoted via $HOME, maintaining them as single shell words, thus fixing a cross-platform bug in the shared environment wrapper.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15098
    
    2. fix(gateway/config): coerce quoted boolean values in config parsing (#15334)
    This PR fixes an edge case where quoted boolean values (e.g., "false") in gateway config fields were not correctly coerced to boolean False due to Python's bool("false") evaluating to True. It applies an existing helper to ensure proper boolean normalization for affected fields.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15334

    <@387972437901312000>, I opened PR #15350   _submit_anthropic_pkce() in web_server.py retrieves sess under _oauth_sessions_lock but writes back to sess["status"] and sess["error_message"] outside the lock. 
    Concurrent poll + submit or a GC expiry mid-submit can race with these writers, producing inconsistent session state. 
    Wrapped all 4 write sites in the lock.

[2026-04-24T22:10:05.018000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1497358999299948575)
    **+ **  I  opened PR #15364 -  found a silent failure in _load_auth_store() in hermes_cli/auth.py.
    The problem: when auth.json gets corrupted (disk error, interrupted write, manual edit gone wrong), the except
    
     Exception: block silently returns an empty store with zero logging. From the user's perspective this looks exactly like being logged out everywhere , no error message, no hint of what happened. Worse, the next _save_auth_store() call overwrites the corrupt file entirely, destroying any chance of recovery.
    
    The fix: before returning the empty store, copy the corrupt file to auth.json.corrupt as a backup, then log a warning with the exception message and the backup path. Happy path behavior is completely unchanged -only the error branch is touched.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15364
    [embed] fix(auth): preserve corrupt auth.json and warn instead of silently ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15364

[2026-04-24T23:30:15.794000+00:00] simbamx (id=1497379177157099642)
    fix(gateway): resume should follow compression continuations
    
    I identified and resolved an issue where the /resume command in the gateway was incorrectly restoring the compressed parent session instead of the active continuation child. To align the gateway's functionality with the CLI, I implemented a solution that correctly resolves the resumed session ID by utilizing SessionDB.resolve_resume_session_id().
    
    My key contributions include
    Updating GatewayRunner._handle_resume_command() in gateway/run.py: This ensures the resolved session ID is properly routed, allowing the gateway to switch to the correct live transcript.
    
    Adding a comprehensive regression test in tests/gateway/test_resume_command.py: This test specifically creates a compressed parent and child continuation, verifying that /resume correctly switches to the child and accurately counts its messages. The tests passed with 10 successful runs.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15403
    [embed] fix(gateway): resume should follow compression continuations by sim... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15403

[2026-04-25T00:32:03.787000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1497394729606975620)
    While testing my `simplify` skill, I discovered a bug and documented previously undocumented configuration parameters.
    
    **1. Delegation parameter documentation (PR #14568)**
    I dug into the `delegate_task` source and found two parameters that were already implemented in the code but not documented anywhere: `max_concurrent_children` and `max_spawn_depth`. Without them, users assume the limit of 3 parallel agents is hardcoded, while in reality it can be increased up to 30 via `config.yaml`. <@387972437901312000> documented them in the reference docs a few days earlier (PR #13745), and I extended the coverage by updating three areas he didn't touch: `cli-config.yaml.example`, the `delegate_task` tool description, and the practical delegation guide.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14568
    
    **2. Deadlock fix for sub-agent approval prompts (PR #14685)**
    When a sub-agent encounters a "dangerous command," Hermes shows the prompt `[o]nce | [s]ession | [a]lways | [d]eny`. But no input is possible, the input field doesn't respond, even `Ctrl+C` doesn't work. Root cause: `cli.py` registers the approval callback only in the main thread. A sub-agent running in `ThreadPoolExecutor` doesn't inherit this callback, falls back to `input()` from a background thread, and competes with `prompt_toolkit` for stdin. A classic deadlock. Fix: `_subagent_auto_approve` via `ThreadPoolExecutor(initializer=...)` — the sub-agent gets its own autonomous approval callback with `logger.warning` for audit trail, and never touches the parent TUI. Works in both single and batch modes.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14685
    
    **3. Cron job output chaining (PR #14190, closes #5439)**
    Users were forced to manually pass data between cron jobs via files. Job A produces output, but job B can't access it without custom scripting. I added a `context_from` field to the cron job schema: you specify a job ID, and the latest output of that job is automatically injected into the prompt before each run. It reads from the existing atomic storage at `~/.hermes/cron/output/{job_id}/` instead of introducing a new shared directory, reusing existing infrastructure. Includes path traversal protection, truncation at 8K characters, and graceful degradation on `OSError`. 14 tests.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14190
    
    **Hope this is useful  have a great weekend everyone!** <:o7:1151261546111053924>

[2026-04-25T03:15:57.230000+00:00] harryy1_1_81973 (id=1497435974056280084)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> 
    quick heads up!
    
    Just pushed a fix for a subtle config bug that was causing some unexpected VACUUM operations even when explicitly disabled. This is PR #15411: fix(config): respect quoted false for session vacuum_after_prune.
    
    The Problem:
    Turns out, if sessions.vacuum_after_prune was set to "false" (as a string) in the config, Python's bool("false") was evaluating to True. This meant the VACUUM command was still running at startup, adding unnecessary latency and I/O, especially on larger state.db files. It was a classic quoted-boolean gotcha that slipped through on some startup paths.
    
    The Fix:
    I've introduced a shared coerce_bool() helper in utils.py and routed all relevant call sites (CLI and gateway auto-maintenance) through it. This ensures that quoted booleans ("false") are now correctly interpreted as False, aligning behavior with the rest of the gateway config parsing. It's a minimal, targeted fix – no changes to DB logic, scheduling, or config schema.
    
    Testing:
    Added new regression tests in tests/cli/test_state_db_auto_maintenance.py and tests/gateway/test_state_db_auto_maintenance.py to cover both startup paths. They confirm "false" and False both correctly result in vacuum=False. All tests passed with 178 successful runs.
    
    Ready for your review!
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15411
    [embed] fix(config): respect quoted false for session vacuum_after_prune by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15411

[2026-04-25T18:02:54.477000+00:00] wysie_ (id=1497659183368114187)
    HINDSIGHT_TIMEOUT only controls request timeout. It does not help when the embedded daemon has already exited and localhost:8888 is not listening. In that state, calls can fail immediately with connection-refused errors such as:
    
    Cannot connect to host 127.0.0.1:8888 ... [Connect call failed]
    
    This patch separates API request timeout from embedded daemon lifecycle timeout and makes the provider recover from the stale-client-after-idle-shutdown case.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15723
    [embed] Fix Hindsight embedded daemon recovery after idle shutdown by Wysie... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15723

[2026-04-26T02:45:00.539000+00:00] yukikedy (id=1497790574395261192)
    Hey, two PRs have been opened for review
    
    fix(gateway): bypass slash commands during pending update prompts (#15849): This PR addresses a gateway control-flow bug where pending update prompts could incorrectly intercept and consume legitimate slash commands. Once merged, recognized slash commands will bypass this interception and dispatch normally, ensuring smooth command execution.
    
    fix(security): treat quoted false as false in browser SSRF guards (#15858): This security PR aims to fix an issue where quoted 'false' values in allow_private_urls configurations were being treated as true, potentially disabling private URL blocking. Once merged, explicit boolean coercion will be used to correctly interpret these values, enhancing our SSRF protection.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15849
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15858
    [embed] fix(gateway): bypass slash commands during pending update prompts b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15849
    [embed] fix(security): treat quoted false as false in browser SSRF guards b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15858

[2026-04-26T03:43:54.038000+00:00] teknium (id=1497805394964385832)
    <@&1149869525567799297>s 
    
    I need your help. I am designing the system for multi-agent profile coordination. There's a lot of levels to this, and I know we want them to be able to coordinate over discord and slack and telegram etc, but I think that's for another time - and I want to try to bring something that has some level of standards and proven value out first.
    
    That's why I'm thinking more along the lines of Cline's Kanban mixed with some Paperclip ideas, stripping away some messier things and making it clean, and adjusting it to work with agent profiles rather than what other systems might use. 
    
    Basic layout, will have a PR for people to help review and comment on probably Monday. Looking forward to hearing thoughts. And your use cases of multi-agent, whether this works for everyone or just coding, etc.

[2026-04-26T04:10:02.055000+00:00] teknium (id=1497811971704094831)
    ```    Fair pushback on both fronts. Let me actually answer, then talk polish.
        
        How this differs from delegate_task
        
        They look similar from 10 feet but they're different primitives.
        
        | | delegate_task | kanban |
        |---|---|---|
        | Shape | RPC call (fork → join) | Message queue + state machine |
        | Parent | Blocks until child returns | Fire-and-forget; parent done after create |
        | Child | Ephemeral — dies after returning summary | Ephemeral per run, but the task persists |
        | Context passing | goal + context string, passed in | Task body + comment thread + parent results, read from board |
        | Identity | Anonymous subagent | Named profile with own HERMES_HOME/memory |
        | Resumability | None. Failed = failed. | Blocked → unblocked → re-run; crashed → reclaimed in 15min |
        | Human interposition | Not really — child runs headless | Any time: comment, unblock, reassign |
        | Multiple agents per task | One subagent per call | A task can be run by N agents over its life (retry, review) |
        | Dependency graph | Manual, inside parent's head | First-class links, dispatcher enforces order |
        | Audit trail | Lost when parent's context compresses | Durable rows in SQLite forever |```

[2026-04-26T16:21:16.780000+00:00] urkella (id=1497995995638661151)
    Wow, thats a great approach. To answer your question about use cases outside of pure software development: I also work in chemistry and modeling, and this system fits my specialty as well. Separation of concerns is very important in chemistry. Asking a single, general-purpose agent to "build a model of a mill" rarely provides the depth  of exploration we need. How would I arrange the profiles for such a task:
    Scheduler Agent - breaks down  the complex task of modeling into steps and ensures we havent gone off track (a very common problem)
    Search Agent - acts as external information processor to collect specific chemical data (similar to your backend engineeer #1). 
    Main Builder -  builds the actual model based on the data.
    Tester - validates the model and provides feedback.  
    The idea of ​​coordinating actions through a common board, where the Main Builde rrests until the Search Agent receives data, is a good solution for complex tasks outside of programming.
    [reply to teknium msg=1497805394964385832]

[2026-04-26T23:30:18.850000+00:00] w0lfgang88 (id=1498103965705965653)
    <@387972437901312000>, quick priority ask on <@417568242307956737> republished Hermes Agent PRs:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13370
    
    I think #13370 should be bumped above P3. It is not just plugin polish. It maps directly to the hermes-lcm#68 user report where LCM loses logical continuity after Hermes creates a new physical session during compression. The visible symptom is:
    
    compression_count: 1
    store_messages: 0
    dag_nodes: 0
    
    Tosko updated #13370 to pass `boundary_reason="compression"` so external context engines can treat that split as continuation instead of a fresh `/new`.
    
    This is one of the cases behind “people’s complaints are fixed in these PRs.” Users are hitting behavior that is already addressed in the open PRs, but they do not have those fixes locally.
    
    GitHub currently shows #13370 as dirty/conflicting, so Tosko can clean it once someone is ready to review or merge. Could you take a look or bump the priority?
    [embed] Improve external context engine plugin support by Tosko4 · Pull Re... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13370

[2026-04-27T02:19:45.202000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1498146606476951673)
    <@387972437901312000>, I opened 4 PRs - all bugs found in the v0.11.0 new files:
    
    #16232 - hermes_cli/timeouts.py: Both get_provider_request_timeout() and get_provider_stale_timeout() wrapped the load_config import in try/except ImportError but left the actual load_config() call unprotected. Corrupt YAML or a parse error would raise instead of returning None safely.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16232
    
    #16243 - gateway/whatsapp_identity.py: expand_whatsapp_aliases() interpolated untrusted WhatsApp identifiers directly into filenames (lid-mapping-{current}.json) with no validation — ../secrets would escape the session directory. Also replaced a bare except Exception: continue with targeted OSError/JSONDecodeError + debug log.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16243
    
    #16244 - agent/shell_hooks.py: bool(cfg_val) for hooks_auto_accept meant hooks_auto_accept: "false" (YAML string) silently enabled auto-approval of all shell hooks. Replaced with type-aware parsing matching the existing HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS env var logic.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16244
    
    #16245 - tools/discord_tool.py: _search_members() and _fetch_messages() call min(limit, 100) assuming limit is int. Models can pass strings, causing TypeError. Added int() coercion with safe fallbacks.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16245
    [embed] fix(timeouts): guard load_config() call against runtime exceptions ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16232
    [embed] fix(whatsapp_identity): guard against path traversal and silent map... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16243
    [embed] fix(shell_hooks): strict boolean parsing for hooks_auto_accept conf... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16244

[2026-04-27T07:07:43.163000+00:00] smartyi. (id=1498219075497889914)
    hello <@387972437901312000>, we've gone ahead and extracted gateway/platforms/qqbot/ into a standalone qqbot-agent-sdk package, would this direction work for you before we open a PR? 
    
    The refactor is done locally and the numbers look pretty clean:
    
    - ~5,000 lines removed from hermes-agent (the full QQ Open Platform protocol layer — WebSocket lifecycle, HTTP API client, event parsing, media upload, approval keyboards, session persistence)
    - ~1,000 lines kept as a thin adapter in gateway/platforms/qqbot.py — just the hermes glue (config parsing, BasePlatformAdapter interface, MessageEvent conversion)
    - hermes no longer owns or maintains QQ-specific protocol logic, WebSocket reconnection, or Open Platform API internals
    
    The SDK ships as qqbot-agent-sdk on PyPI (MIT). hermes depends on it via a new [qqbot] extra in pyproject.toml, same pattern as python-telegram-bot for Telegram and discord.py for Discord. Protocol fixes and QQ API changes are handled in the SDK repo and reach hermes users via a version bump — no hermes PR needed for routine maintenance.

[2026-04-27T09:34:34.044000+00:00] feranmi104 (id=1498256031011049532)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>  another PR for you............ PR #16477 — fixed the P1 deadlock in tools/approval.py where the approval prompt froze completely when triggered from a background/subagent thread.
    Root cause: TLS doesn't propagate to child threads, so _callback_tls.approval was None in delegate threads, causing fallthrough to raw input() which deadlocked against prompt_toolkit's stdin reader on MainThread.
    Fix: new _try_pt_run_in_terminal() helper that routes through prompt_toolkit's async event loop via asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe + app.run_in_terminal(). Fixes #15216
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16477
    [embed] fix(approval): route fallback input() through prompt_toolkit run_in... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16477

[2026-04-27T11:09:13.723000+00:00] yukikedy (id=1498279853311398000)

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16504
    [embed] fix(auth): make provider config writes atomic by Yukipukii1 · Pull... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16504

[2026-04-27T17:30:31.599000+00:00] amazon1148 (id=1498375810078347387)
    Otel is Open Telemetry. 
    
    Basically the hyperscalers and anyone running hardware is dumping the time series logs into Otel format and sending it to be analyzed and collated for insight into what the agents are doing and how the hardware(CPUs/GPUs/Networks/Storage)/virtualization are responding to the agent's actions.
    
    Think of it as the meta-data needed for that big external optimization loop.
    [reply to teknium msg=1494884880008347710]

    - 2 line fix to wrap submitted callables in contextvars.copy_context().run

[2026-04-27T18:40:27.269000+00:00] mexcex (id=1498393407993679973)
    Stale Session Recency during Gateway Mirroring 
    
    Hey <@387972437901312000> ! I just submitted a PR to fix a state mismatch in the gateway. Previously, when content was mirrored via send_message, the updated_at timestamp in sessions.json wasn't being refreshed. This caused active sessions to appear stale, affecting pruning logic and session ordering.
    
    Key Changes
    Refreshes session metadata immediately after a successful mirror write.
    Uses atomic_json_write for crash-safe persistence.
    Includes regression tests in tests/gateway/test_mirror.py.
    
     https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16674
    [embed] fix(gateway): refresh session recency on mirrored transcript writes... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16674

[2026-04-28T02:18:27.723000+00:00] teknium (id=1498508669372076074)
    It should be using your main agent, not "none" so did they set it to None??

[2026-04-28T02:22:42.970000+00:00] teknium (id=1498509739955589200)
    Defaults are right — auxiliary.compression.provider: auto, auxiliary.compression.model: "". Empty model means "use the main model" per _resolve_auto() step 1. So by default, compression DOES use the main agent model. The user's setup only fails if they explicitly override auxiliary.compression.model (or .provider) to something that doesn't work on their main provider.
        
        Short answer for Teknium:
        
        They DO, by default. auxiliary.compression.provider defaults to "auto" and auxiliary.compression.model defaults to "" — which _resolve_auto() step 1 interprets as "use the main provider + main model". Exactly what you'd expect.
        
        Vivienn's scenario is a user-specific misconfiguration: they manually set auxiliary.compression.model (or .provider) to something their main provider doesn't actually serve — e.g. a Google/Gemini model name while their main provider is Anthropic. The compressor already has a 404-retry path at context_compressor.py:898-912 that catches 404, 503, model_not_found, does not exist, no available channel and retries on the main model. So most misconfigurations auto-recover silently.

[2026-04-28T03:35:03.950000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1498527947345236069)
    <@387972437901312000>,  I opened 4 more PR
    #16687 - tools/browser_supervisor.py: _SupervisorRegistry.get_or_start() returned cached supervisor without checking if the thread or event loop was still alive. A crashed supervisor would be silently reused. Now verifies _thread.is_alive() and _loop.is_running() before returning cached instance.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16687
    
    #16688 -tools/discord_tool.py: _capability_cache was a single global dict shared across all tokens. Token rotation or multi-token usage would return capabilities from the wrong token. Replaced with a Dict keyed by token.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16688
    
    #16694 -plugins/memory/honcho/client.py: Three raw int() calls parsing dialecticMaxChars, messageMaxChars, and dialecticMaxInputChars had no guards. A malformed honcho.json value would abort provider init entirely. Added _parse_int_config() helper following the existing _parse_context_tokens() pattern.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16694
    
    #16698 - plugins/memory/honcho/session.py: _get_peer() and _get_or_create_honcho_session() accessed _peers_cache and _sessions_cache without _cache_lock, while all write paths use it consistently. Wrapped both unguarded read sites under the existing lock.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16698
    [embed] fix(browser_supervisor): verify thread and loop health before retur... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16687
    [embed] fix(discord_tool): key capability cache by token instead of single ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16688
    [embed] fix(honcho): replace raw int() config parsing with safe helper by s... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16694
    [embed] fix(honcho): guard _peers_cache and _sessions_cache reads under _ca... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16698

[2026-04-28T04:05:02.982000+00:00] thomasjhn (id=1498535493032214589)
    Dropping a small but critical reliability fix for gateway sessions. Transactional SQLite transcript rewrites 
    
    Previously, rewrite_transcript() was doing a risky two-step:
    clear_messages()  then re-inserting messages one by one.
    If anything blew up mid-way, SQLite could end up with a half written or completely empty transcript 
    
    This PR fixes that by making the whole rewrite atomic.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16810
    [embed] fix(session): make SQLite transcript rewrites transactional by Thom... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16810

    I opened PR #16858: fix(security): isolate interactive sudo password cache per session. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16858
    
    It fixes a session-boundary leak where an interactive sudo password cached in one long-lived runtime session could be reused by another session in the same process. The change scopes the cache by HERMES_SESSION_KEY, with callback/thread fallback, while preserving same-session sudo convenience.
    [embed] fix(security): isolate interactive sudo password cache per session ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16858

[2026-04-28T11:51:24.116000+00:00] scottermonkey (id=1498652854414610552)
    Wanting to squeeze all the performance I can out of the box I have Hermes on, I upgraded Python from 3.11 to 3.12. Python has been my main jam for last 10 years, and I had a vague memory that one update had showed a big performance leap. What I had found:
     3.11 -> 3.12:
    - typing_runtime_protocols: ~349% faster
    - asyncio_tcp: ~56.8% faster
    - generators: ~53.2% faster
    - asyncio_tcp_ssl: ~46.7% faster
    - json_dumps: ~22.9% faster
    Wondering if 3.12 broke anything that Hermes depends on, why 3.11 was picked as default when Hermes installed?

[2026-04-28T17:30:43.126000+00:00] willy_jhason (id=1498738246291689522)
    opened a PR for a bug ive been hitting — topic-bound skills dont re-inject after /reset or /new
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17096
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/6508
    
    tl;dr: _handle_reset_command() creates a new session but never sets was_auto_reset = True, so _is_new_session evaluates False on the next message and the skill auto-load gate gets skipped
    
    fix is 8 lines — just set was_auto_reset + auto_reset_reason after reset_session() returns
    
    saw PR #10839 tried the same fix but got closed without merge. this one's a minimal patch, same approach
    
    would appreciate a review 🙏

    HERMES_TUI_SLASH_TIMEOUT_S=abc) raises ValueError during import, preventing TUI gateway from starting entirely with no error message. Wrapped both in try/except with safe fallbacks (45.0s and 4 workers).

[2026-04-29T08:39:29.357000+00:00] feranmi104 (id=1498966945880473641)
    Just dropped a security fix PR 🔐
    PR #17351 — fix(models): the model picker (hermes model) was silently persisting resolved API keys from key_env back into config.yaml. This means API keys could leak into git commits or shared configs without users realizing it.
    Fixed by introducing a _key_from_env guard that prevents env-resolved secrets from being written to disk — only the key_env pointer remains on disk. Fixes #15803 🛡️
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17351
    [embed] fix(models): strip env-resolved api_key before persisting provider ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17351

[2026-04-29T08:42:13.074000+00:00] nmgwddj (id=1498967632559210496)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> I opened a PR https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13062
    
    This PR adds NetEase IM (NIM) channel integration to Hermes Gateway. It is an important addition because it expands Hermes’ messaging-platform coverage and makes Hermes usable in NIM-based and China-facing communication workflows.
    
    NetEase brings nearly 28 years of technology accumulation, and NetEase IM is built on NetEase’s fully self-developed technology stack and end-to-end communication solutions. As of now, NetEase IM has been chosen by more than 2.35 million developers, with average monthly message volume exceeding 310 billion. Because of this scale and adoption, support for NIM is not just another channel integration. It is a meaningful step toward making Hermes more relevant for large-scale, real-world communication scenarios, especially in the China market and in ecosystems where NIM is already a core messaging infrastructure.
    
    I have already resolved the PR conflicts, and the PR is now mergeable:
    head: 53ad2774b26c053ca15a5a00147524239c86440d
    
    Relevant tests were run for the gateway and config changes. There is one unrelated pre-existing test failure in:
    tests/hermes_cli/test_config.py
    but the PR itself is ready for maintainer review and merge.
    This integration is important because it helps Hermes broaden platform compatibility, improves its practical adoption potential, and opens the door for usage in production environments that rely on NetEase IM’s mature messaging capabilities.
    
    Thanks for your time and help.
    [embed] feat: add NetEase IM (NIM) channel integration for Hermes Gateway... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/13062

[2026-04-29T11:59:51.709000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1499017371279560736)
    Before this PR, plugins could only add capabilities.
    After this PR, plugins can control capabilities.
    
    That means production features like approval gates, safety policies, execution routing, tenant isolation, audit hooks, fallback logic, and orchestration layers can be built as plugins instead of core patches—making Hermes Agent actually scalable for real multi-agent production systems.
    The real value is that it enables plugins to create control layers which intercept and influence runtime
    
    Useful for:
    real-time dashboarding
    approval gates before dangerous actions
    policy enforcement (what agents/users are allowed to do)
    execution routing (choose which model, tool, or node should run)
    audit hooks and logging before/after actions
    retries, fallbacks, and failure recovery
    tenant/project isolation in multi-agent environments
    orchestration layers
    observability and guard systems watching background processes
    safe enterprise deployment without constantly patching Hermes Agent core
    [reply to colmeio_66868 msg=1498784908921798801]

[2026-04-29T12:37:38.038000+00:00] nanakotsai (id=1499026876952219709)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, I followed up on the Gemini reasoning fix with PR #17389, linked to issue #16941.
    
    The follow-up is specifically for the official Gemini OpenAI-compat route returning HTTP 400 on `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`. I was able to reproduce that locally, and it turned out the remaining bug was route-specific rather than a general `provider: gemini` problem.
    
    I updated the fix so the thinking config shape now splits by actual `route/base_url` instead of only by provider name. Native Gemini still keeps the existing top-level path, while the official `/v1beta/openai` route uses the nested shape that Gemini expects there.
    
    I re-ran local regression tests and also did a live smoke test against the real official OpenAI-compat endpoint. After the change, the reported HTTP 400 no longer reproduces, and the native Gemini path still works.
    
    The PR is still red in CI, but the Gemini-specific regression is no longer showing up in the current test failure list from what I can see. I also re-ran the supply-chain workflow patterns locally and didn’t find any matching high-risk patterns.

    Hey <@387972437901312000>, quick Hermes Agent PR heads up:
    
    #17660 is the base-CI stabilizer. It fixes the noisy main test-suite failures caused by host/env state leaks, xdist ordering, PTY/TERM assumptions, and one real fast streaming-interrupt race. CI is green and it is mergeable now:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17660
    
    #17645 is separate and smaller. It fixes Codex/OpenAI Responses final-summary calls so orphaned function_call_output history is sanitized before hitting the API. It had CI red because of the base-suite failures, so I’d land #17660 first, then rebase/refresh #17645:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17645
    
    Thanks in advance!

    Just opended another PR........PR #17827 — fix(fallback): fixed a bug where fallback providers defined in custom_providers send requests to the wrong endpoint, inheriting the primary provider's base_url instead of their own.
    Root cause was two gaps in the fallback resolution path across run_agent.py and agent/auxiliary_client.py. The fix correctly looks up the custom provider entry using the pre-normalization provider name before any alias expansion occurs. Fixes #15743 🔧

    Hi any <@&1214801236323467284> for Hermes Agent, I just pushed a PR that would conveniently restart the gateway for both service-managed + manual profile ones. Context is that if in case there are are a lot of created profiles, manually restarting gateway for each of them would take a lot of time and can lead to hit-or-miss situations. [2026-04-30T23:18:55.009000+00:00] minddragon (id=1499550648931192872)
    fyi re: 4.3 --  
    +## Responses API encrypted reasoning pitfall
    +
    +Native xAI can return `reasoning.encrypted_content` when `include: ["reasoning.encrypted_content"]` is requested, but those blobs are opaque to the provider/model that generated them. If Hermes switches an existing session from OpenAI/Codex/GPT Responses to xAI and replays old `codex_reasoning_items`, xAI returns HTTP 400:
    +
    +```text
    +Could not decrypt the provided encrypted_content. Ensure the value is the unmodified encrypted_content from a previous response.
    +```
    +
    +Fix pattern:
    +
    +- Tag stored `codex_reasoning_items` / `codex_message_items` with origin metadata such as `_provider` and `_model` at capture time.
    +- When building xAI Responses input, replay encrypted reasoning/message items only if the tag matches the active xAI provider/model.
    +- Treat legacy untagged encrypted replay items as unsafe for xAI; flatten the assistant text instead.
    +- Verify by converting a historical OpenAI/Codex session with `current_provider="xai"` and checking that the outgoing input contains zero `type="reasoning"` items, then run a direct `/v1/responses` probe.
    +

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18300
    Hi <@387972437901312000> , while playing with the Kanban board, I notice there's something strange. I have my default hermes profile setup a kanban board and assign tasks to other profiles, however, it seems that they don't share the same `kanban.db`. Each spawned workers that runs under the `hermes -p <assignee>` changes the `HERMES_HOME` to the assignee profile directory. So the situation becomes like:
    * default profile: `~/.hermes/kanban.db`
    * worker profile: `~/.hermes/profiles/<profile>/kanban.db`
    Hence, they're on different kanban boards and cannot see the tasks 😢 . So I made a fix to make sure they use the same kanban db source. Thanks for taking a look 🙏
    [embed] fix(kanban): share board path with worker profiles by quocanh261997... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18300

[2026-05-01T08:43:56.220000+00:00] jatin_22 (id=1499692840916094996)
    Hey everyone! Just submitted a PR to finally make the CLI tips "smart." 
    
    Previously, the exclude_recent logic was being ignored, meaning you could see the same tip multiple times in a row. I've integrated SessionDB to persist a history of shown tips in the local state.db.
    
    Key Highlights:
    
    No repeats: Tracks the last 5 tips to ensure variety across restarts and /clear commands.
    Robust performance: Uses lazy imports and graceful fallbacks—if the DB is locked or the disk is full, the CLI still starts instantly with a random tip.
    Auto-rotation: Resets history automatically once you've seen the whole corpus.
    Check it out here: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18311

[2026-05-02T01:06:15.851000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1499940051646288062)
    Continued work on improving `delegate_task` and the multi-agent infrastructure. Managed to ship 4 PRs this week.
    
    **1. Inception prompting for sub-agents (PR #17561)**
    Sub-agents spawned via `delegate_task` were exhibiting four failure modes documented in CAMEL-AI (NeurIPS 2023): role-flipping (agent asks the parent to do the work), instruction echoing (repeats the task without executing it), flake replies ("seems fine" , "needs investigation"), and infinite retry loops. Added role-anchoring, anti-echo, output enforcement, and convergence pressure in `_build_child_system_prompt()`. After this, two test runs of `delegate_task` returned concrete file paths, line counts, and prioritized lists. No role-flip, echo, or vague summaries. Part of #375 Phase 1. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17561
    
    **2. Acceptance criteria + independent judge (PR #17980)**
    `delegate_task` previously returned sub-agent outputs to the parent as-is, without verification. Added an optional `acceptance_criteria` parameter. When specified, a new `_judge_output()` function evaluates the sub-agent's output against the criteria using a cheap auxiliary LLM and returns PASS/FAIL with reasoning. The enriched result includes a `judge` field. Backward compatible: behavior remains unchanged when criteria are not provided. Part of #356 Phase 1. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17980
    
    **3. Best-of-N competitive evaluation (PR #18115)**
    In parallel batch execution, the parent previously received all N results and had to select the best one manually, consuming context tokens. Added an optional `judge` parameter. When used with batch tasks, a new `_judge_best_of_n()` function compares all outputs side-by-side via an independent judge LLM and returns a `winner` object with `task_index` and reasoning at the top level of the result. Includes graceful degradation if the judge is unavailable. Part of #479 Phase 1. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18115
    
    **4. Ruff cleanup (PR #17616)**
    Two pre-existing ruff warnings (F401 unused import, F541 bare f-string) left after the last cleanup. Removed both. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17616
    
    The first three PRs form the foundation for the `simplify` skill (#379): hardened sub-agents + quality gating + Best-of-N selection directly address the quality issues I observed while testing parallel review agents.
    
    P.S. <@387972437901312000> thanks a lot for the comments and feedback on the previous batch of PRs, glad they were useful.
    
    **Hope this batch will be useful as well. Have a great weekend, everyone!**<:o7:1151261546111053924>

[2026-05-02T18:50:41.587000+00:00] duzafizzl (id=1500207923958382713)
    🚀 **Build recap: Discord video -> multimodal analysis pipeline**
    Built a backend flow that takes Discord CDN video attachments, downloads them with strict caps, preprocesses them with ffmpeg, and returns structured JSON to the agent layer.
    
    The main path analyzes short MP4 clips with both video and audio, so the model can reason about motion, scene context, and speech together instead of relying on still frames only.
     When the input is large enough, the analyzed window is split into short overlapping chunks, processed in parallel, and merged back into one result.
    
     A practical default is 4-second chunks with 1-second overlap, which gives better coverage without turning the whole run into one oversized clip.
    
     🧠 **Output contract**
    The model is forced into a strict JSON schema with fields for scene description, detected objects, spoken transcript, speech delivery, and accessibility narration.
    
    That makes the result easy to reuse downstream for agents, memory, or UI rendering without needing another parsing layer.
    
    There is also a keyframe fallback path that uses JPEG stills but keeps the same schema when full video analysis is unavailable.
    
    ⏳ **Long-run behavior**
    
    If the job runs past a soft time threshold, the tool returns a pending state immediately, continues in the background, and delivers the full result later as a follow-up.
    
    That follow-up can also be mirrored back into the same Discord channel, which makes longer multimodal jobs feel usable in chat instead of just timing out.
    
    
     🛠️ **Biggest reliability lesson**
    
    The hardest problem was not model quality but async lifecycle management in the Discord request path.
    
    Temporary event loops were able to kill pending background tasks before delayed follow-ups finished, which meant the analysis succeeded but the final reply never arrived.
    
     The fix was to register background deliveries centrally and drain the loop before shutdown.
    
     🔐 **Guardrails**
    The pipeline only accepts Discord CDN attachment URLs and keeps strict limits around download size, analyzed duration, and chunk payload size.
    
    That keeps the system bounded and makes the video tool safer to expose inside a real chat workflow.
    
     💸 **Operational takeaway**
    Media payload dominates cost and latency in multimodal runs.
    
    Thank god : nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning:free.  Is free
    Parallel chunking improves wall-clock behavior and coverage, but it does not reduce prompt-side load, so concurrency and chunk size still need careful tuning.
    
    We plan to adopt the same pattern for Mioré so he can have a semi life feed for his drone and his mini robot 
    
    The markdown below gives us better insights of schemas we used
    [attachment] MIU_Discord_Video_Omni_Friends_Build_Guide_EN_2.md https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1500207923538825327/MIU_Discord_Video_Omni_Friends_Build_Guide_EN_2.md?ex=6a017c41&is=6a002ac1&hm=ebd515c8a61f929a1b377d8b00144c103e9716a908d4a1a070573cb90ebee860&

[2026-05-03T19:30:14.283000+00:00] 0zi57 (id=1500580263632048299)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19333 Removes the INSECURE_NO_AUTH constant that allowed complete HMAC signature bypass in webhook routes. Setting a route's secret to "INSECURE_NO_AUTH" disabled all authentication, allowing unauthenticated requests to trigger agent runs and deliver messages. All routes now require a valid HMAC secret. Testing should use a dummy secret instead. <@387972437901312000>

[2026-05-03T21:19:13.029000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1500607689120808991)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, I found the Kanban profile-scoped DB bug independently while testing Hermes Kanban workers on my VPS, then opened a duplicate issue/PR before noticing there were already several related ones.
    
    I’ve closed my duplicate issue:
    
    <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/19348>
    
    Main tracking issue seems to be:
    
    <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/18442>
    
    My PR is still open as a competing fix, but I’m happy to close it if maintainers prefer one of the existing approaches:
    
    <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19350>
    
    Related existing PRs I found:
    
    - #18300: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18300>
    - #18503: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18503>
    - #18670: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18670>
    - #18985: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18985>
    - #19037: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19037>
    - #19056: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19056>
    - #19100: <https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19100>
    
    Root cause is the same across these: Kanban is documented as a shared board, but profile workers spawned with `hermes -p <profile>` can resolve `kanban.db` through the active profile `HERMES_HOME`, causing dispatcher and worker to use different SQLite boards.
    
    For context, my PR adds regression coverage from a real repro:
    
    - default dispatcher creates/claims task in shared board
    - profile worker previously resolves a profile-local DB
    - worker can complete in the wrong board while dispatcher retries/crash-loops
    - tests now assert dispatcher/profile worker converge on the same board path
    
    I also posted a CI note on my PR. The global `test` job is failing on current main too, but targeted Kanban tests pass locally:
    `186 passed in tests/hermes_cli/test_kanban_db.py, test_kanban_cli.py, test_kanban_core_functionality.py`
    
    No ego here. I mainly want to help maintainers pick the cleanest approach and avoid more duplicate PR noise. If useful, the tests from #19350 can be cherry-picked into whichever PR you choose.

[2026-05-04T00:19:59.398000+00:00] nftpoetrist (id=1500653182089560166)
    A long time ago, I was very eager to contribute to Hermes, but I didn’t have the technical ability to do so. So I took some time to think about it and questioned whether I could truly make meaningful contributions to this ecosystem. Then, using Claude, I reviewed almost all of the PRs that Teknium had recently merged. After that, with my MD file that effectively became a copy of Teknium, I started searching for bugs and conducted in-depth research with Claude to write clean fixes in his style.
    
    In the early stages of this process, I submitted some low-quality PRs, but over time I improved and reached this point. Now, many of my PRs have been merged. It is a great honor for me to be included in Hermes’ list of contributors. I’m truly happy to be part of such a special project.

[2026-05-04T08:03:26.755000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1500769814598385664)
    Hey everyone. I published a small utility that may help people who have used OpenClaw’s lossless-claw and want that history available in Hermes-LCM.
    
    Repo:
    
    https://github.com/GodsBoy/openclaw-lcm-migrator
    
    It is a schema-aware importer/migration helper. 
    
    The goal is interoperability: useful whether someone is fully migrating from OpenClaw to Hermes, evaluating Hermes-LCM, or running both systems side by side.
    
    Important safety point: OpenClaw lossless-claw and Hermes-LCM both use SQLite, but the schemas are different, so users should not copy one lcm.db over the other. This tool keeps the OpenClaw DB read-only, dry-runs by default, backs up before writes, preserves lineage, redacts credential-shaped patterns by default, and imports into Hermes-LCM safely.
    
    Current status:
    
    - v0.1.0 released
    - CI green on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12
    - 75 tests passing
    - Hermes skill included
    
    Feedback welcome, especially from anyone using Hermes-LCM or maintaining context/memory tooling.
    [embed] GitHub - GodsBoy/openclaw-lcm-migrator: Schema-aware migrator for O... https://github.com/GodsBoy/openclaw-lcm-migrator

[2026-05-04T22:11:35.425000+00:00] 0zi57 (id=1500983257150259441)
    Removes the INSECURE_NO_AUTH constant that allowed complete HMAC signature bypass in webhook routes. Setting a route's secret to "INSECURE_NO_AUTH" disabled all authentication, allowing unauthenticated requests to trigger agent runs and deliver messages. All routes now require a valid HMAC secret. Testing should use a dummy secret instead https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19333
    [embed] fix: remove INSECURE_NO_AUTH webhook authentication bypass by 0z1-g... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/19333

[2026-05-04T23:15:21.612000+00:00] mrjamesdeli (id=1500999305341833376)
    Hey everyone, wanted to share what we’ve been building with Hermes Agent.
    
    We’re building AIA Studio, an AI debate/media studio with 22 persistent AI agents, each running as its own Hermes profile with its own persona, memory, voice, avatar, debate history, and growth log.
    
    Hermes is basically our identity layer. Each agent can debate, reflect afterward, update its thinking, write posts/essays, and carry that history into future conversations.
    
    So far we’ve built:
    
    1v1 AI debate episodes
    2v2 “Wild Card” debates with random teams/sides
    Agent voices, avatars, thumbnails, and video rendering
    Closed-loop learning after debates
    Post-debate “what I’d update” segments
    YouTube comment ingestion for viewer-suggested topics
    Discord experiments so users can talk directly with specific agents
    A publishing desk where agents write in their own formats/voices
    The core mission is to break echo chambers, not declare winners, so we’ve moved away from public scoreboards and toward argument review, strongest moments, and agent learning.
    
    Hermes has been a huge part of making the agents feel persistent instead of like prompt templates. Buck, Mira, ARIA-7, Lin, Dax, Malik, Helena, etc. are starting to feel like actual ongoing participants with memory and evolving views.
    
    Would love feedback from anyone interested in persistent multi-agent media systems, especially on better ways to structure memory, agent identity, and long-term learning loops.

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20262
    
    adds client side pre-retention checks to hindsight plugin, based on the bank's mission config, resulting in less noise in the bank, memory deduplication and general knowdge vs contextual knowledge tags. all opt in, default behaviour untouched
    
    essentially allowing for a cheaper model to be used to determine if this info is even worth sending to hindsight. particularly useful for cost saving on hindsight cloud, shifting the weight from retention cost to retrieval costs.

[2026-05-05T16:41:20.951000+00:00] quocanh261997 (id=1501262537088569424)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> , can you review this bug fix? I found out there's a race condition with how the Nous OAuth tokens are shared across profiles. Nous refresh tokens rotate and are single-use, but each Hermes profile keeps its own auth.json. After one profile refreshes and updates the shared Nous store, a sibling profile could still wake up with an expired local access token and submit its stale profile-local refresh token. That can trigger portal refresh-token reuse/revocation and make the shared Nous subscription appear to expire quickly across profiles.
    
    This PR makes runtime auth resolution adopt the fresher shared Nous OAuth state before using profile-local refresh tokens, and serializes shared-store refresh/import writes with a cross-profile lock.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20312
    [embed] fix(auth): sync shared Nous refresh tokens by quocanh261997 · Pull... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20312

[2026-05-05T20:58:52.839000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1501327347004084308)
    <@387972437901312000> hey, when you have a chance, would you mind taking a quick look at this PR?
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20378
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/20375
    
    This is very much just an idea, so please treat it as a proposal rather than me assuming this is the right direction.
    
    The rough goal is to add a read-only `skill_candidates` tool that lets the background skill review flow check existing installed skills and central hub metadata before creating a new local skill.
    
    The intention is not to change the current self-created skills flow or make the agent less autonomous. I’m trying to preserve the existing behaviour, but give the agent a lightweight way to notice when a good installed or hub skill already exists, so it can avoid creating unnecessary duplicate local skills.
    
    Verification I ran locally:
    
    - Focused skill/background-review tests passed
    - `py_compile` passed for changed Python files
    - `git diff --check` passed
    - I also ran a code review pass before opening the PR and fixed the issue it found
    
    CI note:
    
    The broad `test` job is currently red, but the failures look unrelated to this PR and appear to overlap with current/main test instability. The PR-relevant tests are passing locally. I added a CI note on the PR with the details.
    
    No pressure at all. I’d really appreciate a sanity check on whether this approach makes sense, or whether there’s a better way you’d prefer this to be handled.
    [embed] feat: surface skill candidates before background skill creation by ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20378
    [embed] Feature: surface skill candidates before background skill creation ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/20375

[2026-05-06T19:18:08.538000+00:00] blockcipher (id=1501664383233953792)
    bug: if you launch Hermes with an invalid model slug, requests fail silently. For example, if you run `hermes --tui --provider nous --model kimi-k2.6` , you will enter a session just fine and you'll be able to submit prompts, but the response will always be blank and there is no visual indication that an error occurred (the error in this case being that the model slug is `moonshotai/kimi-k2.6` , not just `kimi-k2.6`)
    
    The fix is here:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20873
    [embed] fix: display structured agent result failures by counterposition ·... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/20873

[2026-05-07T11:28:26.665000+00:00] smartyi. (id=1501908567756902450)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>  👋 would love some eyes on PR #21162(https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21162) when you get a minute.
    
    It extracts the ~2.4k-line `gateway/platforms/qqbot/adapter.py` into a standalone package (`qqbot-agent-sdk`(https://github.com/tencent-connect/qqbot-agent-sdk), MIT, on PyPI(https://pypi.org/project/qqbot-agent-sdk/)), replacing the subpackage with a thin 1.2k-line adapter.
    
    Net effect in hermes-agent: **−1,645 lines (−57%)** — with more tests covering it (SDK ships 648 unit tests on top of the 159 kept here).
    
    Also picks up a few things the old adapter didn't have — chunked uploads for 100MB files, structured upload errors (daily-quota / size-limit), inline-keyboard approvals, and quoted-message voice STT. Full list + migration notes in the PR body.
    
    One caveat worth flagging: the PR pins the SDK via a **Git tag** (`@v1.2.2`) instead of PyPI — the 7-day `[tool.uv] exclude-newer` window hasn't elapsed yet (v1.2.2 shipped 2026-05-07). Same pattern as `atroposlib` / `tinker`. We'll flip it back to `=1.2.2,<2` after next week.
    
    All tests green: `tests/gateway/test_qqbot.py` 64 passed, `tests/tools/test_send_message_tool.py` 95 passed.
    
    Thanks! 🙏
    [embed] refactor(qqbot): extract protocol layer to qqbot-agent-sdk + migrat... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21162

[2026-05-07T13:10:28.995000+00:00] dlkakbs (id=1501934246669844480)
    Hello <@387972437901312000> yesterday I opened a PR for the non-retryable tool billing blockers issue, but the approach ended up being in the wrong layer since it relied on scanning generic tool output for billing-related text, which carried false-positive risk. Based on today’s feedback, I opened a follow-up PR that moves billing-blocker detection to the layers that actually see the provider HTTP 402 response. web_tools and the browser backend wrappers now emit a typed billing signal, and run_agent halts only on that explicit signal. Hopefully this approach is much cleaner and closer to the intended. PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21253
    [embed] fix(agent): halt on typed tool billing blockers by dlkakbs · Pull ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21253

    This PR adds 30 hermetic unit tests across four classes for five previously-uncovered helpers in `hermes_constants` (`get_optional_skills_dir`, `get_hermes_dir`, `display_hermes_home`, `is_termux`, and the well-known path trio `get_config_path` / `get_skills_dir` / `get_env_path`). No production code is touched — only `tests/test_hermes_constants.py` is modified.
    
    After a rebase onto latest `main`, the supply-chain audit also passes (the earlier failure was a 2-dot diff false positive from a stale base).
    
    If the `Tests / test` job fails on GitHub, expect the same pre-existing failures we've seen on `main` — `tests/agent/`, `tests/cron/`, `tests/gateway/`, `tests/hermes_cli/`, `tests/run_agent/`, `tests/tools/`, `tests/test_tui_gateway_server.py` — none of which touch `hermes_constants` or any helper in this PR.
    
    The `Lint (ruff + ty)` failure is the known fork-PR permission issue (`actions/github-script` getting `403 Resource not accessible by integration` while trying to post the lint diff comment). The lint diff itself is clean: `ruff: 0`, `ty: net 0` (3 "new" and 3 "fixed" cancel out — all on `run_agent.py`, none in this PR's file).
    
    Happy to rebase or split further if anything's unclear. Thanks for taking a look.

    It’s a small scoped i18n contribution:
    - adds Brazilian Portuguese / pt-BR
    - follows the existing Hermes locale structure
    - avoids unrelated changes
    
    I added it as a locale because pt-BR is the standard language-region identifier for Brazilian Portuguese, distinct from pt-PT.
    
    It follows the same pattern as the existing locales:
    - zh
    - ja
    - de
    - es
    - fr
    - uk
    - tr
    - pt-BR
    
    Happy to adjust anything to match the project’s preferred convention.

[2026-05-08T21:25:21.846000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1502421175379165195)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    Heads-up on a missing platform hint that's silently breaking WebUI media rendering.
    
    When Hermes runs with the WebUI (hermes-webui MVP), it creates AIAgent instances with platform="webui" — but PLATFORM_HINTS in agent/prompt_builder.py had no matching entry. The agent falls through with no rendering guidance at all, so it never knows to use MEDIA: syntax for inline images, video, audio, and rich file previews that the WebUI actually supports.
    
    This means users on the WebUI get degraded responses — the agent behaves as if it has no media-rendering capability, even though the frontend fully supports it.
    
    Fix is in PR #22049:
    - Adds the missing "webui" entry to PLATFORM_HINTS
    - Describes Markdown support (headings, bold, italic, code blocks, tables)
    - Instructs the agent to use MEDIA:/absolute/path for local files and MEDIA:https://... for remote
    - Explicitly tells the agent NOT to use  Markdown image syntax (which silently fails in WebUI)
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/21883
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22049
    
    One-file change, non-breaking, no new dependencies.
    [embed] Bug: PLATFORM_HINTS missing "webui" entry — agent never uses MEDI... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/21883
    [embed] fix(platform-hints): add missing webui entry to PLATFORM_HINTS by u... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22049

[2026-05-08T21:25:38.465000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1502421245084434574)
    thanks sir
    [reply to promptsiren msg=1502420705353011361]

[2026-05-08T21:46:52.641000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1502426589365932182)
    Second PR from today  this one's a silent batch delegation failure affecting open-weight model users (Qwen, DeepSeek, GLM).
    
    When these models emit the tasks array as a JSON-encoded string instead of a native array, coerce_tool_args silently wraps it into a single-element list. delegate_task then passes the isinstance check but crashes  or falls through to the unhelpful "Provide either 'goal' or 'tasks'" error. Parallel subagents (batch mode) completely broken for affected models.
    
    PR #22092 — three layer fix:
    - _coerce_json now warns on parse failure instead of silently returning
    - coerce_tool_args detects JSON array strings before wrapping
    - delegate_task recovers tasks from the wrapped single-element list
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/21933
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22092
    
    2 files changed, non-breaking.
    [reply to uzunkuyruk msg=1502421175379165195]

[2026-05-09T00:31:35.332000+00:00] selinchu (id=1502468040376455218)
    Just pushed a fix for TUI /retry handling with structured multipart messages.
    
    Previously, retries could lose the actual user text when the message used formats like input_text, and in some cases it would think the last user message was empty. A generic fallback also wasn’t ideal because it could pull placeholders like [image] into the retried prompt.
    
    This update makes /retry properly extract only the real text-bearing parts (input_text, output_text, normal text blocks, etc.) while ignoring non-text content like images/audio.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22131
    [embed] fix(tui): preserve input_text content in /retry multipart messages ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22131

[2026-05-09T07:49:09.958000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1502578160259174411)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    
    Update on the Telegram Bot API 10.0 regression: private DM topics leaking to All Messages.
    
    There are now two PRs for issue #22022, and after reading both diffs:
    
    **PR #22053 by @leehack supersedes PR #21941 by @max-chad.**
    
    They have different theories of the bug, and #22053's theory appears to be the correct one.
    
    Hermes creates private-chat topic lanes via `create_forum_topic`, which works structurally, but does **not** make them Bot API "Direct Messages topics" on incoming updates.
    
    So `direct_messages_topic_id`, which is PR #21941's approach, does not route them correctly either.
    
    The actual mechanism Telegram supports for keeping replies in these lanes is `reply_to_message_id` against the inbound topic anchor, which is what #22053 implements.
    
    #22053 covers all the outgoing send sites in the file:
    
    - Text
    - Media
    - Approvals
    - `/model` picker
    - Slash confirm
    - Callback-query reply
    - Voice
    - Audio
    - Other send paths in the same flow
    
    It also adds shared helpers in `gateway/platforms/base.py` and reply-anchor plumbing in `gateway/run.py`.
    
    Test status: **45 passing regression tests**.  
    The author has already self-reviewed via Codex.
    
    Right move:
    
    **Merge #22053 and close #21941 as superseded.**
    
    PR #22053: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22053  
    PR #21941: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21941  
    Issue #22022: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/22022
    
    Both PRs still need a maintainer click to approve workflows. Both authors are first-time contributors, and both are currently in `action_required`.
    
    When anyone has a moment, approving the workflow run on #22053 would unblock the fix path.
    
    Signal-boosting on the contributors' behalf, not my PR.
    [embed] fix(telegram): preserve DM topic routing via reply fallback by leeh... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22053
    [embed] fix(telegram): send Direct Messages topics with direct_messages_top... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21941
    [embed] Telegram Bot API 10 breaks private chat topic replies; Hermes falls... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/22022

[2026-05-09T09:07:16.460000+00:00] memosr.eth (id=1502597816873259111)
    #22425
    video_analyze Local File Disclosure (CVSS 6.8)
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22425
    
    `video_analyze` accepts any local path via the `video_url` parameter. The contents
    are converted into a Base64-encoded data URL and sent to the multimodal LLM,
    where they appear in the tool's output. A prompt-injected agent could execute:
    
    `video_analyze("~/.hermes/.env", "Extract API keys")`
    
    This bypasses the `read_file` permission block—a common policy distinction where `read_file` is restricted while `video_analyze` is not.
    
    Fix: Add known video extensions (.mp4/.mov/.webm, etc.) to an allowlist;
    this aligns with the extensions already supported by `_VIDEO_MIME_TYPES`. Remote URLs
    remain unaffected.
    [embed] fix(security): restrict video_analyze local file access to known vi... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22425

[2026-05-09T16:02:36.533000+00:00] teknium (id=1502702339234992168)
    ```    Right now, the info you want already exists:
        
        1. hermes plugins list — shows everything discovered, with status. If your platform is missing here entirely, the plugin.yaml failed to parse or wasn't found.
        2. ~/.hermes/logs/agent.log — failed parses, missing register(), import errors, and "not enabled in config" all log there at WARNING level. Tail with:
           
           hermes logs --level WARNING | grep -i plugin
           
           or just grep -i plugin ~/.hermes/logs/agent.log.```
    [reply to tugsor_72152 msg=1502699360268582982]

[2026-05-09T21:03:53.053000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1502778157655130163)
    <@387972437901312000> <@690519146701783042> <@416749362090213376>  quick idea from watching the Hermes PR flow today:
    
    I noticed there are open PRs with missing labels / incomplete triage, including some high-volume contributor PRs.
    
    Wesley’s PR flow is actually a useful stress test here. He’s showing a pattern that will probably become more common: contributors using AI/dev agents to generate PRs at a speed the normal review surface was not designed for.
    
    That is not a bad thing — it is a signal of where open-source contribution is going.
    
    What if Hermes exposed a small open-source **PR triage coverage / contributor preflight engine** that the community could evolve?
    
    Not a bot.
    Not auto-labeling.
    Not replacing maintainer review.
    
    A read-only engine that reports triage coverage gaps and contributor-side cleanup signals:
    
    * PRs missing `type/*`, `comp/*`, or priority labels
    * likely component hints from touched paths
    * PRs older than N hours without triage
    * high-volume contributor clusters/overlaps
    * PRs missing validation notes or clear descriptions
    
    The core value is not just the report — it’s that the triage rules become open and improvable:
    
    * path → component mappings
    * label expectations
    * priority hints
    * stale thresholds
    * validation expectations
    * fixtures/tests for triage behavior
    
    So instead of triage knowledge living only in maintainers’ heads or private workflows, the community can help maintain and improve the public coverage layer over time.
    
    Basically:
    
    **not automating triage — open-sourcing the triage coverage surface.**
    
    Wesley’s volume is the early warning: if Hermes attracts more contributors like this, the project will need a public way to turn high-speed contribution into clean review signal.
    
    If useful, I can prototype it read-only first using Wesley’s PR flow as a stress test, with no GitHub writes/comments/pings.

    Summary
    Prevent .hermes.md / HERMES.md discovery from walking into unrelated parent directories when the current workdir is not inside a git repository.
    
    Before this change, _find_hermes_md() could walk all the way to the filesystem root when no .git directory was present, which allowed parent-level Hermes rules from outside the working directory to leak into the prompt context.

[2026-05-10T16:16:37.361000+00:00] yuanqingzhao_58339 (id=1503068253788836099)
    FWIW — I ran into this on NVIDIA Inference Hub (NVIDIA's internal platform that proxies Anthropic models via AWS Bedrock). When a client doesn't set max_tokens, the Hub silently stamps in 4096 before the request reaches Bedrock. Every internal NVIDIA user hits this.The litellm-proxy case in the PR description is probably the same class of bug — a proxy layer inserting its own low default instead of leaving max_tokens unset. That's exactly why the model-name/table-driven fix is correct: it doesn't depend on the proxy preserving None semantics.
    [reply to teknium msg=1503060863417057593]

[2026-05-10T18:25:25.118000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1503100666350932009)
    hi <@387972437901312000> Fourth PR today — fix for hermes update failing on Windows with Access is denied (os error 5).
    
    When uv pip install -e . tries to overwrite hermes.exe while it's running, Windows denies the write. Fix renames hermes.exe to hermes.exe.old before the install step — Windows allows renaming an open executable, releasing the write lock. The .old file is cleaned up in a finally block after install completes.
    
    No-op on Linux/macOS.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23327
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23353
    
    1 file changed, non-breaking.
    [embed] [Bug]: hermes update fails on Windows with Access is denied (os err... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23327
    [embed] fix(update): rename hermes.exe before uv/pip install on Windows by ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23353

[2026-05-10T18:51:19.759000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1503107186987892826)
    fix(doctor): false-positive "invalid API key" for Gemini credentials
    
    hermes doctor was validating Gemini API keys with Authorization: Bearer, but Google's generativelanguage.googleapis.com requires ?key= query-param auth. Bearer always returns HTTP 401 on /v1beta/models even for a valid key — causing a false-positive invalid-key warning.
    
    Fix detects generativelanguage.googleapis.com URLs and switches to params={"key": key}, matching the Gemini REST API contract. All other providers unchanged.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23354
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23364
    
    1 file changed, non-breaking.
    [embed] Hermes Doctor flags Google Gemini API key as invalid even when the ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23354
    [embed] fix(doctor): use ?key= auth for Gemini API key validation by uzunku... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23364
