[2026-05-10T19:43:28.251000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1503120308834668725)
    fix(oneshot): kanban worker agents now inherit fallback_providers from profile config
    
    Workers spawned via `hermes -p <profile> chat -q ...` were constructed without a fallback chain — profile-level fallback_providers was silently ignored, so workers had no failover when their primary provider hit rate limits or timeouts.
    
    Fix reads fallback_providers / fallback_model from the profile config and passes it to AIAgent, mirroring the same logic already used in interactive CLI sessions.
    
    Also suppressed a pre-existing Windows footgun false-positive in tools/process_registry.py (os.killpg inside an explicit `if not _IS_WINDOWS:` guard).
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23362
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23368
    
    2 files changed, non-breaking.
    [embed] Profile config fallback_providers not inherited by kanban worker ag... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/23362
    [embed] fix(oneshot): pass fallback_providers from profile config to AIAgen... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23368

    * added a tiny helper for numeric env parsing
    * no behavior change for valid configs
    * added regression test coverage for malformed env values

[2026-05-11T02:54:59.438000+00:00] quocanh261997 (id=1503228904343863488)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> , I see a pretty weird situation in Kanban mode in which if my `AgentA` create and assign a Kanban ticket to `AgentB` then when `B` finishes, somehow `AgentC` reports back the task status. Digging more into the code, I found that every agent profile has their Kanban notifier watcher on all the time, and status notification is just randomly picked from which watcher receives it first even though that profile doesn't play any role in the ticket itself. So I create this PR https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23521 to solve that, which deterministically allow only the profile that create the Kanban ticket to be able to notify back to users when the task is done. 
    
    Please take a look whenever u have the time. Thanks a bunch 🙏
    [embed] fix(gateway): route kanban notifications to creator profile by quoc... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23521

[2026-05-11T05:00:38.413000+00:00] wesleysimplicio (id=1503260525096599612)
    <@387972437901312000>  I’m opening the first PR for a cache-aware context execution roadmap in Hermes.
    
    V1 introduces a Stable Context Prefix Builder.
    
    The idea is simple: before implementing real cache, Hermes needs a deterministic context layout.
    
    Stable context should always come first:
    
    - system prompt
    - agent instructions
    - skills
    - project summary
    - repository context
    - long-lived memory
    
    Dynamic context should always come last:
    
    - current task
    - recent tool results
    - file diffs
    - temporary execution state
    
    This creates a stable prompt prefix that future local runtimes, prompt caches, or KV-cache-aware engines can potentially reuse.
    
    This PR does not implement real cache yet. It only introduces the structural foundation needed for future versions:
    
    V1: Stable Context Prefix Builder  
    V2: Context Fingerprinting & Cache Metadata  
    V3: Provider Cache Capability Hooks  
    V4: Real Local Prefix Cache  
    V5: Smart Delta Context  
    V6: Cache-Aware Runtime Routing
    
    The goal is to make Hermes more deterministic, local-runtime-friendly, and ready for future cache-aware execution.
    
    Inspiration: ds4 of Antirez(redis)
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23479
    [embed] feat(runtime): add stable context prefix builder by wesleysimplicio... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23479

    One use case for this is turning Hermes Agent sessions into Spotify episodes
    
    Instead of just a basic summary, the agent could reflect on:

    - where it got stuck
    - how problems were resolved
    - notable outputs or decisions
    - what changed between iterations

    A natural fit for persistent agents and long-running workflows

[2026-05-11T17:00:37.347000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1503441714558406767)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, I opened a PR that adds a host-managed async compression capability for context engines, and would appreciate feedback on the direction:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23892
    
    The PR adds infrastructure for progressive / lower-disruption context compression. Instead of only compressing explicitly or near the context limit, a context engine can opt in to prepare a compression candidate in the background after a turn. The host still owns snapshotting, digest validation, and the actual transcript rewrite.
    
    This does not change the default compressor behavior. The idea is to let lower-threshold background preparation coexist with the existing hard-threshold compression path, while keeping compression policy separate from the host-owned safe apply path.
    
    Would appreciate your thoughts on whether this direction makes sense for this project.
    [embed] feat(agent): add host-managed async compression for context engines... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23892

[2026-05-11T17:19:40.942000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1503446511143620618)
    Progressive context compression seems like a promising direction that several people are exploring, and my thought is to keep the first experiments at the plugin/context-engine layer while the host provides the safe validation/apply path.

[2026-05-11T18:04:10.613000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1503457708555239494)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>1, no rush, whenever you have a sec 👋
    
    PR up for review: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23914
    
    **`fix(tool_result_storage): spill to local fs when no sandbox env`**
    
    Closes the proximate failure mode from #23767.
    
    Today when `env is None`, for example bare CLI or local custom providers, oversized tool results fall through to inline truncation. The preview keeps inflating the prompt every turn, and on lower-context local models that drives the compression-grows-size loop the reporter hit on his 65K-context Qwen setup.
    
    Fix mirrors the spill-to-disk pattern from your #20468, but for tool results.
    
    Helper is self-contained so this PR does not block on #20468 landing first. Once both are in, the two helpers can be unified in a follow-up.
    
    After one round of review I hardened the write a bit:
    
    - Atomic write via tempfile + `os.replace`
    - Refuses a symlinked spill root
    - Uses mode `0o600`
    - Uses `surrogateescape` for byte round-trip
    
    Tests: **59 passed locally**
    
    CI is mostly green.
    
    `Windows footguns` and `e2e` are failing on `origin/main` too, so I am treating those as out of scope.
    
    Known remaining footgun:
    
    - `Path.write_text` timeout for NFS/FUSE stalls is pre-existing and applies to the sandbox path equally
    
    Happy to adjust shape if you want it landed differently 🙏
    [embed] fix(tool_result_storage): spill to local fs when no sandbox env (#2... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/23914

[2026-05-11T19:53:15.267000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503485158823563324)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    this is what `/exhaust` needs
    ```
    current observation → current latent
    goal observation → goal latent
    try candidate action sequences
    roll them forward in latent space
    pick the sequence whose final latent is closest to the goal latent
    execute the first action(s)
    re-observe and replan
    ```

[2026-05-11T19:53:43.215000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503485276045967521)
    ```
    The paper uses Cross-Entropy Method sampling to optimize action sequences, and only executes part of the plan before replanning, because long rollouts accumulate error.
    ```

[2026-05-11T19:55:17.529000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503485671627554957)
    if you want me to implement it I would love

[2026-05-11T19:58:33.896000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503486495250448616)
    /exhaust is JEPA-like persistence for agents: instead of hallucinating the next move, the agent maintains a compressed model of the task state, predicts action outcomes, and keeps searching until reality converges with the goal.

[2026-05-11T19:59:00.189000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503486605531545661)
    /exhaust turns failure into a planning loop: observe, compress, simulate, act, verify, repeat.

[2026-05-11T20:00:09.759000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503486897329143809)
    long run, sit there until it figures it out hahahaha. like agent on-promise

[2026-05-11T20:01:21.453000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503487198035579088)
    gonna lab a bit around this concept

[2026-05-11T20:18:12.299000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503491437830869052)
    ```
    Thinking the first /exhaust slice should be symbolic/text-latent, not visual JEPA:
    
    observe → compress state → sample candidate actions → predict outcome → choose lowest-cost next step → execute → re-observe/replan.
    
    MPC/CEM-inspired persistence for agent tool-use, with traces first so it’s inspectable before it gets “smart.”
    ```

[2026-05-11T20:20:28.409000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1503492008717713529)
    The paper is useful less because Hermes needs a visual JEPA,
    and more because it gives a planning pattern for /exhaust:
    
    compressed state → candidate rollout → goal distance → partial execution → re-observe/replan.
    
    For Hermes, the latent can start as symbolic/text state rather than learned visual embeddings.

[2026-05-11T21:12:13.561000+00:00] 0zi57 (id=1503505032669167637)
    Found and fixed in Hermes v0.13.0 on Windows
    **Problem:** `skill_view()` in `tools/skills_tool.py` uses hardcoded `/` separators for file categorization (references/, templates/, assets/, scripts/). On Windows, `Path.relative_to()` returns `\`-separated paths, so all checks fail and files incorrectly fall into "other".
    
    **Fix:** Replaced `str.startswith("dir/")` with `Path.parts[0] == "dir"`. Platform-agnostic, zero behavior change on POSIX, fixes Windows-only bug
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24016 <@387972437901312000>
    [embed] fix(skills_tool): Use Path.parts for platform-agnostic file categor... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24016

[2026-05-12T03:37:17.807000+00:00] quocanh261997 (id=1503601938900586508)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> , I've pushed out a PR for the following issue:
    Root cause: after `hermes update` gracefully drained a systemd-managed gateway, the restart path could wait behind systemd RestartSec or get stuck on one slow profile, delaying other profile gateways from picking up the new code.
    
    Fix: update now requests systemctl restart --no-block after drain and restarts multiple gateway profiles through a bounded worker pool (agent.update_gateway_restart_concurrency, default 4). One slow/stuck gateway no longer blocks the rest, while still avoiding a restart stampede on small machines. Tests and docs were added. 
    
    Much appreciate for the review. Thanks 🙏 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22469
    [embed] fix(gateway): update restart after systemd drain by quocanh261997 ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/22469

[2026-05-12T07:04:16.674000+00:00] kevins0792 (id=1503654027404120178)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, adding a bit more context on the motivation behind this PR.
    
    Dynamic context compression is becoming an important direction for agent runtimes, especially terminal/coding agents. [TACO](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19572>) focuses directly on observational context compression for terminal agents, and [ACON](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.00615>) looks at a broader version of the same problem for long-horizon agents: keeping observations and interaction history compact without losing useful task context.
    
    For Hermes, progressive compression would be hard to make pleasant if it only uses the current synchronous flow: lower thresholds could mean more frequent visible pauses unless candidate preparation can happen off the main path.
    
    This PR does not add a specific compression policy. It adds a host-managed capability for context engines to prepare compression candidates in the background, while the host owns snapshotting, digest validation, and the final transcript rewrite at a safe point.
    That allows adaptive / progressive compression to be explored at the plugin layer first, without changing default compressor behavior or hardcoding one strategy into core. This seems especially relevant for local LLMs and long-running terminal sessions.
    
    Would be interested to hear whether this capability-level approach fits Hermes’ context management direction. Thanks.
    [reply to kevins0792 msg=1503441714558406767]

[2026-05-12T09:33:42.991000+00:00] scottermonkey (id=1503691634863247431)
    Anyone interested in helping push this along? 
    
    Hermes currently ships a large set of bundled skills directly into each profile's skills directory via bundled-skill sync. That makes updates heavier, expands the default install footprint, and creates an ongoing maintenance burden for content that many users will never use.
    
    A concrete example is the Apple/macOS skill set (apple-notes, apple-reminders, findmy, imessage). These are valid skills, but on non-macOS systems they still exist as bundled content and can resurface through sync/update behavior even though they are platform-specific and irrelevant for most installs.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/19986

[2026-05-12T19:30:17.393000+00:00] muskibro (id=1503841767466991717)
    Opened a PR for the kanban dispatcher bug <@387972437901312000> 
    Problem: shutil.which("hermes") was returning None in certain environments — even though the binary was installed at /usr/local/bin/hermes and PATH was correctly set. This caused the kanban dispatcher to deadlock with 0 workers spawned.
    Fix: Added a hardcoded fallback path in _resolve_hermes_bin(). If shutil.which() fails, it now checks whether /usr/local/bin/hermes exists and is executable before falling back to sys.executable.   
    PR:https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24517
    [embed] fix: add hardcoded fallback path in _resolve_hermes_bin for symlink... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24517

[2026-05-12T19:57:22.553000+00:00] kkurottal6611 (id=1503848583881818255)
    #24516 — fix(tts): report configured TTS backends accurately in setup and status surfaces
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24516
    
    This fixes inaccurate TTS backend reporting in setup/status output. Some valid configs like tts.provider: xai could be shown as unavailable or mislabeled. The PR aligns detection with the TTS providers Hermes already supports and adds regression tests for xAI and local-engine-backed providers.
    
    #23502 — feat(curator): add report subcommand for latest run artifact visibility

[2026-05-12T23:28:09.263000+00:00] ahmetarif0x (id=1503901628028289086)
    #24600 Windows Lock Bug
    
    Hermes' scoped-lock system reads /proc/<pid>/cmdline to identify the process. On Windows this file doesn't exist, so the read fails and the lock gets marked as "stale" and deleted, even though the process is still running.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24600
    [embed] fix(gateway): consult lock record argv when cmdline is unreadable i... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24600

[2026-05-13T00:16:32.882000+00:00] _.exo._ (id=1503913806688948364)
    for the shape:
    Not OpenAI-only. custom_providers already accepts api_mode: chat_completions, anthropic_messages, codex and bedrock shapes. The schema validates all four, and the primary path at hermes_cli/main.py reads whichever the user declares. The cascade in _try_activate_fallback catches some of these when the URL or provider name happens to match a pattern. What it misses is the user who already declared api_mode explicitly on a URL the heuristic doesn't recognise. That declaration is honoured on the primary path and dropped on the fallback path. 
    
    and for the auth:
    
    the credential_pool in auth.json already stores auth_type: oauth entries. That's how the nous built-in works with device_code, how anthropic works with dashboard PKCE, and how openai-codex works with dashboard device_code. Each carries access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, and status tracking. Before this PR, auxiliary calls on a custom:<name> provider only checked config.yaml.api_key and key_env. If both were empty it fell through to "no-key-required" even when a valid pool entry existed for that provider. The fix is a pool lookup that matches what built-in providers already do. Any OAuth-sourced custom provider in the pool becomes usable for auxiliary calls, same as API-key ones.
    [reply to teknium msg=1503911384826773576]

[2026-05-13T00:17:34.773000+00:00] _.exo._ (id=1503914066278613065)
    i have one big auth.json that all my profiles point to so i noticed auxullary calls were failing with 401 using the same providers that were working for primary models

[2026-05-13T00:19:33.115000+00:00] sylwester0_42058 (id=1503914562641072128)
    pr #24633 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24633 
    
    It fixes a false duplicate suppression issue in the idempotency cache. Previously, different webhook routes could accidentally dedupe each other if they reused the same X-Request-ID, and headerless requests could collide because the fallback ID used a millisecond timestamp.
    
    This PR scopes explicit dedupe keys by route and disables dedupe for generated fallback IDs, while preserving duplicate detection for real retries on the same route.
    [embed] fix(webhook): scope idempotency keys by route and disable fallback ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24633

[2026-05-13T06:27:59.933000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504007285356367933)

[2026-05-13T06:35:11.901000+00:00] jez_wikitribune (id=1504009097161478155)
    > two deterministic, engine-managed mechanisms: 
    > - **recursive context compression,** in which a hierarchical summary DAG automatically compacts older messages while retaining lossless pointers to every original; and 
    > - **recursive task partitioning,** in which engine-managed parallel primitives like `LLM-Map` replace model-written loops
    from <https://papers.voltropy.com/LCM>
    
    neato!

[2026-05-13T07:08:53.914000+00:00] tiz.io (id=1504017578098557008)
    <@1486446814029807787> I've been toying with a context compaction tool of my own design I call Accordion, but frankly I feel like it's well trod territory:
    It taps into the real-time output stream, vectorizing everything in the conversation into an accordion-compressed session index. On each turn, it free-associates against both the session context and stateful memory, runs the hits through a reranker to filter noise, and surfaces the most relevant connections as 'recollections' before the next thought. When the session ends, a janitor process scans for memorable chunks and promotes them to stateful memory.

[2026-05-13T07:19:08.206000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504020154626084965)
    Funny you say accordion I call my latest codex patch accordion 😂. Our cortex system does that, in real testing we found it accidentally prompt injections short prompts

[2026-05-13T07:20:07.343000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504020402664505364)
    We stopped using it as prompt level and just use lcm grep enrichment with levels of leaf summaries and daily/weekly/monthly rollups so the agent always keeps a small index in context of where it is “in time”

[2026-05-13T07:20:36.674000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504020525687635978)
    So an agent can go as small as 2k tokens on something all the way to the source with embeddings and rerankers built into tool

[2026-05-13T07:21:39.147000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504020787718525019)
    Goal being “agent has perfect memory and can converse as a person quickly on any memory in history without rewriting/ie can context switch with a chunk size basis wise to match its context window” call it active context management

[2026-05-13T07:43:36.783000+00:00] tiz.io (id=1504026314284335184)
    That's spot on. Is this OSS? I don't want to reinvent the wheel. Even if it isn't, I'd love to put it to use.

[2026-05-13T07:46:28.093000+00:00] eva_electricsheep (id=1504027032810422283)

    Hey <@387972437901312000>, could you review this bug fix when you get a chance?
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/24927
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24929
    
    It fixes a file mutation verifier false positive where successful write_file or patch results could be treated as failed if nested lint or LSP diagnostics contained error text. The PR centralises landed mutation classification so verifier footers, CLI tool status, and tool guardrails agree on the same success contract.

    PR #24987
    
    d8c4460 (merged today) added WhatsApp to the cron home-channel
    registry but missed Yuanbao — which is already in
    _KNOWN_DELIVERY_PLATFORMS and has YUANBAO_HOME_CHANNEL wired
    across gateway/config.py and yuanbao.py.

    returned "" and cron jobs silently failed to deliver.

[2026-05-13T18:49:16.501000+00:00] muskibro (id=1504193833603174611)
    Opened a PR for the kanban dispatcher bug <@387972437901312000> 
    Problem: shutil.which("hermes") was returning None in certain environments — even though the binary was installed at /usr/local/bin/hermes and PATH was correctly set. This caused the kanban dispatcher to deadlock with 0 workers spawned.
    Fix: Added a hardcoded fallback path in _resolve_hermes_bin(). If shutil.which() fails, it now checks whether /usr/local/bin/hermes exists and is executable before falling back to sys.executable.   
    PR:https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24517
    [embed] fix: add hardcoded fallback path in _resolve_hermes_bin for symlink... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/24517

[2026-05-13T21:27:10.415000+00:00] markelphoenix (id=1504233570078560317)
    I am trying a local Qwen3.6 brain for Hermes for overnight coding. Any tips? I feel like it gets lost eventually for longer run tasks or asks /goals. Any plugins or skills to help it along?
    [reply to waefrebeorn msg=1504214023086932040]

[2026-05-13T21:34:57.228000+00:00] waefrebeorn (id=1504235528034193468)
    use llama.cpp, compile for your machine, target 196k and report it as 185k to hermes in yaml
    or target 256k reported as 248k
    
    set compression window to .85 in yaml
    
    set -t to be machine thread amount and -tb one below machine thread amount
    
    set checkpoint every 32567 instead of 8192
    
    64 checkpoints instead of 32
    
    use a iq2 unsloth quant at q4_0 ctk and ctv. 
    
    —jinja needed. use preserve thinking false template
    
    temp 0.6 minp .95 and —mmap 
    
    using -ngl99 is a meme if you have 8gb vram only use on high vram scenarios 
    
    i use a 5050 laptop 8gb and 96gb system ram in BAR which translates to 8+48gb vram with the 8gb being the 3b fast loading 
    
    targeted 35b a4b outperforms 27b dense via just letting it run longer in clock time when compared to token usage in this manner
    [reply to markelphoenix msg=1504233570078560317]

[2026-05-13T21:35:21.244000+00:00] waefrebeorn (id=1504235628764598362)

[2026-05-13T21:36:23.767000+00:00] waefrebeorn (id=1504235891005194320)
    i have added mind palace skill to the hermes pull request 
    
    this creates a human readable auto goal structure that you can use for any project 
    
    i cleaned it up from my presentation last night and basic prompt from before 
    
    “hermes, i would like you to create a markdown mind palace in a .hermes directory in this project, i would like a index system of all project files and devils advocate details, the mind palace is a prestige prompt structure system that uses multi stage directions based on project needs, the mind palace needs a 4 phase devils advocate loop structure as well as a slate markdown
    
    to consider and control this as a goal, create it as a looping 1000 step devils advocate review process and then roadmap the extension phase plane
    
    then use this to create the mind palace skill alias mp”

[2026-05-14T07:55:43.066000+00:00] k_sze (id=1504391748401631294)
    Could somebody explain why we want/need this at all?
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/0f0e20ef81709a6dd590b25af380b116db67628c/tools/environments/docker.py#L392-L395
    ```python3
                writable_args.extend([
                    "--tmpfs", "/home:rw,exec,size=1g",
                    "--tmpfs", "/root:rw,exec,size=1g",
                ])
    ```

[2026-05-14T08:06:26.175000+00:00] undifined. (id=1504394445796282378)
    Why docker? Or what is the question
    [reply to k_sze msg=1504391748401631294]

[2026-05-14T08:11:08.675000+00:00] k_sze (id=1504395630687293482)
    Those lines cause container creation to fail when the image happens to have a large `/root` folder (e.g. lots of preinstalled command line tools under `/root/.local/bin`, `/root/go`, etc). When Podman tries to create a container, it sees that the `/root` folder is now a tmpfs, so tries to copy everything from the original image's `/root` into that tmpfs `/root`, which would fail if there's more than 1g of files.
    [reply to undifined. msg=1504394445796282378]

[2026-05-14T08:14:25.263000+00:00] undifined. (id=1504396455237124116)
    I have never used /root to store anything, but a few scripts and temporary files,  always working with users

[2026-05-14T08:24:56.318000+00:00] k_sze (id=1504399102073507860)
    Unfortunately, if I preinstall the Rust toolchain in the image using the official rustup script, there is no option to specify an alternative install location, it will go into `/root/.cargo` and `/root/.rustup`, and that alone is already over 1g.
    [reply to undifined. msg=1504396455237124116]

[2026-05-14T08:26:57.522000+00:00] undifined. (id=1504399610440061109)
    *   ~/.rustup: 1.4G
    *   ~/.cargo: 33M

[2026-05-14T08:30:59.204000+00:00] ahmetarif0x (id=1504400624127578112)
    PR #25554 — fix(agent): forward custom_providers to compressor on fallback activation
    
    PR #25494 (merged earlier today) fixed _check_compression_model_feasibility not forwarding custom_providers to get_model_context_length — but _try_activate_fallback() had the same gap and was left behind. When a primary provider fails over to the fallback model, the compressor was silently falling through to the 256K default instead of honoring the per-model context_length set in custom_providers. One-line behavioral fix + regression test.
    <@387972437901312000>

[2026-05-14T16:00:39.146000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1504513786206359714)
    Hi maintainers and <@387972437901312000>, opened a PR for the approval-gate hardening you invited back from GHSA-6cjf-cff6-j9mg, the RG-4 "approval regex class" follow-up.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/25793  
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25795
    
    It extends the approval gate's pre-match normalization to de-obfuscate shell idioms:
    
    - `${IFS}`
    - ANSI-C `$'...'` quoting
    - Empty-quote splits
    - `eval` / `command` / `builtin` wrappers
    
    It also widens/adds `DANGEROUS_PATTERNS` entries for:
    
    - Octal `chmod`
    - `dash` / `ash`
    - Base64-pipe-to-shell
    - World-writable script paths
    
    Includes per-class regression tests.
    
    This is pure heuristic hardening under `SECURITY.md` section 3.2, with no posture change.
    
    CI status:
    
    All CI checks are green except the `test` job, which is red on `main` itself right now due to pre-existing failures in unrelated modules, including Bedrock/TTS missing deps, DingTalk, Feishu, etc.
    
    Approval-specific status:
    
    - Zero failures in `test_approval.py`
    - `22904` passed
    
    I left a comment on the PR with the full breakdown.
    
    Would appreciate a review when someone has a chance. Thanks!
    [embed] approval gate misses shell-idiom-obfuscated dangerous commands (RG-... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/25793
    [embed] fix(approval): de-obfuscate shell idioms in dangerous-command detec... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25795

[2026-05-14T17:21:58.445000+00:00] nftpoetrist (id=1504534251469537371)
    PR #25856 — Telegram slash-confirm prompt silently disappearing
    
    The "Approve / Cancel" button prompt for commands like /reload-mcp was silently failing whenever the command contained _, ., [ or similar characters. Telegram throws BadRequest: Can't parse entities, the error gets swallowed — user sees nothing.
    Root cause: every other dynamic send in the adapter was upgraded to format_message() + MarkdownV2 today, this one was missed. 2-line fix.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25856
    [embed] fix(telegram): escape send_slash_confirm preview with format_messag... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25856

[2026-05-14T19:06:52.939000+00:00] _voscko_ (id=1504560652491030781)
    Hi all! Could we get #21012 merged or cherry-picked first?
    
    Short version: this is a CI unblocker, not a risky runtime change. It only touches tests, and it makes the shared PR checks match Hermes’ actual lazy optional-dependency model.
    
    Right now several PRs inherit red checks because parts of the test suite assume integrations like Bedrock, DingTalk, Feishu, Matrix, faster-whisper, KittenTTS, etc. are installed or eagerly loaded. Hermes intentionally keeps those optional, so CI should test that contract instead of failing because the environment is lean.
    
    What Hermes gets from this: useful CI signal again. PRs stop being blocked by optional-dep noise, and real regressions become easier to spot. I checked the PR: all checks are green, merge state is clean, and the commit cherry-picks cleanly onto current main.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21012
    
    <@387972437901312000>

[2026-05-14T21:44:21.380000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1504600282124779660)
    hey <@387972437901312000>   Can you check it when you have time?
    
    5 PR opened today across security and cron reliability:
    fix(security): IPv6 scope ID bypass in URL safety — both is_always_blocked_url() and is_safe_url() silently skipped IPv6 addresses with scope IDs (fe80::1%eth0), allowing potential SSRF bypass against link-local/metadata endpoints. Stripped scope ID before parsing, fail-closed on unparseable.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25961
    
    fix(cron): secrets redaction failure leaks stdout/stderr — when redact_sensitive_text() raises in _run_job_script(), stdout/stderr were left unredacted and could leak API keys/tokens into cron delivery. Replaced bare except Exception: pass with warning log + [REDACTED] fallback.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25904
    
    fix(cron): silent channel name resolution failure causes wrong delivery target — resolve_channel_name() failure was silently swallowed, causing cron jobs to use raw chat_id with no indication. Added warning log with platform_key, chat_id, and exception.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25947
    
    fix(model_tools): MCP async cleanup failure masks thread leak — asyncio.gather() failure during worker thread cleanup was silently swallowed, masking potential thread leaks. Added warning log, worker_loop.close() still runs unconditionally.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25950
    
    fix(cron): grace period computation failure causes long-period jobs to be skipped — croniter parse failure in _compute_grace_seconds() silently fell back to MIN_GRACE (120s). For 6-hour jobs correct grace is ~3h, 120s causes silent job skips on small time drifts.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25952
    [embed] fix(security): handle IPv6 scope IDs in URL safety checks to preven... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25961
    [embed] fix(cron): log and redact on secrets-redaction failure by sprmn24 ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25904
    [embed] fix(cron): log channel name resolution failures instead of silently... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25947

    BuG: Teknium decided to use the word "prune".
    
         Git evidence:
         - Commit 436672de0e by Teknium, dated 2026-05-05, titled: feat(curator):
         add archive and prune subcommands (#20200)
         - The commit message explicitly introduces both verbs: "Adds 'hermes
         curator archive <skill>' and 'hermes curator prune [--days N] [--yes]
         [--dry-run]'"
         - The _cmd_prune() function and its docstring naming were added in that
         single commit.
    
         So it was a human naming decision, not an LLM artifact. The word was
         chosen despite the actual operation being "bulk-archive" — there was no
         code distinction between what prune does vs what archive does; both call
         the same archive_skill() function.

[2026-05-15T08:17:51.093000+00:00] ajitpriyadarshi (id=1504759706416320513)
    NEURON 🧠 — an open-source cognitive memory layer for AI agents.
    
    Today, most agents “remember” using flat RAG + vector search. Useful, but memory stays shallow, brittle, and forgetful.
    
    NEURON treats memory more like a brain:
    
    • surprise-based memory formation
    • semantic belief extraction
    • synaptic weighting (“myelination”)
    • consolidation + pruning (“sleep cycles”)
    • hybrid retrieval (vector + graph walk)
    • adaptive retrieval strategies
    
    Goal: move beyond context windows toward persistent, evolving world models for agents.
    
    Works with:
    Postgres + pgvector
    Neo4j
    in-memory graphs
    
    Integrates with:
    LangChain
    LangGraph
    CrewAI
    LlamaIndex
    
    Looking for people to test, critique, or break it 😄
    GitHub: https://github.com/Z-Sigma/neuron
    
    #AI #LLMs #Agents #OpenSource #RAG #GraphDB
    [embed] GitHub - Z-Sigma/neuron: Cognitive memory layer for AI applications https://github.com/Z-Sigma/neuron

[2026-05-15T09:37:00.809000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1504779628168872066)
    Hey maintainers 👋
    
    Could someone please review this PR when you get a chance?
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26073
    
    Context: we hit this exact issue while dogfooding Hermes in a live Telegram gateway session.
    
    What happened:
    - The agent attempted to edit `/root/.ssh/config` with `patch`
    - Hermes correctly blocked it because it is a protected system/credential file
    - The agent then recovered through another route and the SSH alias was actually present/usable afterwards
    - But the final response still appended the old file-mutation verifier footer saying the file was “NOT modified”, which was stale/misleading
    
    This PR improves that behaviour by:
    - Reporting “file-edit attempt failed” instead of claiming the file was not modified
    - Detecting when the file changed later in the same turn
    - Adding “file changed later; verify the final diff” so the user gets the right audit signal
    
    I mirrored the PR locally and verified:
    
    `python -m pytest -o addopts="-m 'not integration'" tests/run_agent/test_file_mutation_verifier.py tests/agent/test_tool_result_classification.py -q`
    → 41 passed
    
    `python -m py_compile run_agent.py`
    → passed
    
    This preserves the safety guardrail while removing a noisy/misleading footer in real use.
    [embed] fix: refine file mutation verifier recovery reporting by KShad10 ·... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26073

[2026-05-15T11:30:03.686000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1504808077617266738)
    Hey <@387972437901312000> , quick context on the Brave Search work:
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/26276
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26286
    
    alt-glitch noted this is related to:
    
    - #10644, the original Brave Search tracking issue
    - #21337, the already-merged `brave-free` provider
    - #21726, an open docs PR for Brave/Parallel env vars
    
    I added an issue comment clarifying the lineage after alt-glitch’s note: this should be seen as the paid/rich-mode continuation of the Brave work, not a duplicate of the free provider or the docs-only env-var PR.
    
    Small personal note: I have been using this kind of Brave workflow from the beginning as a custom Hermes tool, and even before that as a shell-based tool when I was using OpenClaw. I disabled the default open web search path for my own setup because Brave’s richer search plus grounding flow was just that useful in day-to-day research. This PR is basically turning that proven local workflow into a clean upstream Hermes feature.
    
    Why this is needed:
    
    Hermes currently has `brave-free`, but it is intentionally basic search only. Brave’s Search API has richer research surfaces that are very useful for Hermes: web search with extra snippets, images, news, videos, discussions, suggestions, and grounding context. This PR gives users who have Brave API credits a native way to use those capabilities without local scripts or custom one-off tools.
    
    What this PR adds:
    
    - New explicit `brave-search` provider, separate from `brave-free`
    - New `brave_search` tool
    - Default `both` mode: web results plus grounding context
    - Modes: `web`, `llm`, `images`, `news`, `videos`, `discussions`, `suggest`, and bounded `raw`
    - Generic `web_search` compatibility still returns the standard Hermes `{ data: { web: [...] } }` shape
    - Existing `brave-free` users stay backward-compatible and are not silently moved
    - Supports `BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY`, with `BRAVE_API_KEY` as a legacy fallback
    - Clean errors for plan or endpoint access issues, with no secret leakage
    
    Why it is better than the current state:
    
    `brave-free` is great as a basic search backend, but this unlocks the richer Brave Search API product for real research workflows. It means Hermes can search the web, pull news/videos/images/discussions, get suggestions, and return source-grounded context through one native tool. It also keeps the provider split clean: free/basic stays simple, paid/richer capabilities are opt-in.
    
    Validation:
    
    - Focused local tests: 191 passed
    - Ruff passed
    - Code review gate was run before publishing, and actionable findings were fixed
    - CI is mostly green: lint, attribution, docs, builds, Nix, and supply-chain checks pass
    - Remaining CI failures are documented on the PR and appear unrelated to this diff:
      - Discord e2e fixture failures
      - Nous provider parity context-window tests
    
    I think this is a strong merge because it closes the gap between the already-merged basic Brave provider and the original broader Brave Search vision in #10644, while avoiding scope creep into Brave Answers. It gives Hermes a much stronger native research backend without breaking existing users or adding heavy dependencies.
    [embed] Feature: add Brave Search API paid provider with rich search modes ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/26276
    [embed] feat(web): add Brave Search API rich modes by GodsBoy · Pull Reque... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26286

[2026-05-15T16:03:28.016000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1504876882363940974)
    <@387972437901312000>  I opened 2 more PRs today focused on concurrency safety and resource leak prevention:
    
    **fix(memory):** TOCTOU race in Windows _file_lock() — write_text() then open('r+') has a window where another process can delete the lock file, causing uncaught FileNotFoundError and memory writes proceeding without the lock. Replaced with single open('a+') call.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26423
    
    **fix(delegate)**: heartbeat thread orphan in _run_single_child() — _heartbeat_thread.start() was called before the try/finally block. If _register_subagent() raised, _heartbeat_stop.set() never ran and the thread kept resetting parent gateway timeouts. Moved .start() inside the try block.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26435
    [embed] fix(memory): eliminate TOCTOU race in Windows file lock creation by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26423
    [embed] fix(delegate): move heartbeat thread start inside try block to prev... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26435

[2026-05-15T16:33:33.353000+00:00] cryptoworlldz (id=1504884454496145553)
    I found a bug in hermes doctor around active provider validation. 
    
    If model.provider is set to 'openrouter' but neither 'OPENROUTER_API_KEY' nor 'OPENAI_API_KEY' is configured, Hermes runtime fails with 'No LLM provider configured'.
    
    But 'hermes doctor' was not surfacing that as a real blocking issue in the final summary, even though the active provider was unusable.
    
    I opened both the issue and the fix here:
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/26436
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26453
    
    <@387972437901312000>  Lately I have been focusing on the builder side of web3. I would like to know if I am doing it right or wrong. Even though I have been using Hermes for a very long time, this is the first time I am trying to make a contribution like this.
    [embed] [Bug]: hermes doctor does not flag missing credentials for an activ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/26436
    [embed] Fix doctor summary for active OpenRouter without credentials by wor... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26453

[2026-05-15T16:49:00.883000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1504888344838930567)
    <@808920669147889664> 
    https://magaav.github.io/simulation-hermes-agent-expectation-trace/
    Hermes already has regression tests for correctness. Lab v1 explores a different layer: recovery diagnostics. When behavior fails, can the agent produce a useful trace, classify the failure, and suggest the next repair path?
    
    For v1, the inputs were curated from Hermes issue/PR-linked failure cases and frozen into 12 fixed fixtures. The dataset is still small and hand-selected, so I’m not claiming statistical significance or production reliability gains.
    
    The goal is to evolve this into a real community-backed recovery-eval harness: every useful failure dump can potentially become a reusable fixture for testing future Hermes recovery behavior.

[2026-05-15T16:50:40.737000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1504888763657228350)

    [embed] LeWorldModel: Stable End-to-End Joint-Embedding Predictive Architec... https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19312

[2026-05-15T21:02:18.884000+00:00] gokhe (id=1504952089875583097)
    I noticed that when users return to an old Discord thread and ping Hermes, the agent starts from a completely blank context with no awareness of what was discussed before. This makes it frustrating to continue a conversation that happened a few days ago, since you have to re-explain everything.
    
    This PR adds automatic thread history restoration: the first time Hermes receives a message in a thread that has no existing Hermes session, it fetches the prior messages from Discord's API and injects them as read-only context before responding. The agent can then pick up naturally where the thread left off.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26551
    [embed] feat(discord): restore thread history context on first message in a... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26551
    [reply to gokhe msg=1504420961343111238]

On a remote SSH session, the OAuth provider redirects to http.://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback — which only the remote machine's listener can catch. Without a port-forward, the flow silently times out. [2026-05-16T21:59:01.412000+00:00] iftakharr (id=1505328748977979412)
    Hey Hermes folks — we just added Hermes support to DCP.
    
    DCP is a local permission layer for agents: instead of giving Hermes raw wallet keys, API keys, or .env secrets, Hermes can request scoped actions and you approve what matters.
    
    Would love a few Hermes users to try it, especially VPS setups.
    Desktop: https://dcpagent.com
    Repo: https://github.com/1lystore/dcp
    
    Remote Hermes install uses an invite from Desktop, then:
    curl -fsSL https://dcpagent.com/install.sh | sudo bash -s -- 'dcp_vps_v1_...'
    
    Then run /reload-mcp in Hermes. And ask list all DCP tools to see whta DCP can do
    [embed] DCP gives agents permission, not keys. https://dcpagent.com/
    [embed] GitHub - 1lystore/dcp: Permission layer for AI agents — wallet si... https://github.com/1lystore/dcp

[2026-05-16T22:13:35.351000+00:00] 0x001185 (id=1505332414543823050)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27147
    **PR: fix(skills): return None instead of truthy stub when skill load fails**
    #27147
    
    When a skill fails to load, the error message was being injected into the model as a user prompt. Now returns None so callers properly detect the failure.  ## Tests Added
    
    - `test_returns_none_when_skill_load_fails` in `tests/agent/test_skill_commands.py` — verifies that `build_skill_invocation_message()` returns `None` when a registered skill exists in the command cache but `_load_skill_payload()` fails.
    [embed] fix(skills): return None instead of truthy stub when skill load fai... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27147

[2026-05-16T22:40:32.896000+00:00] 0x001185 (id=1505339199019155508)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27154
    **PR #3: fix(gateway): add missing logger definition to prevent NameError**
    
    `hermes_cli/gateway.py:3702` referenced `logger.debug()` but `logger` was never defined in the module. If `discover_plugins()` fails inside `_all_platforms()`, the except block crashes with a `NameError` instead of logging the error gracefully.
    
    **Fix:** Added `import logging` and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at module level.

[2026-05-17T08:37:16.291000+00:00] 0x001185 (id=1505489369342152777)
    🐛 **PR #4: fix(agent) — NameError in rate-limit fallback decision**
    
    **The Bug:** When the agent hits a 429 rate limit, `run_conversation()` tries to check if the credential pool can recover by calling `_pool_may_recover_from_rate_limit()`. But that function is defined in `run_agent.py` — and after the recent refactor that extracted `conversation_loop.py`, the call site forgot to import it. Result: instead of falling back gracefully, the agent crashes with a `NameError` right when it's already being rate-limited. 💀
    
    **The Fix:** One character — prefixed the call with `_ra().` — which is the existing lazy-import pattern used throughout `conversation_loop.py` for `run_agent` symbols. Clean, minimal, matches codebase conventions.   <@387972437901312000> 
    
    📎 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27359
    [embed] fix(agent): resolve NameError in rate-limit fallback decision by 0x... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27359

    #27366
    When xAI returns a Grok subscription error via SSE instead of HTTP 403, status_code is None so the classifier skips status-based checks. The error fell through to unknown/retryable=True, burning all retries. Fix adds an explicit pattern match at step 1 so both paths return auth/non-retryable. Three regression tests added.

    [embed] fix(error_classifier): classify xAI Grok entitlement SSE errors as ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27366

[2026-05-17T21:37:37.500000+00:00] minddragon (id=1505685751726084096)
    <@387972437901312000> Have a bit more todo but it will be a completely fresh implementation from origin main through to a proper full oauth/api/whatever implementation to include browser oauth port stuff so that xai oauth (random ports) can post back to a remote (via ssh) headless

[2026-05-17T21:38:40.594000+00:00] minddragon (id=1505686016361762927)
    On origin main: the xai-outh provider has key_env: ' and relies on Auth tokens stored in authjson under providers.xai-oauth. The main chat works because the agent session carries that OAuth context. But the auxiliary client resolves credentials independently - it checks key_env first (empty, so nothing), then tries the vault (which has xai API key, not xai-outh Auth tokens), and falls through with no auth. The xai provider (non-Auth, key_env: XAI_API_KEY) would work fine for aux tasks if the key is set. But xai-outh specifically has no credential resolution path for the auxiliary client. That's a design gap in the upstream implementation - OAuth providers aren't wired into the auxiliary credential resolver.

[2026-05-17T22:30:22.369000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1505699026149052520)
    fix(qqbot): cap _typing_sent_at and _last_msg_id with bounded eviction
    Two dicts in QQAdapter grow without bound until the process restarts:
    
    _last_msg_id — one entry per unique chat_id, no eviction
    _typing_sent_at — one entry per unique chat_id, no eviction
    The same class already bounds _seen_messages with DEDUP_MAX_SIZE + DEDUP_WINDOW_SECONDS — these two maps simply missed the same treatment.
    
    Fix: FIFO eviction for _last_msg_id (cap 2 000), TTL eviction for _typing_sent_at (cap 2 000 / 1 h window), symmetric with the existing pattern.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27641
    [embed] fix(qqbot): cap _typing_sent_at and _last_msg_id with bounded evict... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/27641

[2026-05-18T05:12:27.132000+00:00] selinchu (id=1505800212738867321)
    Hey, I opened a small bugfix PR for the MSGraph webhook adapter.
    
    The issue was about accepted_resources: when it was configured as a plain string, it was being treated like an iterable and split into individual characters. That made the allowlist fail to match valid Graph resource paths.
    
    This PR makes scalar strings behave the same as a single-item list, while keeping the existing list/tuple/set behavior unchanged. 
    
    Would appreciate it if someone could take a look when they have a chance. <@387972437901312000>

[2026-05-18T06:58:00.574000+00:00] axmaiqiu (id=1505826777119850496)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, I’ve been testing the newer Kanban runtime path on current main and split a few reliability fixes into small focused PRs rather than bundling them together:
    #26177 fixes a Kanban worker timeout mismatch where long-running worker commands can be killed by the inherited terminal timeout before `task.max_runtime_seconds`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26177
    #25591 aligns repeated-failure diagnostics with the actual Kanban failure limit, so the warning appears before the task is effectively blocked. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25591
    #25640 adds a diagnostic for missing `auxiliary.triage_specifier`, which can otherwise leave triage tasks stuck without an obvious config hint. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25640
    #25639 makes Kanban worker log retention configurable; the current single `.log.1` rotation can lose early failure context for long-running workers.  https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25639
    All four are rebased on current main, mergeable, one commit each, and include focused tests. Would appreciate a look when you have time.

[2026-05-18T15:03:22.403000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1505948922923978922)
    Silent deadlock in the new kanban auto-decompose (shipped yesterday). If the LLM returns a cyclic task graph 
    — A waiting on B, B waiting on A — the decomposition succeeds silently but those tasks sit in todo forever, never dispatched.
    
    link_tasks() already guards this with _would_cycle(), but decompose_triage_task bypasses it for atomicity and the cycle check was never ported. Fix: Kahn topological sort in pre-validation, before any DB writes. <@387972437901312000> 
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28050
    [embed] fix(kanban): detect cycles in decompose_triage_task sibling-link pr... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28050

[2026-05-18T15:10:20.574000+00:00] magnus919 (id=1505950676860141670)

    The one gotcha with this is the default filesystem memory provider needs a circuit breaker (in the PR) to reject a claude import because of its scaling limitations. But use any optional memory provider and it'll happily ingest.

[2026-05-18T17:32:15.768000+00:00] dskwe. (id=1505986392172527790)
    Hi team! Could a maintainer help approve the CI workflows for this security PR so the test suite can run?
    
    PR #17443: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17443
    
    What it fixes: A critical SSRF bypass vulnerability where attackers can hit cloud metadata endpoints (like AWS IMDSv1 at 169.254.169.254) using IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:x.x.x.x).
    
    Why it happens: Python's ipaddress module treats them as distinct IPv6 addresses, completely bypassing the existing frozenset and network range checks.
    
    Status: Branch conflicts are resolved, and outdated description notes have been fully cleaned up. Ready for review!
    
    Thanks! 🙌
    [embed] fix(url_safety): block IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses to prevent SSRF b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17443

[2026-05-18T22:21:51.657000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1506059271933136927)
    fix(spotify): return 400 when loopback callback receives no code or error
    
    _SpotifyCallbackHandler was returning 200 "Spotify authorization received" when the callback URL had neither code nor error — browser showed success while the CLI wait loop timed out after 180s. Symmetric fix with _XAICallbackHandler (bf6eeb3f9). +3 tests. All blocking CI green, 1 approval.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28207
    [embed] fix(spotify): return 400 when loopback callback receives no code or... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28207

[2026-05-18T22:49:17.989000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1506066177150025900)
    PR #28240 — fix 15-minute Anthropic stream hang (P1)
    
    Telegram/gateway users on claude-opus-4-7 were seeing agents go silent for ~15 minutes before recovering.
    
    Root cause: three stream cleanup sites in chat_completion_helpers.py called _replace_primary_openai_client() unconditionally. On Anthropic-only configs this silently fails (no OPENAI_API_KEY), leaving the in-flight httpx stream unclosed — worker thread blocks until the 900s timeout fires.
    
    Fix: guard all three sites with api_mode == "anthropic_messages" and call _anthropic_client.close() + _rebuild_anthropic_client() instead. Same pattern already used correctly in the interrupt path, just missed at these three sites.
    
    Two competing PRs were open — one covered only the stale-stream detector, the other only the retry paths. Ours is the first to cover all three + Bedrock parity via _rebuild_anthropic_client().
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28240
    [embed] fix(streaming): rebuild Anthropic client on stream cleanup instead ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28240

[2026-05-19T05:11:45.551000+00:00] axmaiqiu (id=1506162426200985711)
    Hi <@387972437901312000>, I have three small gateway-related PRs that came from existing community issues. I rechecked the underlying failure modes against current main, and they still look reachable. I refreshed the branches so the fixes stay small and independently reviewable.
    - #25051 fixes #25010: partial streamed replies can remain visible while the complete final response is suppressed. This now first tries to edit the existing streamed message to the complete `final_response`, and only falls back to a normal final send if that edit cannot be confirmed.
      https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25051
    - #21640 fixes #21611: follow-up messages arriving during the narrow final-delivery window can interfere with a completed turn’s final reply. This queues those follow-ups instead of interrupting or suppressing the already-completed response.
      https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/21640
    - #25561 fixes #25242: gateway auto-continue recovery notes can become durable session/compression context after interrupts. This keeps the recovery note API-only, adds a one-shot acknowledgement for recovered tool tails, and has been updated for the new `conversation_loop` split.
      https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/25561
    All three are intentionally scoped as small cherry-pickable fixes with focused tests. Would appreciate a look when someone has time — and happy to rebase or reshape them if you prefer a different approach.

[2026-05-19T13:51:05.377000+00:00] quocanh261997 (id=1506293119984074864)
    Hi <@387972437901312000> , after `hermes update` today, I found a bug that a commit adds a new column `task.session_id` into the Kanban tables. However, on startup, it tries to create an index on that column first before running migration, causing the issue that the `session_id` wasn't found. I've fixed it by moving index creation until after migration, so legacy boards can open and upgrade normally: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28754

[2026-05-19T15:39:40.483000+00:00] cryptoworlldz (id=1506320446318972928)
    Found a real sqlite fd leak in the kanban specify helpers.
    
    kanban_specify was using with kb.connect() as conn: on sqlite connections, which does not actually close them. In a live repro on current main, 50 calls to list_triage_ids() grew the fd count by 47
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/28802
    [embed] [Bug]: kanban specify helpers leak sqlite connections in long-lived... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/28802

[2026-05-19T15:57:20.115000+00:00] cryptoworlldz (id=1506324890737836243)
    Tracked down a pretty sneaky sqlite fd leak in the kanban specify flow
    It turned out the helper was using with kb.connect() as conn: on sqlite connections, which does not close the connection the way it looks like it does. In a quick live repro, 50 calls to list_triage_ids() grew the process fd count by 47
    Opened the writeup + fix here
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28803
    [embed] fix(kanban): close sqlite connections in specify helpers by worlldz... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28803

[2026-05-19T18:05:43.940000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1506357202921848967)
    <@387972437901312000> I think Hermes will eventually hit a serious context-engineering wall.
    
    As the harness grows, the hard part won’t just be model intelligence, but orientation: helping agents understand the repo, preserve architectural intent, avoid stale assumptions, and make safe changes without drowning in context.
    
    One possible direction is a Hilbert-style repo/context map.
    
    Large repos destroy locality: related files, tests, contracts, failures, runtime paths, and assumptions get scattered across token space. Hilbert curves are useful because they can linearize complex/high-dimensional structure while preserving neighborhoods.
    
    So instead of feeding the model a flat pile of files, Hermes could maintain a locality-preserving coordinate system for the repo. When an agent works on one area, it retrieves the nearby semantic/architectural neighborhood: relevant modules, contracts, tests, recent failures, memory chunks, and recovery traces.
    
    That gives the agent better orientation with fewer tokens, and reduces accidental mutations outside the task boundary.
    
    If Hermes is moving toward self-evolving agent infrastructure, the next bottleneck may not be bigger context windows.
    
    It may be better coordinates.

[2026-05-19T18:12:16.126000+00:00] teknium (id=1506358847869026334)
    mmm I'm not sure I agree, context rot is going away even for models with 1M context; 10, 100M context is coming - the only way to achieve that is extremely cheap methods for good context - I honestly expect context issues will be largely a thing of history in a year or so

[2026-05-19T19:55:18.167000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1506384777228583058)
    I am claiming that it is already fixed and moving from 1M to 100M is not the way bc it becomes more expensive
    [reply to teknium msg=1506358847869026334]

[2026-05-19T19:57:27.150000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1506385318222499880)
    ```
    yool = the smallest callable capability atom, basically an action/tool opcode. Example: read sector, open page, spawn
      agent, scan index, route packet.
    
      tuple = the addressable envelope that binds yools to map position, authority, lane, agent terms, source pointers, and
      receipts. So yes: tuple is closer to “map plus routing key plus context,” and yool is closer to “action atom,” but in
      this system maps can themselves be tuple-addressed.
    
      The fundamentals I’d point him at:
    
      - Tuple spaces: Gelernter’s Linda is the closest ancestor for “tuples as coordination substrate”: Generative Communic
      - Hilbert indexing: why multidimensional catalogs can map to compact locality-preserving keys: Jagadish Linear Cluste
        ring of Objects with Multiple Attributes (https://dl.acm.org/doi/pdf/10.1145/93605.98742), plus Moon/Jagadish/Falou
        tsos/Saltz clustering analysis (https://drum.lib.umd.edu/items/c713788f-517c-4c2e-a6a2-7ea9d7e69c5b).
      - HAMT/hash-trie fundamentals: why catalogs are not limited to 256 tools: Bagwell, Ideal Hash Trees
        (https://lampwww.epfl.ch/papers/idealhashtrees.pdf).
      - Token/compression foundation: Shannon, A Mathematical Theory of Communication
        (https://people.math.harvard.edu/~ctm/home/text/others/shannon/entropy/entropy.pdf).
      - For the MCP point: official MCP tools are model-invoked and cacheable, which supports your design decision to keep
        MCP as edge/snapshot/dashboard surface, not inner loop: MCP tools spec
        (https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft/server/tools).
    
      Short answer to his “256x256?”: no. Base-256 glyphs are alphabet slots, not the whole address space. A tuple of length
      k gives 256^k symbol paths before you even count pointer catalogs, recursive cubes, maps, and authority layers. That
      is why one yool can be one atom, while tuples can expand into effectively unbounded catalogs.
    ```

[2026-05-19T19:59:52.165000+00:00] colmeio_66868 (id=1506385926459490375)
    but that is just one fundamental that compounds... scale from 1M to 100M can sole couple problems but there are limits there in which using tuples/yool can fix categorically the context

[2026-05-19T21:37:12.147000+00:00] sprmn24 (id=1506410421119094944)
    <@387972437901312000>  , I opened a few PRs today , take a look when you get a chance:
    
    **PR #28917**
    Issue: socket.getaddrinfo() has no timeout — a slow or unresponsive DNS server blocks the SSRF safety check for up to 30s, stalling the gateway request handler.
    Fix: Wrapped both call sites in ThreadPoolExecutor with a 5s fail-closed timeout.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28917
    
    **PR #28923**
    Issue: _extract_retry_delay_seconds returned an unbounded float from provider error messages — a response of "retry after 999999999 seconds" would freeze the credential for 31+ years.
    Fix: Capped both return paths at 3600s.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28923
    
    **PR #28932**
    Issue: Proxy retry gate only triggered on 401 — a 429 from xAI was silently forwarded to the client with no key rotation or cooldown.
    Fix: Widened gate to {401, 429}, added mark_exhausted_and_rotate on rate-limit so the offending key gets its cooldown and the next key is tried.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28932
    **
    PR #28935**
    Issue: Bare except Exception in channel name resolution silently dropped the real error — import failures, DB errors, and permission issues all returned the same generic message.
    Fix: Bound the exception, added logger.warning(exc_info=True), and surfaced str(e) to the caller.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28935
    [embed] fix(url_safety): add 5s timeout to socket.getaddrinfo() calls by sp... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28917
    [embed] fix(credential_pool): cap retry delay at 3600s to prevent indefinit... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28923
    [embed] fix(xai-proxy): handle 429 rate-limit responses in proxy retry path... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28932
    [embed] fix(send_message): log and surface channel resolution errors by spr... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/28935

[2026-05-19T22:18:13.440000+00:00] helvio88 (id=1506420744530428085)
    I made a few changes to xai.py and ../server.py to accommodate /audio/speech mapping (openai standard) to /tts (xai standard). Do you want those snippets?
    [reply to sprmn24 msg=1506410421119094944]

[2026-05-19T22:35:20.197000+00:00] helvio88 (id=1506425051061420134)
    this goes after `body = await request.read()`

[2026-05-19T22:35:47.506000+00:00] helvio88 (id=1506425165603410042)
    ```
            # ============================================================
            # Safely apply optional adapter transformations.
            # Adapters that don't implement map_path() / transform_request_body()
            # will fall back to identity (no-op). This keeps Nous + other
            # adapters working without modification.
            # ============================================================
            map_path_fn = getattr(adapter, "map_path", lambda p: p)
            rel_path = map_path_fn(rel_path)
    
            transform_body_fn = getattr(adapter, "transform_request_body", lambda p, b: b)
            body = transform_body_fn(rel_path, body)
    ```

[2026-05-19T22:37:04.502000+00:00] helvio88 (id=1506425488548040795)
    `xai.py` map functions:
    
    ```python
        # ============================================================
        # PATH REWRITING - Grok uses /tts instead of /audio/speech
        # ============================================================
        def map_path(self, requested_path: str) -> str:
            """
            Translate incoming paths to what xAI actually expects.
            """
            path = requested_path.lower().rstrip("/")
    
            if path == "/audio/speech":
                logger.debug("proxy: rewriting /audio/speech → /tts for xAI")
                return "/tts"
    
            if path == "/tts":
                return "/tts"
    
            return requested_path
    ```

[2026-05-19T22:37:19.745000+00:00] helvio88 (id=1506425552482074726)
    and
    
    ```python
        # ============================================================
        # REQUEST BODY TRANSFORMATION - OpenAI TTS → xAI TTS
        # ============================================================
        def transform_request_body(self, path: str, body: bytes) -> bytes:
            """
            Convert OpenAI-compatible TTS request body to xAI format.
    
            Mappings:
                input     → text
                voice     → voice_id
                model     → dropped (irrelevant for xAI)
                + text_normalization: true
                + language: "auto"
            """
            normalized_path = path.lower().rstrip("/")
    
            # Only transform TTS paths
            if normalized_path not in {"/audio/speech", "/tts"}:
                return body
    
            if not body:
                return body
    
            try:
                data = json.loads(body)
                if not isinstance(data, dict):
                    return body
    
                # input -> text
                if "input" in data:
                    data["text"] = data.pop("input")
    
                # voice -> voice_id
                if "voice" in data:
                    data["voice_id"] = data.pop("voice")
    
                # model is not used by xAI TTS
                data.pop("model", None)
    
                # Add text_normalization
                data.setdefault("text_normalization", True)
    
                # Add language
                data.setdefault("language", "auto")
    
                transformed = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
                logger.debug("proxy: transformed OpenAI TTS body → xAI format")
                return transformed
    
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError, KeyError) as exc:
                logger.warning("proxy: failed to transform TTS body, forwarding original: %s", exc)
                return body
    ```

[2026-05-20T05:01:05.783000+00:00] .s0uthpaw (id=1506522130685366292)
    TL;DR: The 405 happens because `/dashboard-plugins/{name}/{file}` is a GET-only route — plugin installation should POST to `/api/dashboard/agent-plugins/install` instead. The 404 is because the "example" plugin doesn't exist on disk (it's a template, not bundled with Hermes). To install plugins via Tailscale dashboard, use `curl -X POST http://<tailscale-ip>:<port>/api/dashboard/agent-plugins/install -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"identifier": "hermes-achievements"}'` or install via CLI first (`hermes plugins install <name>`), then force rescan.
    
    Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/extending-the-dashboard
    
    Full details attached.
    [attachment] dashboard-plugin-405-fix-20260519_1440.txt https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1506522130890756176/dashboard-plugin-405-fix-20260519_1440.txt?ex=6a0e9191&is=6a0d4011&hm=62aac605949fea0854678d8495f1c54a943ddeb500e6047bd261887be13e41e2&
    [embed] Extending the Dashboard | Hermes Agent https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/extending-the-dashboard
    [reply to cctno msg=1506519251005997157]

[2026-05-20T07:05:49.223000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1506553518507560981)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, quick nudge on this PR when you have a review window:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26286
    
    This adds a first-class paid Brave Search API integration to Hermes, alongside the existing free Brave fallback. I refreshed it against current main today, resolved the web_tools.py conflict, and the PR is mergeable again. Local focused verification is green: 252 relevant tests passing across the Brave provider/tool path, web provider registry, Hermes tools config, toolsets, and xAI-adjacent registry coverage.
    
    Why this is worth reviewing:
    
    - It gives Hermes a proper high-quality search backend, not just scraped or limited fallback search.
    - It adds a direct `brave_search` tool for agents, with rich modes instead of forcing every search through one generic web_search shape.
    - Default `both` mode returns regular web results plus Brave LLM grounding context, which is excellent for agent research because it gives both source URLs and compact grounding snippets.
    - It supports specialised modes agents can call directly:
      - `web`: normal Brave web results
      - `llm`: Brave LLM Context / grounding endpoint
      - `images`: image search metadata
      - `news`: fresh news results
      - `videos`: video results
      - `discussions`: forum / discussion-style results via Brave filtering
      - `suggest`: query suggestions / autocomplete
      - `raw`: bounded raw Brave API payloads for debugging or power users
    - It keeps backwards compatibility: existing users with BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY are not silently moved from `brave-free` to the paid backend unless they explicitly choose `brave-search` in config.
    - It plugs into Hermes cleanly as both:
      - a web provider plugin under `plugins/web/brave_search/`
      - a registered tool under the `web` toolset
    - It includes docs and env var coverage so users know about `BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY` / `BRAVE_API_KEY` and the new web backend option.
    - The implementation normalises Brave responses into Hermes-friendly shapes: web, news, videos, images, discussions, FAQ, infobox, locations, LLM context, and suggestions.
    - It gives Hermes a strong paid-search story for current information, OSINT-style research, SEO/GEO work, news monitoring, media discovery, and grounded agent answers.
    
    In short: this makes Hermes materially better at real-time research. Firecrawl/Parallel/Exa/Tavily are still valuable, but Brave paid search gives us a broad, fast, source-rich baseline with LLM context and media/news/discussion endpoints in one provider. It is the kind of tool agents will actually use every day.
    
    Would appreciate your review when you can. The PR is scoped, tested, documented, and refreshed against current main.
    [embed] feat(web): add Brave Search API rich modes by GodsBoy · Pull Reque... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26286

[2026-05-20T10:17:05.038000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1506601651564314624)
    sorry I was out doing a run.
    
    Yep, Hermes already supports Brave via the existing `brave-free` web provider path.
    
    This PR is slightly different though:
    
    1. It adds a separate paid Brave Search API provider as `brave-search`, without changing existing `brave-free` behaviour.
    2. It is already implemented as a web provider plugin:
       `plugins/web/brave_search/plugin.yaml`
       `plugins/web/brave_search/provider.py`
       `plugins/web/brave_search/client.py`
    3. The extra top-level `brave_search` tool is there because the paid Brave API exposes richer modes than the generic `web_search` shape can cleanly represent:
       - web
       - LLM context / grounding
       - images
       - news
       - videos
       - discussions
       - suggestions
       - bounded raw API output
    
    So the intent is not “replace Brave support”, it is:
    
    - keep `brave-free` as-is for existing users
    - add `brave-search` as the paid/richer provider plugin
    - expose a dedicated `brave_search` tool for the endpoints that do not map nicely to generic web_search
    
    If you prefer, I can trim the scope so the PR is only the provider plugin first, and move the dedicated `brave_search` tool into a follow-up PR. But the current shape is plugin-based already, with the direct tool just exposing the richer paid API surface to agents.
    [reply to teknium msg=1506594002885939220]

[2026-05-20T11:56:54.276000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1506626772249084059)
    fix(feishu): transcribe native voice notes — PR #29295
    
    When a user sent a voice note on Feishu, the bot never transcribed it. The adapter was classifying Feishu's audio message type as MessageType.AUDIO, but the gateway only runs STT for MessageType.VOICE. Since Feishu's audio type is exclusively for in-app voice recordings (file uploads go through a different type), the fix is a one-liner — return VOICE directly instead of resolving from the MIME type.
    
    The same bug was fixed for Discord and DingTalk yesterday (#28918, 93734c26e). Feishu was missed.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/29295
    [embed] fix(feishu): transcribe native voice notes by EloquentBrush0x · Pu... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/29295

[2026-05-21T06:33:47.127000+00:00] eloquentbrush0x (id=1506907844518088796)
    (My King <@387972437901312000> , we struggled a lot with Claude on this fix. Hopefully it works.)
    
    PR #29683 — Telegram observe mode: location & media parity fix
    
    The recent "observe unmentioned group messages" feature (a9db0e2) only wired up text and command handlers. Location pins and media messages (photo, voice, video, sticker, etc.) had two bugs:
    
    Observe gap: unmentioned messages were silently dropped instead of being stored as shared group context
    Attribution gap: when the bot was triggered via a media/location message, it used the per-user session instead of the shared group session — so it couldn't see any of the previously observed group chatter
    Fix adds the observe check and _apply_telegram_group_observe_attribution call to both _handle_location_message and _handle_media_message. Sticker path is handled separately since _handle_sticker overwrites event.text with the vision description. 4 new tests, all 35 pass.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/29683
    [embed] fix(gateway): extend observe+attribution to location and media hand... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/29683

[2026-05-21T12:33:38.581000+00:00] jason2335129 (id=1506998405640228915)
    Thanks for the review! I looked into the `slash_access` system from PR #29077. Here's why this PR is still needed:
    
    Yuanbao group chat has platform-specific message formats and requirements that the generic `slash_access` system cannot directly cover. This PR adds `OwnerCommandMiddleware` in the Yuanbao inbound pipeline (upstream of `gateway/run.py`) to handle these scenarios:
    
    1. **Group slash commands require special parsing** — Yuanbao group messages start with a `TIMCustomElem` @-mention element, so after `_extract_text()` the text becomes `"@BotName /stop"` instead of `"/stop"`. Since `event.is_command()` checks `text.startswith("/")`, the generic layer can't recognize these as slash commands. The adapter middleware parses commands directly from `msg_body`.
    
    2. **Auto-discovered owner identity** — Bot owner is queried via `QueryBotInfoReq` over the Yuanbao WebSocket, no manual `allow_admin_from` config needed.
    
    3. **Owner commands skip the serialization queue** — Group messages use a serial queue to prevent concurrent interruptions. This blocks control commands like `/stop` and `/approve` from taking effect promptly. Owner commands are dispatched directly to ensure immediate execution.
    
    4. **Group command allowlist** — We want group commands focused on session control (`/stop`, `/approve`, `/deny`, `/reset`, etc.) rather than exposing everything. `GROUP_ALLOWLIST` enforces this. The generic `can_run()` always permits all commands for admins and cannot enforce such restrictions.
    
    All changes are Yuanbao platform-specific, living entirely in the adapter layer with no core file modifications.
    [reply to teknium msg=1506913530337558538]
