[2026-06-01T14:53:34.319000+00:00] pixelprox_99030 (id=1511019886439174287)
    Hey guys I have a problem setting up discord with my heremes bot.  I keep getting either a timeout error, or a 401 error does anyone know what to do?

[2026-06-01T15:01:59.898000+00:00] ancientalies (id=1511022006991196312)
    ```bash
    cat > ~/collect-hermes-discord-debug.sh <<'EOF'
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    
    TS="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
    OUT="$HOME/Desktop/hermes-discord-debug-$TS"
    ZIP="$OUT.zip"
    
    mkdir -p "$OUT"
    
    redact() {
      sed -E \
        -e 's/(DISCORD[_A-Z0-9]*TOKEN|BOT[_A-Z0-9]*TOKEN|TOKEN|API[_A-Z0-9]*KEY|SECRET|PASSWORD|AUTHORIZATION)[=:][[:space:]]*[^[:space:]]+/\1=<REDACTED>/Ig' \
        -e 's/Bot[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/Bot <REDACTED>/g' \
        -e 's/Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/Bearer <REDACTED>/g' \
        -e 's/[MN][A-Za-z0-9_-]{23,28}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{6,10}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{27,45}/<DISCORD_TOKEN_REDACTED>/g'
    }
    
    {
      echo "=== SYSTEM ==="
      date
      uname -a || true
      echo "SHELL=$SHELL"
      echo "PWD=$PWD"
    
      echo
      echo "=== COMMAND PATHS ==="
      command -v hermes || true
      command -v python3 || true
      command -v node || true
      command -v npm || true
    
      echo
      echo "=== VERSIONS ==="
      hermes --version 2>&1 || true
      python3 --version 2>&1 || true
      node --version 2>&1 || true
      npm --version 2>&1 || true
    
      echo
      echo "=== RELEVANT ENV VAR NAMES ONLY ==="
      env | grep -Ei 'discord|hermes|token|secret|api|bot' | sed -E 's/=.*$/=<REDACTED>/' || true
    
      echo
      echo "=== HERMES DIRECTORY TREE ==="
      if [ -d "$HOME/.hermes" ]; then
        find "$HOME/.hermes" -maxdepth 4 -type f | sed "s|$HOME|~|g" | sort
      else
        echo "~/.hermes not found"
      fi
    } | redact > "$OUT/system-info.txt"
    
    if [ -d "$HOME/.hermes/logs" ]; then
      mkdir -p "$OUT/logs"
      for f in "$HOME/.hermes/logs"/*.log; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        base="$(basename "$f")"
        {
          echo "===== $base : last 800 lines ====="
          tail -n 800 "$f" 2>&1 || true
        } | redact > "$OUT/logs/$base.tail.txt"
      done
    fi
    
    # Try to capture a fresh gateway startup log for 30 seconds.
    {
      echo "=== FRESH HERMES GATEWAY STARTUP CAPTURE ==="
      echo "Started at: $(date)"
      echo
      timeout 30s hermes gateway 2>&1 || true
      echo
      echo "Ended at: $(date)"
    } | redact > "$OUT/hermes-gateway-fresh-start.txt" || true
    
    cd "$HOME/Desktop"
    zip -r "$(basename "$ZIP")" "$(basename "$OUT")" >/dev/null
    
    echo
    echo "Created debug bundle:"
    echo "$ZIP"
    echo
    echo "Before sending, quickly inspect these files for secrets:"
    echo "$OUT/system-info.txt"
    echo "$OUT/hermes-gateway-fresh-start.txt"
    echo "$OUT/logs/"
    EOF
    
    chmod +x ~/collect-hermes-discord-debug.sh
    ~/collect-hermes-discord-debug.sh
    ```
    
    send the log file so someone can actually see whats going on
    [reply to pixelprox_99030 msg=1511019886439174287]

[2026-06-01T15:13:20.685000+00:00] ancientalies (id=1511024862418964640)
    Your biggest issue is showing `PrivilegedIntentsRequired: Shard ID None is requesting privileged intents that have not been explicitly enabled in the developer portal.`
    
    You need to enable some settings:  
    MESSAGE CONTENT INTENT = ON
    SERVER MEMBERS INTENT = ON
    PRESENCE INTENT = optional; leave OFF unless Hermes explicitly asks for it
    
    save changes then 
    
    ```bash
    pkill -f "hermes gateway" || true
    sleep 2
    hermes gateway
    ```
    run in another terminal 
    
    ```bash
    tail -f ~/.hermes/logs/gateway-error.log ~/.hermes/logs/agent.log ~/.hermes/logs/gateway.log 2>/dev/null
    ```
    
    after that you should not see `PrivilegedIntentsRequired`
    [reply to pixelprox_99030 msg=1511023651518943262]

[2026-06-01T15:13:51.302000+00:00] ancientalies (id=1511024990835970058)
    Enable Message Content Intent + Server Members Intent → Save → restart Hermes gateway.

[2026-06-01T22:19:55.708000+00:00] uzunkuyruk (id=1511132215726047512)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    Fix for MCP HTTP discovery blocking startup for 60s on non-MCP content type responses.
    
    When a misconfigured HTTP MCP URL returns text/html, Hermes waited the full 60s connect_timeout before surfacing CancelledError with no actionable context. Fix adds an httpx response event hook that detects non-MCP content types immediately and a non-retryable error class to skip the 3x retry loop entirely.
    
    All checks passed ✅
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/36052
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/36064
    
    2 files changed (fix + tests), non-breaking.
    [embed] Bad HTTP MCP endpoint returning HTML blocks discovery for 60s befor... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/36052
    [embed] fix(mcp): fail fast on non-MCP HTTP content type instead of waiting... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/36064

[2026-06-01T23:40:50.884000+00:00] kinh0. (id=1511152579810431057)
    This is a follow-up to the already merged PRs #29653, which made unmentioned Telegram group messages observable as context, and #30798, which separated observed Telegram group context from addressed user turns.
    
    I observed that unmentioned group messages with attachments were being recorded as context, but the attached files/media were not cached. This meant that if a user later mentioned the bot and referred to “the file above,” the agent could see the prior text context but not access the actual file.
    
    I opened #36957 to cache observed Telegram group media/documents and expose their local paths in the observed context, while keeping the same require-mention and chat allowlist gates.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/36957
    [attachment] image.png https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1511152580087124168/image.png?ex=6a1f6a02&is=6a1e1882&hm=7335add5f984f05540c4499eafb2345c2da831ced103c21c39d49d0766bcffcc&

[2026-06-02T08:08:27.948000+00:00] axmaiqiu (id=1511280325995794452)
    Hey <@387972437901312000> , I’d like to get your thoughts on a gateway/session workspace feature.
      My thinking is that in real Hermes usage, different sessions often handle different tasks or even different repos. For more advanced and long-running workflows, it feels important that each session can have its own workspace instead of every gateway session sharing the same global cwd.
      I opened an issue here: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37277
      And a PR here: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37275
      The PR adds a `/workspace` / `/cwd` command for gateway sessions, so a specific chat/thread/session can be bound to a project directory. After that, terminal commands, file tools, context files, checkpoints, footer/status, etc. use that session workspace.
      The change is a bit larger than a simple command because the cwd has to flow through several tool/runtime surfaces to avoid inconsistent behavior. So I’m not claiming the implementation is already perfect or final.
      When you have time, could you take a look and let me know whether you think this feature is valuable for Hermes, whether it is something that could be merged, and if there are any design changes or simplifications you’d prefer?
    [embed] [Feature]: Add session-scoped workspace binding for gateway chats ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37277
    [embed] feat(gateway): add session workspace binding by qWaitCrypto · Pull... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37275

[2026-06-02T11:19:36.223000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1511328427427369000)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37355
    
    **Problem**: tick() in cron/scheduler.py blocks the ticker thread for up to 600 seconds via as_completed(_futures, timeout=600). The gateway's cron ticker calls tick() synchronously at 60-second intervals, so when one job runs long, other jobs' next_run_at expires past the grace window — causing them to be perpetually fast-forwarded instead of running.
    [embed] fix(cron): decouple tick() dispatch from job completion #37312 by k... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37355

[2026-06-02T12:30:23.842000+00:00] techmeat (id=1511346243232862339)
    #37366 - small memory-plugin fix.
    
    Standalone memory providers are supposed to "just work" per the docs, but any provider with a relative import (`from . import config`) fails - the loader never registers the `_hermes_user_memory` parent namespace (only does it for bundled ones). Same gap breaks `cli.py` discovery.
    
    Fix registers the parent package before loading user plugins, so anyone can drop in their own memory backend with subpackages/relative imports. Hit it building Open Second Brain. Pure importlib change, +4 regression tests.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37366
    [embed] fix(memory): register parent packages for user-installed provider i... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37366

[2026-06-02T12:38:11.481000+00:00] 0xgr1mm (id=1511348204652728431)
    small macos cross-platform test fix → 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37385
    
    on macos /tmp is a symlink to /private/tmp, so file_tools' Path.resolve() turns /tmp/x into /private/tmp/x. three assertions in tests/tools/test_file_tools.py compared against the unresolved path and failed on apple silicon — linux ci doesn't catch it since /tmp isn't a symlink there. test-only, no prod code touched. open to feedback.
    [embed] test(macos): resolve /tmp symlink in file_tools path assertions by ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37385

[2026-06-02T13:00:36.053000+00:00] blackestk (id=1511353844196708425)
    Hi — I've been running a Hermes agent for about a week and built a tiered memory architecture that keeps the working context lean while persisting important information to files. Resets don't cause memory loss because the agent reads from structured markdown and SQLite on wakeup rather than relying on session context. The agent has maintained consistent persona and memory across model swaps and hard restarts. Happy to share the approach if anyone's interested in the context management side of things.

[2026-06-02T17:43:31.969000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1511425046349090859)
    **Problem**: The launchd plist shipped with KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit=false, which meant launchd only restarted the gateway on non-zero exit. When gateway run --replace triggered a clean exit (code 0), launchd did not relaunch it, leaving the gateway down indefinitely until manually restarted.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37534
    [embed] fix(launchd): use unconditional KeepAlive so gateway restarts after... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37534

[2026-06-02T23:48:29.198000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1511516889984139426)
    **Problem**:
    Anthropic enforces two independent ceilings per image:
    
    1. 5 MB encoded byte size → "image exceeds 5 MB maximum"
    2. 8000 px longest side → "image dimensions exceed max allowed size: 8000 pixels"
    Hermes only guarded #1. A tall screenshot (e.g. 1200×12000 at 0.06 MB) passes every byte check but fails the pixel check, returning a non-retryable HTTP 400 that permanently bricks the conversation thread — every subsequent turn replays the oversized image and gets rejected.
    
    **Solved**: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37727
    **Resolves**: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37677
    [embed] fix(vision): guard image pixel dimensions, not just bytes #37677 by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37727
    [embed] Oversized image (>8000px) permanently bricks a conversation thread ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37677

[2026-06-03T07:20:36.937000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1511630671968338192)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37965
    `When hermes update triggers a venv rebuild on Windows, the running python.exe inside the venv is locked by the OS, so shutil.rmtree() silently fails to fully remove the directory. The subsequent uv venv command then fails with A directory already exists, leaving the venv in a half-deleted state (pyvenv.cfg gone, python.exe still present) which bricks the CLI with ModuleNotFoundError.`
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37868
    Fixes [#37721](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37721) — stale tracked.json entries with category: "cron-output" pointing to cron control-plane files (e.g. cron/jobs.json) could cause quick() to delete the live scheduler registry.
    [embed] fix(update): add --clear flag to uv venv rebuild to prevent Windows... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37965
    [embed] fix(cron): re-validate stale cron-output entries before deletion #3... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37868
    [embed] [Bug]: disk-cleanup can still delete cron/jobs.json from stale trac... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37721

[2026-06-03T08:41:47.827000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1511651101961621545)
    <@387972437901312000>, could you please take a look at PR #32609 when you get a chance?
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/32609
    
    It fixes an important Telegram streaming formatting bug that shows up specifically on long streamed replies that split into multiple Telegram messages.
    
    The short version:
    
    - Telegram streaming is genuinely buttery smooth now, especially with the recent improvements.
    - But there is still a flaw on long multi-part streamed replies.
    - Earlier split messages sometimes show raw Markdown syntax like `##`, `**bold**`, and code fences instead of rendered formatting.
    - The final split message usually renders correctly, which makes the issue a bit sneaky.
    
    Root cause:
    
    In the current overflow path, Hermes seals an existing streamed message as the first chunk, then resets `_message_id = None`:
    
    ```py
    chunk = self._accumulated[:split_at]
    ok = await self._send_or_edit(chunk)
    self._message_id = None
    ```
    
    Because that sealed chunk is never edited again, Telegram never gets the final `MarkdownV2` formatting pass for it. Only the last chunk gets finalised, so only the last message renders properly.
    
    PR #32609 fixes that by finalising the sealed overflow chunk immediately:
    
    ```py
    ok = await self._send_or_edit(chunk, finalize=True)
    ```
    
    I verified it locally:
    
    - On current `origin/main`, a focused regression test fails because the sealed overflow edit uses `finalize=False`.
    - On PR #32609, the same regression passes.
    - Relevant suite passes on the PR head: `tests/gateway/test_stream_consumer.py` plus all `tests/gateway/test_telegram*.
    [embed] Improve media safety, logging, and Telegram formatting by ITheEqual... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/32609
    [reply to xdarkchain msg=1508822124121296957]

[2026-06-03T08:42:15.268000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1511651217057517660)

    [attachment] 2026-06-03_10-38-28.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1511651216898265148/2026-06-03_10-38-28.jpg?ex=6a213a67&is=6a1fe8e7&hm=8fe04a1008fb6246b083cc1610c6efb2838afc6f20923fe0e74ab3842eaff4cb&

[2026-06-03T08:43:20.418000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1511651490316423250)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38051
    <@387972437901312000> 
    
    this and above ones
    
    `When hermes update triggers a venv rebuild on Windows, the running python.exe inside the venv is locked by the OS, so shutil.rmtree() silently fails to fully remove the directory. The subsequent uv venv command then fails with A directory already exists, leaving the venv in a half-deleted state (pyvenv.cfg gone, python.exe still present) which bricks the CLI with ModuleNotFoundError.
    
    Fix: Adding --clear to the uv venv command makes it handle any leftover directory gracefully, recreating the venv even if the previous shutil.rmtree call was only partially successful.
    
    `
    [embed] fix(config): add --clear flag to uv venv rebuild to prevent Windows... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38051

[2026-06-03T08:52:14.283000+00:00] cyberyellowninja (id=1511653729508786306)
    Could someone explain what the Hermes subscription includes in simple terms? Thank you.

[2026-06-03T08:57:00.438000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1511654929729716265)
    The Hermes subscription is basically the Nous Portal subscription.
    
    In simple terms: it gives Hermes one account to use models and tools, instead of you having to set up separate API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Firecrawl, FAL, Browser Use, TTS, etc.
    
    What it includes:
    
    - Access to 300+ models through Nous Portal
    - Monthly credits that Hermes spends when you use those models/tools
    - Hosted tool usage on paid plans, like web search, page extraction, image generation, text-to-speech, and browser automation
    - Higher rate limits depending on the plan
    - Nous Chat access using the same subscription
    - One login / one bill instead of multiple provider accounts
    
    The plans are roughly:
    
    Free:
    - $0/mo
    - very tiny credits, mainly for trying it
    - model access / pay-as-you-go
    - no managed Tool Gateway
    
    Plus:
    - $20/mo
    - $22 monthly credits
    - $10 rollover cap
    - 300+ models
    - hosted tool usage
    
    Super:
    - $100/mo
    - $110 monthly credits
    - $50 rollover cap
    - higher limits
    
    Ultra:
    - $200/mo
    - $220 monthly credits
    - $100 rollover cap
    - highest limits
    
    The important bit: it is not “unlimited Hermes usage”. It is more like prepaid credits plus easier setup. If you run expensive models or huge agent tasks, you can burn credits quickly. If you use cheaper models and keep tasks sensible, it can go a lot further.
    
    Also, Hermes Agent itself is open source. You do not need Nous Portal to use Hermes. You can still use your own OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic, local Ollama, etc. Nous Portal is mainly the easier bundled option. And it is worth it.
    [reply to cyberyellowninja msg=1511653729508786306]

[2026-06-03T10:31:38.535000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1511678745394675853)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, mind reviewing #38108 when you get a minute?
    
    It fixes a MEDIA delivery gap that hits single-user / root gateways.
    
    When the agent generates a deliverable in its working dir, for example:
    
    ```text
    /root/work/proposal.docx
    ```
    
    and replies with:
    
    ```text
    MEDIA:<path>
    ```
    
    the gateway silently drops the attachment because `/root` is on the delivery denylist.
    
    The user gets a "here is your file" reply with nothing attached.
    
    Reproduced live on a Telegram DM. Copying the same file into:
    
    ```text
    ~/.hermes/cache/documents/
    ```
    
    delivered fine.
    
    The fix stages a safe local deliverable into the matching media cache and delivers the staged copy.
    
    It keeps the full credential / system denylist intact:
    
    * Refuses `/etc`
    * Refuses `~/.ssh`
    * Refuses `~/.hermes/.env`
    * Refuses credential filenames like `id_rsa` / `*.pem`
    * Resolves symlinks first
    * Strict mode only stages fresh files
    
    It does **not** allowlist `/root`, home, or arbitrary workdirs.
    
    Adds **21 new tests**.
    
    This builds on the diagnosable-rejection + canonical-cache-roots work from my #33251 that you shipped as #34485.
    
    That improved the logging and cache roots, but did not cover workdir deliverables. This closes that gap.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38108
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/38106
    Related attachment reports where sends appear successful but never arrive: #32644, #13356
    
    Why it matters: this is the difference between "the agent says it sent the file" and the file actually arriving, for the most common case, agent-generated docs.
    
    Thanks!
    [embed] fix(gateway): stage safe local MEDIA files before delivery by GodsB... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38108
    [embed] [Bug]: Gateway silently drops safe local MEDIA files from working d... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/38106

    Life-Harness suggests the next Hermes reliability layer should be runtime-side, not model-side: a small harness around the agent loop that validates tool calls, detects repeated failures, regulates trajectories, injects environment contracts, and turns solved failures into skill candidates.
    
    Hermes already has tools + skills + trajectories + memory. This would connect them into a feedback loop where failed trajectories become executable runtime wisdom.

[2026-06-03T15:20:18.806000+00:00] ahmetarif0x (id=1511751391876743309)
    3 Hermes Desktop bug fixes
    
    #38289 — connection-config:apply sent SIGTERM then reloaded after a hard-coded 150ms delay. If the backend took longer to die the port was still bound when the new connection started. Now waits for actual process exit with a 5s SIGKILL fallback.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38289
    
    #38291 — Profile creation discarded the API response entirely, so a silent model write failure (model_set: false) showed a success toast with no warning. Now surfaces the failure to the user.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38291
    
    #38294 — handleSaveDesc / handleAutoDescribe cleared the saving indicator unconditionally in finally. If two saves overlapped, the first to finish cleared the spinner while the second was still in-flight. Fixed with an in-flight counter.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38294
    [embed] fix(desktop): wait for backend exit before reloading on connection-... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38289

[2026-06-03T17:57:36.019000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1511790974416719892)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38397
    
    Opened a PR for the Windows hermes update brick (#37881) — the one where the venv rebuild nukes site-packages/certifi and leaves you with FileNotFoundError on every model call. Heads up: the two existing PRs (#37895, #38051) just add --clear, but that's not enough — when the gateway/desktop is running, the locked python.exe is inside the venv being rebuilt, so uv venv --clear can't delete it either and rmtree has already gutted it. Real fix is to move the venv aside atomically (os.replace) so it bails before deleting anything when it's in use, plus restore-on-failure. Tests included. Hit this in prod today on Windows 11.
    [embed] fix(uv): never gut the venv in place when rebuilding on Windows by ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38397

[2026-06-03T19:30:07.091000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1511814257300209778)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, could you please review this when you get a chance?
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/38424
    
    It fixes incomplete Mem0 recall / profile results.
    
    Hermes writes Mem0 memories with both "user_id" and "agent_id", but reads them back with only "user_id".
    
    On Mem0 Platform that means "mem0_profile", "mem0_search", and prefetch can miss agent-attributed memories.
    
    The PR changes the read filter from:
    
    {"user_id": self._user_id}
    
    to:
    
    {
        "OR": [
            {"user_id": self._user_id},
            {"user_id": self._user_id, "agent_id": self._agent_id},
        ]
    }
    
    This keeps older unattributed memories visible and restores recall for memories saved by the active agent.
    
    Verification done:
    
    - Focused Mem0 tests: "17 passed"
    - Ruff: passed
    - Local Hermes test slice: "4913 passed, 0 failed"
    - GitHub CI: all blocking checks passed
    - PR is "MERGEABLE" and "CLEAN"
    
    Thanks.
    [embed] fix(mem0): read agent-attributed memories by GodsBoy · Pull Reques... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429
    [embed] Mem0 provider read filter misses agent-attributed memories · Issue... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/38424

[2026-06-03T23:07:03.895000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1511868853733425264)
    Folded the two call-site guards from @f3rs3n''s #38511 into this PR (credited as co-author on the commit):
    
    - `rebuild_venv()` now returns `False` (and restores the moved-aside venv) if `uv venv` exits 0 without actually producing an interpreter.
    - Both `hermes update` rebuild call sites now abort instead of continuing into dependency install when `rebuild_venv()` returns `False`.
    
    So this PR now covers the Windows-safe move-aside/restore **and** the call-site abort + interpreter-exists check, with tests for each. Thanks @f3rs3n — happy to restructure however maintainers prefer.

[2026-06-03T23:11:01.311000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1511869849528438876)
    **fix(cli): restore session cwd on resume (-c).**
    
    After resume, the agent started in the launch directory instead of where the previous session left off. Added cwd persistence in `_ensure_db_session` and `_persist_session` in `run_agent.py`, a new `get_live_terminal_cwd` getter in `terminal_tool.py`, and cwd restore via `os.chdir` in `_init_agent` resume block in `cli.py`. Follows design #19242: TERMINAL_CWD is not touched.

[2026-06-04T03:25:13.209000+00:00] ahmetarif0x (id=1511933820625096755)
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38672
    
    fix(tui): re-wire sudo/secret callbacks on background and preview-restart threads
    
    prompt.background and preview.restart spawn daemon threads that call AIAgent.run_conversation() without first calling _wire_callbacks(). The sudo callback is threading.local — wiring it on the session-build thread leaves it unset on the worker thread, so any sudo command silently hangs the gateway. preview.restart explicitly enables the terminal toolset, making this a realistic scenario.
    
    Direct sibling gap of f66a929a6 — that fix covered _run_prompt_submit but missed these two handlers.
    [embed] fix(tui): re-wire sudo/secret callbacks on background and preview-r... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38672

[2026-06-04T07:09:02.989000+00:00] cryptoworlldz (id=1511990149205200896)
    Fix for this 
    
    The root cause was that `kanban --board ...` stored its board pin in process-global environment state, so concurrent in-process calls could stomp each other and write to the wrong board. The fix moves that override to a context-local scope and adds a regression test for the concurrent create case.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38325
    [embed] fix(kanban): isolate board override per concurrent call by worlldz ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38325
    [reply to cryptoworlldz msg=1511759775850041556]

[2026-06-04T14:33:54.998000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512102103605317652)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39121
    
    Problem:
    hermes mcp serve crashed with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'mcp_serve' on any standard pip-installed environment.
    Why:
    mcp_config.py line 748 had a bare top-level import — from mcp_serve import run_mcp_server. The file mcp_serve.py exists at the repo root but is not bundled into the pip wheel, so Python can't find it outside a source checkout.
    Fix:
    One-line change — replaced the bare import with the correct package namespace
    
    <@387972437901312000>
    [embed] fix(mcp): namespace `mcp_serve` import in `mcp_command` dispatcher ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39121

[2026-06-04T15:20:59.853000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1512113951905808485)
    Two small PRs up for review 
    
    **1. Remember desktop window size/position** — #39154 (fixes #39101)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39154
    The desktop window opened at a hardcoded 1220×800 every launch, ignoring wherever you left it. Now it persists size/position/maximized to a `window-state.json` in userData (same pattern as connection.json/updates.json — no new dep), with an off-screen guard so it can't reopen onto an unplugged monitor. Validation logic is split into a testable `window-state.cjs` (mirrors connection-config.cjs) with 16 unit tests. Verified live on Windows 11 + 65/65 platform tests + type-check clean.
    
    **2. Fix ui-tui type-check (spawn stdio → never)** — #39156
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39156
    `npm run type-check -w ui-tui` is red on main today — 18 errors in `execFileNoThrow.ts`. A union-typed `stdio` (`as const` tuple | `'pipe'`) collapses spawn()'s overloads to `never`; surfaced by the `@types/node@25` bump. One-line fix: type it as `StdioOptions`. Type-only, runtime unchanged → 18 errors to 0 (clean on TS 5.9 and 6.0).
    
    Both Windows-tested; happy to adjust on review
    [embed] feat(desktop): remember window size, position, and maximized state ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39154
    [embed] fix(tui): type spawn stdio as StdioOptions so ui-tui type-check pas... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39156

[2026-06-04T17:42:22.220000+00:00] .moizz. (id=1512149529531646233)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39239 - update current_turn_user_idx after preflight compression
    
    > When preflight context compression replaces the messages list with a compressed [summary, ...tail] structure, the current_turn_user_idx set earlier becomes stale. This causes memory/plugin context injection to target the wrong message, potentially corrupting tool results or causing provider API errors from malformed role alternation.
    
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39240 - rebuild FTS5 indexes independently when trigram creation fails
        
    
    > _rebuild_fts_indexes() rebuilt both messages_fts and messages_fts_trigram in one call, but was only invoked when both trigram_enabled and triggers_need_repair were true. If trigram FTS5 creation failed (e.g. unavailable tokenizer), the main messages_fts table never got its indexes rebuilt even when triggers were missing. Split into two independent methods so each table rebuilds separately.
    
    
    <@387972437901312000> pls have a look my first 2 PR's :>
    [embed] fix: update current_turn_user_idx after preflight compression by mo... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39239
    [embed] fix: rebuild FTS5 indexes independently when trigram creation fails... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39240

    > Desktop chat got permanently stuck in "busy" state after a gateway crash mid-stream — the sawAssistantPayload guard prevented the flag from clearing. Removed the guard so running=false always resets busy.
    [embed] fix(desktop): clear stuck busy flag when session crashes mid-stream... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39253

[2026-06-04T18:16:57.276000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512158232947458111)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39256
    
    Bug #1 — EventBridge._poll_once early-return guard fires too late
    PR: #39256
    
    Bug: The early-return check ran after self._sessions_json_mtime = sj_mtime was already assigned, making sj_mtime == self._sessions_json_mtime trivially True. The guard effectively became a pure state.db check. When a new conversation was opened (sessions.json changed, state.db not yet written), the EventBridge reloaded the sessions index but exited before processing any messages for the new session.
    
    Fix: Moved the early-return check to after both mtimes are read but before either cache is updated.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39259
    
    Bug #2 — Anthropic credential sync leaves stale error fields
    PR: #39259
    
    Bug: _sync_anthropic_entry_from_credentials_file and the Anthropic retry-success path only cleared 3 of the 6 error fields (last_status, last_status_at, last_error_code) when syncing fresh tokens. Every other provider sync (Codex, xAI, Nous) clears all six. The leftover last_error_reason, last_error_message, and last_error_reset_at persisted to auth.json and appeared in logs alongside an otherwise healthy credential.
    
    Fix: Added the three missing fields to both affected replace() calls
    
    <@387972437901312000>
    [embed] fix EventBridge._poll_once: move early-return guard before mtime ca... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39256
    [embed] fix: clear all error fields when syncing Anthropic credential from ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39259

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39265 - fix(desktop): auto-retry port when local gateway fails to bind
    > pickPort() had a TOCTOU race — it tested a port by opening a temporary listener, then immediately closed it. Between close and the gateway spawn, another process could grab the port, causing a cryptic boot failure. Now the full port range is iterated with automatic retry on failure.

[2026-06-04T20:58:50.733000+00:00] .moizz. (id=1512198974138875906)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39311  -  conversation_loop.py line 801 checks BOTH api_call_count < max_iterations AND budget.remaining > 0. Line 813 increments api_call_count every iteration. Line 822 consumes budget. Line 4008 refunds budget for execute_code... but api_call_count was never decremented. After 90 execute_code calls the loop hard-exits with budget still available.
    
        The compression-restart path at line 3505 already does it correctly (decrement + refund). This fix mirrors that pattern.
    
    
    
     https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39312 - when a pre-expiry OAuth token refresh fails due to a TRANSIENT network error (DNS timeout, blip), the generic except block in _refresh_entry() calls _mark_exhausted(entry, None) which applies the DEFAULT 3600s TTL. A working credential with a still-valid access token gets locked out for 1 hour.
    
        Anthropic refresh window = 120s. 1 hour lockout on a transient error = 30x amplification.
    [embed] fix: decrement api_call_count when refunding execute_code iteration... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39311
    [embed] fix: do not lock out credential for 1 hour on transient OAuth refre... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39312

[2026-06-05T02:06:21.313000+00:00] drexux (id=1512276361480503386)
    hey team, whenever you have a moment (no rush), I'd appreciate a review on two small, focused cross platform fixes. Both are tested and follow the AGENTS md rule of resolving state paths via get_hermes_home() instead of hardcoding ~/.hermes.
    
    #39458 — fix(cli): use canonical get_hermes_home() fallback in load_hermes_dotenv
    Parameterless load_hermes_dotenv() fell back to a hardcoded ~/.hermes, so on the platform-native home, installer-written keys (API keys, Bitwarden token, MCP ${VAR} refs) didn't resolve. → 8 tests pass.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39458
    
    #39461 — fix(cli): resolve dashboard auth audit log path via get_hermes_home()
    _resolve_log_path() duplicated the home logic and hardcoded ~/.hermes, so auth audit events (login/logout/WS-ticket) could land outside the active Hermes home, breaking the $HERMES_HOME/logs/dashboard-auth.log contract. → 51 tests pass.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39461
    
    Both are small and self contained, happy to address any feedback or rebase if needed. Thanks a lot
    [embed] fix(cli): use canonical get_hermes_home() fallback in load_hermes_d... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39458
    [embed] fix(cli): resolve dashboard auth audit log path via get_hermes_home... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39461

[2026-06-05T06:38:12.732000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1512344776530460742)
    ## Fix: PR #39622
    
    **Issue**: #39525 Gateway failed after I close and open My laptop and change my Wifi
    **Fix**: Added a periodic polling health check (`_polling_health_check_loop`) to the Telegram adapter that probes `bot.get_me()` every 5 minutes while polling is active. When a laptop sleeps and wakes on a different WiFi, the long-poll TCP connection silently dies without PTB's error callback firing. The health check detects this stale connection (15s probe timeout) and triggers the existing reconnection ladder, so the gateway recovers automatically without requiring a manual reload.
    **Files**: `gateway/platforms/telegram.py` (+69 lines crazy number right?)
    **PR**: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39622
    [embed] fix(gateway): add periodic polling health check to detect stale con... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39622

[2026-06-05T10:17:48.374000+00:00] coexismail (id=1512400039178272828)
    Second PR from me 🙂
    
    Bug: Some profiles rendered tool and skill category labels nearly invisible in the startup banner (#39675). Root cause was in hermes_cli/banner.py — category labels used [dim {color}] which applies Rich's dim modifier on top of an already-muted banner_dim color, causing double-dimming. On light mode and github-dark skins where banner_dim is already dark (#475569, #444444), the extra dim modifier made categories nearly invisible while tool names rendered normally.
    
    Fix: removed the redundant dim modifier from toolset and skill category lines — [dim {dim}] → [{dim}]. Two lines changed, color now renders at intended brightness across all skins.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39726
    [embed] fix(banner): remove redundant dim modifier from toolset and skill c... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39726

[2026-06-05T12:38:47.746000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1512435520356094034)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39745
    
    Fixes #39704. When Gateway Session Hygiene creates a temporary AIAgent for compression, it didn't pass the session_db parameter, causing _compress_context to skip session rotation. This meant compressed messages overwrote the original transcript in the same session, permanently deleting the originals. The fix passes session_db=self._session_db to the hygiene agent so compression can properly rotate to a new session, preserving the original messages.
    [embed] fix(gateway): pass session_db to hygiene agent for proper session r... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39745

[2026-06-05T14:07:39.081000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512457881595678971)
    Just shipped a new skill for Hermes: **dependency-audit**
    
    It scans all project dependencies for security vulnerabilities and license issues in a single command. Supports Python, Node.js, Rust, and Ruby. Auto-detects ecosystems from lock files, cross-references findings with the OSV database, outputs a CRITICAL → LOW severity table, and drops a ready-to-run upgrade script at the end.
    
    Hermes already had osv_check.py doing this for MCP packages — this skill brings the same logic to all project dependencies.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39863

[2026-06-05T14:54:18.503000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1512469623222571171)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39896
    
    The denormalized sessions.message_count column can lag behind the actual messages table after a partial write, crash, or race condition, causing session search/browse to report message_count=0 and empty preview for sessions that actually had persisted messages. This computes message_count via a correlated subquery on the messages table in list_sessions_rich and _get_session_rich_row, overriding the stale denormalized value.
    [embed] fix(state): compute message_count from messages table (#39734) by k... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39896

[2026-06-05T16:48:48.946000+00:00] _hwrd_ (id=1512498439949385749)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39946
    
    Problem: FileSyncManager.sync() bumped its rate-limit clock even when the sync failed, so the rollback's "retry next cycle" got skipped — one network blip left the remote with stale files for up to 5s on the next command. 
    
    Fix: don't advance the clock on failure.
    [embed] fix(file-sync): don't rate-limit retry after a failed sync cycle by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39946

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39914
    
    **Problem**: Non-profile cron jobs intermittently failed with wrong profile script path because _job_profile_context mutated a module-global _hermes_home that concurrent non-profile jobs could see.
    The module-global _hermes_home was shared across all threads, but profile and non-profile jobs run concurrently in separate thread pools, so a profile job's mutation bled into parallel jobs.
    Replaced the mutation with a ContextVar so each job's contextvars.copy_context() slice stays isolated.

[2026-06-05T18:07:17.259000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512518188045434880)
    Bug Fix Summary — display.show_reasoning ignored by API server
    
    Issue: #7556 | Open for: 55 days | PR: #40005
    
    What was broken
    
    The display.show_reasoning config option — which exposes the model's internal reasoning trace alongside its response — was fully implemented in the TUI gateway but completely absent from the OpenAI-compatible REST API server (gateway/platforms/api_server.py). The words show_reasoning, last_reasoning, and any related logic had zero occurrences in the file.
    
    Who was affected
    
    Every user running Hermes connected to an OpenAI-compatible client — Open WebUI, Continue.dev, any custom API integration — who had display.show_reasoning: true set in their config. Their setting was silently ignored; no reasoning output was ever returned regardless of configuration.
    
    What we fixed
    
    Four targeted changes in a single file:
    
    Config read — load_config() is now called at request time to read display.show_reasoning (fail-open: defaults to false on any error).
    History sanitisation — Incoming assistant messages are stripped of <think>…</think> blocks before being sent to the agent, preventing prior reasoning output from polluting the next turn's context.
    Non-streaming — When reasoning is available, the response is prefixed with <think>\n{reasoning}\n</think>\n\n, the format natively supported by Open WebUI's "Show Thinking" feature.
    Streaming — After all content deltas are flushed, a custom event: hermes.reasoning SSE event is emitted carrying the reasoning as JSON. Clients that don't recognise it silently ignore it per the SSE spec.
    No behaviour change for any user with show_reasoning: false (the default).
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40005
    [embed] fix(api-server): honour display.show_reasoning in /v1/chat/completi... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40005

[2026-06-05T19:44:21.819000+00:00] drexux (id=1512542618020614204)
    fix(commands): stop surfacing gateway-only /whoami in CLI help and autocomplete
    #40052
    
    /whoami reports per-platform slash-command access tiers (admin / user / unrestricted)
    — a messaging-gateway-only concept. Its handler lives in the gateway
    (gateway/run.py → _handle_whoami_command, backed by gateway/slash_access.py),
    and the docs list it only as a messaging command.
    
    But its registry entry in hermes_cli/commands.py was missing gateway_only=True,
    so it leaked into the CLI's COMMANDS / COMMANDS_BY_CATEGORY dicts and showed up in
    classic-CLI /help and autocomplete. The classic CLI has no whoami dispatch
    branch, so actually running it printed Unknown command: /whoami — the CLI advertised a
    command it doesn't support.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40052
    [embed] fix(commands): stop surfacing gateway-only /whoami in CLI help and... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40052

[2026-06-06T00:54:39.006000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512620704162648176)
    Bug Fix: Memory Review Counter Reset on Interrupted Turns
    
    _turns_since_memory was being reset to 0 the moment the nudge threshold was reached — before the turn had any chance to complete. Since the background memory review is only spawned on non-interrupted turns, any interruption (Ctrl-C, network drop, stream cancel) at that point would zero the counter without ever running the review. The next attempt would then be pushed a full interval into the future instead of retrying on the next turn. In gateway deployments with frequent interruptions this caused the memory review cadence to silently degrade.
    
    Fix: Moved the counter reset into the spawn block so it only clears when the review is actually launched. On interruption or exception the counter is preserved and the review retries on the next eligible turn. Bug was not previously reported ' found through direct code review
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40212
    [embed] fix(conversation_loop): defer _turns_since_memory reset to spawn si... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40212

[2026-06-06T04:15:48.995000+00:00] .mkos. (id=1512671329365659718)
    Neat - thanks!
    
    I think there may be a readability issue in `readableOn()`:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/apps%2Fdesktop%2Fsrc%2Fthemes%2Fcontext.tsx#L57-L71
    
    It currently uses a fixed luminance cutoff of `0.58` to choose between `#ffffff` and `#161616`:
    
    ```ts
    return luminance > 0.58 ? '#161616' : '#ffffff'
    ```
    
    This seems to be a brightness heuristic. A contrast could be a safer approach. For some built-in theme colors, such as Slate #58a6ff and Mono #9a9a9a, the heuristic chooses white text even though #161616 has much higher contrast.
    
    A more robust approach would be to compare the contrast ratios of the two candidate foreground colors and choose the better one:
    
    ```ts
    function contrastRatio(a: number, b: number): number {
      const lighter = Math.max(a, b)
      const darker = Math.min(a, b)
      return (lighter + 0.05) / (darker + 0.05)
    }
    
    function readableOn(hex: string): string {
      const rgb = hexToRgb(hex)
      if (!rgb) return '#ffffff'
    
      const bg = relativeLuminance(rgb)
      const dark = relativeLuminance([0x16, 0x16, 0x16])
      const light = 1
    
      return contrastRatio(bg, dark) >= contrastRatio(bg, light)
        ? '#161616'
        : '#ffffff'
    }
    ```
    
    This keeps the same foreground colors but selects the one with better actual contrast. This is especially noticeable in the thinking trace UI, where small text plus fade/motion effects make low contrast harder to read.
    [embed] hermes-agent/apps/desktop/src/themes/context.tsx at main · NousRes... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/apps%2Fdesktop%2Fsrc%2Fthemes%2Fcontext.tsx
    [reply to kyssta69 msg=1512669423733964965]

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/34599
    
    **Problem:** The planned-stop watcher fired a gateway shutdown for *any* `.gateway-planned-stop.json` marker on disk — including stale markers from other processes — causing a startup crash-loop. Not Windows-only; confirmed on Debian 12 too.
    **Fix:** Watcher now checks `target_pid == os.getpid()` (with cross-platform start_time fallback) before firing, so it only acts on markers targeting itself.
    Fixes #34597 · CI green · verified on Debian by imrasalghul, code-reviewed by liuhao1024. Ready for merge 🙏
    [embed] fix(gateway): only fire planned-stop watcher for markers targeting ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/34599

    **Problem**: hermes secrets bitwarden setup crashes with EOFError in non-TTY environments even when --access-token is provided.

    Added sys.stdin.isatty() checks before each interactive prompt to default to US Cloud for region and auto-select single projects (or error with --project-id guidance) in non-TTY mode.

    Problem: Images in Hermes Desktop were flashing and disappearing when clicking to open the lightbox, or when scrolling back to older messages (#39102). Root cause: MediaAttachment component was calling setSrc('') unconditionally at the start of every useEffect run, clearing the image source before the async IPC call completed. The virtualizer remounting the component on scroll made this especially visible.
    
    Fix: removed the single setSrc('') line so the previous src is preserved during async reload.

[2026-06-06T11:26:18.033000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1512779664202924122)
    Yes. Set Telegram reply mode to `off`.
    
    In `~/.hermes/config.yaml`:
    
    ```yaml
    telegram:
      reply_to_mode: "off"
    ```
    
    Then restart the gateway:
    
    ```bash
    hermes gateway restart
    ```
    
    Valid values:
    
    ```yaml
    telegram:
      reply_to_mode: "off"     # never reply-to, send standalone messages
      # reply_to_mode: "first" # default, first chunk replies to the user message
      # reply_to_mode: "all"   # every chunk replies
    ```
    
    Or via env var:
    
    ```bash
    TELEGRAM_REPLY_TO_MODE=off
    ```
    
    Important: quote `"off"` in YAML, because some YAML parsers treat bare `off` as boolean false.
    [reply to oscarbrey msg=1512727275156799538]

[2026-06-06T11:31:13.726000+00:00] brokie0928 (id=1512780904429387896)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40447
    
    Problem
    tests/hermes_cli/test_curses_arrow_keys.py and test_curses_color_compat.py fail at collection on Windows with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_curses', which aborts the entire pytest run (collection errors, not test failures).
    
    Root Cause
    Both modules do a top-level import curses. The curses module's _curses C extension is Unix-only and is not shipped with CPython on Windows, so the import raises before any test executes.
    
    Fix
    Guard both modules with pytest.skip(..., allow_module_level=True) when sys.platform == "win32", so they are skipped during collection on Windows. curses has no Windows equivalent, so a platform skip is the correct call rather than a cross-platform rewrite. Behavior on macOS/Linux/CI is unchanged.
    [embed] test(cli): skip curses tests on Windows where _curses is unavailabl... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40447

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40481
    
    PR #40481 — Logs Page Rehaul
    
    Rewrote the Logs page UI from scratch to make it actually useful:
    
    Search bar — type a query, press Enter → backend filters lines server-side. Escape or ✕ clears it.
    Highlighted matches — every search match is wrapped in a yellow <mark> tag so it stands out in dense log output.
    Error/warning banner — shows a count of errors and warnings in the current view. Disappears while searching to stay out of the way.
    Download button — exports visible lines as hermes-<file>.log, no server round-trip.
    Scroll controls — ↑↓ buttons in the card header to jump to top or bottom instantly.
    Click-to-copy — click any line while searching to copy it to clipboard.
    Footer line count — shows how many lines are visible (or how many matched).
    New backend endpoint GET /api/logs/summary — scans log files and returns error/warning counts per file.
    API client update — added search? param to getLogs() and the new getLogsSummary() method with proper TypeScript types.
    [embed] feat(logs): Logs Page rehaul - search + highlight, error summary ba... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40481

[2026-06-06T13:29:43.337000+00:00] frowtek (id=1512810724299116604)
    What does this PR do?
    AIAgent._hook_jsonable() in run_agent.py calls isinstance(value, SimpleNamespace), but SimpleNamespace was never imported — so sanitizing any hook payload that contained one raised NameError: name 'SimpleNamespace' is not defined.
    
    Bedrock, Codex-responses, and the auxiliary client build their response / message / tool_call objects as SimpleNamespace and hand the raw objects to the post_api_request hook. The hook call sites swallow exceptions (except Exception: pass), so the crash silently dropped the observability hook for those providers. Adding the missing import restores it. 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40509
    [embed] fix(agent): import SimpleNamespace for hook payload sanitization by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40509

[2026-06-06T13:47:34.523000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1512815217178841139)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40520
    
    **Problem**: CLI input locks up when lazy dependency installer fires. bare input() in tools/lazy_deps.py deadlocks when prompt_toolkit owns the terminal. Detects prompt_toolkit via get_app_or_none() and uses run_in_terminal() to release the terminal before prompting.
    [embed] fix(cli): use prompt_toolkit run_in_terminal for lazy-dep install p... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40520

[2026-06-06T14:54:20.128000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512832017904173086)
    New Skill: github-pr-reviewer
    
    Give Hermes a GitHub PR URL → it fetches the diff, reviews every changed 
    file, and posts inline comments directly to the PR — like a senior dev 
    doing a code review.
    
    What it checks:
    - Bugs — null dereference, off-by-one, race conditions
    - Security — SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, missing auth checks
    - Error handling — silent except blocks, unclosed resources
    - Performance — N+1 queries, blocking I/O in async
    - Tests — new endpoints with no test coverage
    
    Usage: "review PR https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/42"
    
    Only needs `gh` CLI authenticated. Posts comments automatically, never 
    auto-approves or requests changes without your confirmation.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40530
    [embed] feat(skills): add github-pr-reviewer - automated PR code review wit... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40530

[2026-06-06T15:06:14.126000+00:00] imperatormorsus (id=1512835012628709416)
    I have a question, I am working on creating something for me (for now) I may produce a version of it for general consumption, but I need to ask... usign AI to help with the development of said programming, is there a reason why NOT to follow programming SOP? [2026-06-06T15:07:28.548000+00:00] davidg1982 (id=1512835324777205873)
    I like this concept. I am going to incorporate this concept into my process and see if it will add value
    [reply to yap.bi msg=1512832017904173086]

[2026-06-06T15:08:06.153000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1512835482504134667)
    The AI doesn't ignore SOP intentionally  it just doesn't know yours 
    unless you tell it explicitly. 
    
    Best approach: paste your SOP/style guide directly into the system prompt 
    or at the start of the conversation. The more specific you are (naming 
    conventions, error handling patterns, folder structure, etc.), the more 
    it follows it.
    
    Also works well: show it an example of "good" code from your codebase 
    and say "follow this style."
    [reply to imperatormorsus msg=1512835012628709416]

[2026-06-06T15:08:43.773000+00:00] davidg1982 (id=1512835640293855412)
    We have a contributor.md (something to make sure AI follows), and there are tests to pass for code to be integrated into main once it is approved to be merged. Also, if you dev something for yourself that isn't part of the main, you'll have to rebase and keep that code alive. Just takes an hour or two every version to deal with. Dealing with PRs that are in main is something I am dealing with because I am running multi-agent code that is out there as a PR, but isn't in main, and I have to reconcile with that every release.
    If I am wrong, please correct me. 🙂
    [reply to imperatormorsus msg=1512835012628709416]

[2026-06-06T15:10:40.342000+00:00] imperatormorsus (id=1512836129219674283)

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40579
    
    **Problem**: The CLI input locks up completely when the lazy-dependency install prompt appears under prompt_toolkit (the TUI), because a bare input() deadlocks when prompt_toolkit owns the terminal.
    The lazy_deps.py code used a bare builtin input() for the install confirmation prompt, which cannot read keystrokes when prompt_toolkit is running.
    The fix detects whether a prompt_toolkit app is active and skips the interactive confirmation when it is, since reaching that code already means the user opted into lazy installs.

[2026-06-06T17:00:17.679000+00:00] frowtek (id=1512863716570566737)
    What does this PR do?
    When no agent is running, /queue had no canonical branch in the gateway's idle
    dispatch chain. Because queue is in GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS, it also skipped
    the unknown-command notice — so the literal /queue <prompt> text was forwarded
    verbatim to the agent.
    
    Naively enqueuing in the idle path would be worse: the FIFO drain only runs at
    the tail of a completed agent run (_dequeue_pending_event in
    _process_message_background). With no run in flight, the queued message would
    be stranded and never consumed.
    
    The fix mirrors the existing idle /steer behavior — there is no in-flight turn
    to queue behind, so strip the prefix and run the payload now as a normal user
    turn. An empty payload returns a usage hint.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40671
    [embed] fix(gateway): dispatch /queue when session is idle by Frowtek · Pu... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40671

[2026-06-06T20:30:03.047000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1512916503429906522)
    <@387972437901312000> sensei, when you have a moment, would appreciate a look at these three PRs. The latest upstream (56236b16) included related approval and config fixes: `fix(approval): close remaining prompt_toolkit deadlock vectors`, `fix(approval): wake blocked gateway approvals on session cleanup`, and `fix(code-exec): restore approval context in execute_code RPC threads + guard entry`. These PRs cover adjacent gaps in the same areas.
    
    **1. fix(approval): return approval_required instead of blocking on input() when callback is None.**
    `check_dangerous_command` and `check_all_command_guards` fell through to `prompt_dangerous_approval` when `approval_callback=None`, blocking a worker thread for up to 60s then silently denying. Added an early-return guard before the blocking path in both functions. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39976
    
    **2. fix(approval): guard _permanent_approved reads under _lock before save_permanent_allowlist.**
    All four `save_permanent_allowlist(_permanent_approved)` call sites read the set outside `_lock`, creating a TOCTOU window where a concurrent `approve_permanent()` could cause a lost update in the persisted `command_allowlist`. Wrapped all four reads in `with _lock: save_permanent_allowlist(_permanent_approved.copy())`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39865
    
    **3. fix(config): guard config caches under _CONFIG_LOCK to prevent thread-safety issues.**
    `_CONFIG_PARSE_WARNED` and `_LAST_EXPANDED_CONFIG_BY_PATH` were read and written outside `_CONFIG_LOCK` in `_warn_config_parse_failure` and `save_config`, despite `_CONFIG_LOCK` already guarding the other caches. Parallel gateway requests could create duplicate `.bak` files or lose cache entries. Wrapped both in `with _CONFIG_LOCK:`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/39715
    
    **Have a great weekend everyone!**
    <:o7:1151261546111053924>

[2026-06-07T01:42:37.891000+00:00] nftpoetrist (id=1512995166947119175)
    fix(osv_check): uvx --from=PKG install target bypasses malware scan
    
    When using uvx --from=PKG BINARY, the actual install target is in --from=PKG, not the first positional arg. Since _parse_package_from_args skips everything starting with -, the real package is never sent to OSV — only the binary name is checked.
    
    Fix mirrors the npx --package= fix from #40567: added --from and --from= handling so uvx installs are checked correctly.
    
    PR → https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40908
    [embed] fix(osv_check): honor uvx --from/--from= install target when parsin... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40908

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40983
    
    **Problem**: Gateway startup fails on macOS 26 Aqua sessions because launchctl cannot bootstrap the job under the user/<uid> domain. The user/<uid> domain was hardcoded in PR #40581, which works for Background/SSH sessions but breaks GUI login sessions that require gui/<uid>. _launchd_domain() now detects the active session manager via launchctl managername and selects gui/<uid> for Aqua sessions, user/<uid> otherwise.

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41130
    
    **Problem**: When you manually trigger a cron job with cronjob(action="run"), last_run_at stayed null and the job never executed. trigger_job() only updated next_run_at to now but never called the scheduler to actually run the job. The fix calls tick() immediately after triggering so the job executes right away and last_run_at gets populated.

[2026-06-07T11:48:11.337000+00:00] cryptoworlldz (id=1513147560465006672)
    Opened a fix for the WeChat busy-session interrupt issue.
    
    The root cause was that Weixin/WeCom plain-text follow-ups were still getting delayed in platform-level text batching even when the session was already active, so the gateway’s interrupt logic often saw them too late. The fix keeps batching for idle sessions, but bypasses it for active ones so busy-session interrupt handling can react immediately.
    
    Bug1 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40724
    Bug2 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40727
    PR https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41172
    [embed] [Bug]: WeChat platform: user message interrupt mechanism rarely tri... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40724
    [embed] [Bug]: WeChat platform: user message interrupt mechanism rarely tri... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40727
    [embed] fix(gateway): bypass WeChat text batching for active sessions by wo... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41172

[2026-06-07T11:54:18.216000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513149099266805981)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41130
    
    **Problem**: When a cron job was manually triggered via cronjob(action='run'), the tool only scheduled it for the next scheduler tick by setting next_run_at to now, but never actually executed the job. The scheduler tick may not run in all contexts like CLI sessions or cron job sessions. The fix adds a new _execute_job_now helper that calls run_job directly after triggering, advances next_run_at to prevent double-execution, saves output, delivers results, and marks the job as run with last_run_at populated.
    [embed] fix(cron): execute job immediately on manual trigger (#41037) by ky... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41130

[2026-06-07T13:39:19.475000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513175528662761604)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41306
    
    **Problem**: Telegram typing indicator stays visible for hours after a background process notification because the typing loop only relied on task.cancel() to stop. Under flood control, send_chat_action can be blocked and the CancelledError doesn't propagate fast enough to stop the loop within the 0.5s timeout. The fix sets the cooperative stop_event before cancelling, so the typing loop exits on its next polling tick within 0.25 seconds.
    [embed] fix(gateway): set stop_event before cancelling typing task (#41168)... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41306

[2026-06-07T23:25:32.500000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513323055022342165)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41486
    
    **Problem**: IMAP email fetch crashes with 'int object has no attribute decode' on iCloud because (RFC822) returns only UID metadata without the message body. Replaced with (BODY.PEEK[]) and added defensive parsing of the fetch response to handle missing body literals gracefully.
    
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41451
    
    **Problem**: Email login fails for custom domain providers like iCloud and Fastmail because IMAP/SMTP auth uses the public From address instead of the login username. Added EMAIL_LOGIN_USER env var that defaults to EMAIL_ADDRESS, so existing setups are unaffected while custom domain users can set their actual login username.
    
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41446
    
    **Problem**: Tirith security scanner crashes with SIGSEGV on every tool call when the binary is missing or corrupted, causing a 20-minute agent hang with no response delivered. Added a circuit breaker that disables tirith after 3 consecutive failures, preventing the retry loop from hanging the user.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41538
    
    **Problem**: Vite build crashes with JavaScript heap out of memory on RPi 3B+ and other low-RAM devices. Node.js default heap limit exceeds available RAM during the 2307-module Vite transform step. Added _web_build_env() that detects total RAM and caps NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size when total RAM is 2GB or less (55% of RAM, min 256MB). High-RAM machines unaffected.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41580
    
    **Problem**: hermes cron list crashes with TypeError when any job has deliver: null in jobs.json, making the entire cron list command unusable. The join(deliver) call on line 95 fails because deliver is None instead of an iterable. Fixed by defaulting null/missing deliver to ['local'] using the or fallback pattern.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41581
    
    **Problem**: WhatsApp JID targets (group @g.us, DM @s.whatsapp.net, LID @lid) were not recognized by send_message, silently falling back to home channel. Bare-digit targets without JID suffix crashed the Baileys bridge on jidDecode(). Added WhatsApp JID regex pattern and reject bare digits for WhatsApp.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41582
    
    **Problem**: hermes setup tools vision custom endpoint wizard never asked for model name, leaving AUXILIARY_VISION_MODEL empty for non-OpenAI endpoints. This caused vision to fail when using a custom base URL. Added model name prompt after API key input for custom endpoints.
    [embed] fix(email): use BODY.PEEK[] for IMAP fetch and handle missing body ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41486
    [embed] fix(email): add EMAIL_LOGIN_USER for custom domain IMAP/SMTP auth (... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41451
    [embed] fix(security): handle tirith SIGSEGV crash and disable broken binar... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41446
    [embed] fix(build): cap Node.js heap for Vite build on low-RAM devices (#41... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41538
    [embed] fix(cron): tolerate deliver: null in cron list (#41392) by kyssta-e... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41580

    Hey team, I noticed cron jobs are invisible to the agent. When a cron job delivers to a chat, the agent in that same chat has no idea what was sent. You can't follow up on scheduled outputs because the context doesn't exist.
    
    There's a PR already open that seems promising: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/37073
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/37070
    
    It introduces 3 modes: off (current behavior), auto (injects last delivery into the system prompt automatically), and button (shows an accept/dismiss inline button on Telegram).
    
    Would be great to get eyes on this! 🙏

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41647
    
    **Problem**: Line 1: hermes update breaks when the managed uv binary is corrupted, wrong architecture, or missing because resolve_uv() only checks one path. Line 2: The fallback resolver chain (venv, ~/.local/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, PATH) was removed in a refactor, leaving no recovery path. Line 3: Restored the 5-step fallback chain so resolve_uv() probes trusted locations before falling back to shutil.which.
    [embed] fix(cli): restore uv fallback resolver chain (#41534) by kyssta-exe... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41647

[2026-06-08T01:03:41.295000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513347754418634999)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41650
    
    **Problem**: Line 1: After context compression, the agent acts on stale instructions from 'Pending User Asks' and 'Remaining Work' in the summary instead of the latest user message, especially when topics overlap. Line 2: The preamble's conflict-resolution language was ambiguous (allowed using summary as background), and section names like 'Pending User Asks' sounded like active tasks the model should execute. Line 3: Strengthened the preamble to explicitly say topic overlap does NOT mean resume summary task, renamed sections to 'Historical ... (DO NOT EXECUTE)', and added explicit DO NOT ACT instructions in section descriptions.
    [embed] fix(agent): strengthen compression preamble vs stale tasks (#41607)... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41650

[2026-06-08T07:49:31.255000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513449885553135706)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41794
    
    **Problem**: Discord compact tool progress shows full fenced bash code blocks instead of short preview lines, filling chats with terminal output.
    The progress_callback in gateway/run.py was using _bash_block in both verbose and compact modes.
    Fix removes _bash_block from compact/all/new modes so only verbose mode shows fenced code blocks.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41850
    
    **Problem**: Auxiliary functions (title generation, vision, compression, session_search) fail with SSL/connection errors behind corporate proxies because they use OpenAI clients without TCP keepalive. auxiliary_client.py creates OpenAI clients without any custom httpx transport, so they use default transport without keepalive. Added a keepalive httpx transport builder mirroring run_agent's implementation, auto-injected into all OpenAI/AsyncOpenAI constructors in auxiliary_client.py.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41862
    
    **Problem**: When HERMES_UID/HERMES_GID is set to 1000, files inside /opt/data/ subdirectories remain owned by UID 10000. The targeted chown was gated on the top-level directory check, but usermod -u already fixes the top-level dir, so the subdirectory chown never ran. Now also probes the sessions subdirectory to detect missed ownership and trigger the full targeted chown.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41877
    
    **Problem**: Approval cards on Discord and Teams showed Always Allow for Tirith findings, but the backend only persists Tirith approvals for the current session, not permanently. The UI was promising broader persistence than the backend grants. Now hides Always Allow when Tirith findings are present, keeping the UI honest.
    [embed] fix(progress): skip fenced bash block in compact tool progress mode... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41794
    [embed] fix(agent): add TCP keepalive transport to auxiliary clients (#4174... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41850
    [embed] fix(docker): probe subdirectory ownership for UID remap chown (#416... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41862
    [embed] fix(gateway): hide Always Allow for Tirith session-only findings (#... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41877

[2026-06-08T09:04:48.281000+00:00] axmaiqiu (id=1513468831333482648)
    <@387972437901312000> I was trying the Hermes Windows desktop client and initially ran into repeated freezes / hangs during normal use.  While digging through the local Windows logs, I found one clear bug and one likely related desktop-specific issue.  The clear bug was in Windows PTY stdin handling: `pywinpty` expects `str`, but the current code can pass `bytes`,  which produced `bytes object cannot be converted to PyString` errors.  The likely related issue was in the desktop WebSocket disconnect path. Detached WS sessions were falling back to  stdio, but in the desktop app stdout is captured by Electron and written to `desktop.log`, not consumed as JSON-RPC.  That could dump raw JSON-RPC stream frames into the desktop log after disconnects and potentially amplify freezes/log  churn.  I tested a local patch on my Windows install, and after restarting the desktop client the issue no longer reproduced  for me. I opened a PR with the narrow fixes and regression tests. When you have time, could you take a look and see  whether it makes sense to merge? https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41953
    [embed] fix(tui): handle Windows PTY stdin and detached WS frames by qWaitC... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41953

[2026-06-08T10:20:41.296000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1513487928062513245)
    <@387972437901312000> latest gateway privacy regression + fix, details below.
    
    ## What's happening
    
    After #41215, where terminal tool calls render as native bash code blocks on markdown platforms, the messaging gateways, WhatsApp / Telegram, started posting the full terminal command into the chat as a fenced bash block before the final answer.
    
    This includes heredocs and all, in both `all` / `new` and `verbose` modes.
    
    `execute_code` and `process` leak the same way through the argument preview.
    
    Before #41215, terminal progress was a 40-character truncated preview, so this only started after that change landed.
    
    After pulling latest `main` this morning, I saw full shell commands showing up in the chat to everyone in it, including internal paths, `systemctl` restarts, `rm -rf`, and similar sensitive command details.
    
    ## Scope
    
    To be clear, the short previews are fine and are not the issue.
    
    Lines like these are useful context and stay:
    
    ```text
    search_files: "manifest.yaml"
    read_file: "/path/..."
    todo: "updating 2 task(s)"
    ```
    
    The bug is specifically raw shell command, code, and process input reaching the chat.
    
    ## The fix, PR #41950
    
    `terminal`, `execute_code`, and `process` now render as generic labels on messaging gateways:
    
    ```text
    Running a terminal command...
    Running code...
    Managing a background process...
    ```
    
    so the body never reaches the chat.
    
    Every other tool keeps its normal preview.
    
    This does not revert #41215. The native bash block is preserved behind a per-channel opt-in:
    
    ```text
    display.show_tool_args
    ```
    
    Default is off, so a trusted personal channel can still get full command detail by opting in.
    
    CLI / TUI is unchanged.
    
    Both render paths, the live progress callback and the structured stream-event renderer, go through one formatter.
    
    Tests added, CI green.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/41955
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41950
    [attachment] 2026-06-08_10-20-00.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1513487928280485958/2026-06-08_10-20-00.jpg?ex=6a27e8f9&is=6a269779&hm=64b6b2f8a4010e7a8a501f3db63fe94679728124a72fc17d492fe9cbf08ed6e2&
    [embed] Gateway tool progress leaks raw shell commands and code bodies to m... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/41955
    [embed] fix(gateway): sanitise messaging tool progress so chats never recei... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/41950

    Hey  👋 I have 3 open PRs that could use a review. All are focused bug fixes with narrow scope, clean commit history, and passing tests:
    
    1. fix(desktop): read composer text from DOM on Enter submit to avoid stale state — #38968
    Fixes #38923 — First Enter press doesn’t submit in Desktop composer. Root cause: submitDraft() reads stale draft React state before onInput commits. Fix: read from DOM via composerPlainText(). Already approved by @tonydwb. (+1/-1, single file)
    
    2. fix(agent): restore global model.context_length override on /model switch — #41010
    Fixes #40979 — /model switch silently drops the global model.context_length override from config.yaml. Root cause: switch_model() clears _config_context_length but never restores it from config. Fix: re-read global override after per-model custom_providers. (+12/-0, single file)
    
    3. fix(tui): return error when model switch is blocked during run — #41663
    Fixes #40795 — Switching models via Desktop picker while a background agent is running silently fails, UI briefly shows wrong model then reverts. Root cause: _mirror_slash_side_effects returns _ok with warning, Desktop ignores it. Fix: return _err(4009) so Desktop shows error notification. (+9/-0, single file)
