[2026-06-14T07:07:36.154000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515613663648813188)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46016
    
    **Problem**: Upstream proxy sends non-contiguous content_block indices in Anthropic SSE stream, causing IndexError in SDK accumulate_event. The error was not classified as a stream-parse error so the retry loop spun on it. Added try/except to catch IndexError and re-raise as a recognized ValueError, plus updated _is_provider_stream_parse_error to detect the new pattern.

[2026-06-14T07:10:29.059000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515614388864815104)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46017
    
    **Problem**: Dashboard crashes on Chinese Windows with UnicodeDecodeError because Path.read_text() uses GBK instead of UTF-8. The index.html and CSS files contain non-ASCII characters that GBK cannot decode. Fixed by explicitly passing encoding='utf-8' to both read_text() calls.

[2026-06-14T07:15:50.759000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515615738172280832)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46020
    
    **Problem**: Profile create auto-starts a resident gateway in Docker s6 containers, creating zombie processes for worker profiles that share the bot token. These zombies lose the token lock, persist gateway_state: running, and get auto-resurrected on container restart. Fixed by adding start_now parameter to register_profile_gateway() and passing start_now=False from profile creation.

[2026-06-14T07:51:04.914000+00:00] flownium (id=1515624605581180928)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46031
    [embed] fix: configure skill description truncation by itsflownium · Pull ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46031

[2026-06-14T08:58:27.806000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515641562699268276)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46016
    
    **Problem**: When a malformed Anthropic stream crashes get_final_message() with IndexError, the partial stream recovery stub lacked the content attribute that AnthropicTransport.validate_response() checks, causing the conversation loop to retry the same unrecoverable error up to 10 times.
    The stub was OpenAI-shaped (choices[].message.content) but Anthropic validation expects a top-level content list of content blocks.
    Added a minimal content list to the stub when in anthropic_messages mode so validation passes and the recovered text is preserved.
    [embed] fix(streaming): catch IndexError from malformed upstream streams (#... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46016

[2026-06-14T08:58:37.894000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515641605011411006)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46020
    
    **Problem**: In Docker (s6-overlay), hermes profile create immediately started a resident gateway for the new profile. For profiles sharing the main bot token (e.g. Kanban workers), the gateway could never connect, persisted gateway_state as running, and was auto-resurrected on every container restart.
    register_profile_gateway() created the s6 service directory and triggered rescan without a down marker, so s6 started the service unconditionally.
    Added a start_now parameter to register_profile_gateway and passed start_now=False from profile create, writing an s6 down marker so the service stays stopped until explicitly started.
    [embed] fix(profiles): register s6 gateway without auto-starting (#45963) b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46020

[2026-06-14T08:58:44.950000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515641634606288916)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46017
    
    **Problem**: On Chinese Windows with GBK locale, the dashboard failed to start because Path.read_text() defaulted to GBK encoding when reading index.html and CSS files containing UTF-8 characters.
    Two read_text() calls in the SPA serving endpoints lacked explicit encoding, inheriting the system's GBK default on Chinese Windows.
    Added encoding='utf-8' to both read_text() calls so the dashboard works correctly regardless of system locale.
    [embed] fix(web_server): use UTF-8 encoding for dashboard reads on Windows ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46017

[2026-06-14T09:14:28.134000+00:00] flownium (id=1515645590606970950)

    [embed] fix: keep desktop notifications background by itsflownium · Pull R... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46063

[2026-06-14T09:34:58.854000+00:00] teknium (id=1515650752620658799)
    ```    Real bug, overstated framing.
        
        What I confirmed:
        
        ◆ The core claim is true for subprocesses, not the Hermes Python process itself.
          - hermes_constants.py:285 says get_subprocess_home() returns {HERMES_HOME}/home when it exists.
          - tools/environments/local.py:230 and tools/environments/local.py:390 inject that path as HOME for terminal/background subprocesses.
          - tools/code_execution_tool.py:1264 does the same for execute_code.
          - agent/copilot_acp_client.py:71 does the same for ACP children.
        
        ◆ This is intentional profile isolation from #4426 / PR #7357.
          - New profiles create a home/ dir: hermes_cli/profiles.py:47.
          - Tests explicitly assert named profiles get different subprocess homes: tests/test_subprocess_home_isolation.py:45.
          - Tests also assert the parent Python process HOME stays unchanged: tests/test_subprocess_home_isolation.py:260.
        
        ◆ Live repro from current code:
          - Parent process: HOME=/real/home/user, Path.home=/real/home/user
          - Active profile: /tmp/.../.hermes/profiles/worker
          - Terminal subprocess env: HOME=/tmp/.../.hermes/profiles/worker/home
          - Background subprocess env: same profile home
        
        So “Hermes corrupts sacred $HOME” is imprecise. It does not mutate global $HOME in the running Python process. It deliberately rewrites $HOME for agent-spawned subprocesses when profile isolation is active.
        
        But the user-facing failure is real:
          - ~/.hermes/scripts/... resolves under <profile>/home/.hermes/..., not real ~/.hermes/...
          - ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh, ~/.config, ~/.azure, ~/Library/Keychains, Claude/Codex auth, etc. can disappear from subprocesses
          - Related open issues confirm it: #25114, #36144, #29015
          - #29015 has a concrete macOS Claude Code auth repro caused by the same local terminal env override.
        
        Scope: not “all users of shells on all OSes.” It hits users who:
          1. use named profiles with {HERMES_HOME}/home present, and
          2. run external CLIs/scripts via Hermes that expect real-user ~.
        
        That is common enough for multi-profile coding/kanban setups. P2 seems fair; P1 language is exaggerated unless we consider Claude/Codex delegation auth a mainline flow.
        
        Existing fix proposal:
          - PR #25151 adds HERMES_REAL_HOME to subprocess envs.
          - That helps scripts find the real home, but does not fix opaque CLIs that only honor $HOME like claude, az, gh, ssh, etc.
          - So it’s a partial mitigation, not a full fix.
        
        My recommendation:
          - Don’t remove per-profile HOME isolation wholesale. That would regress the original design: per-profile git/ssh/gh/npm state and Docker persistence.
          - Salvage the problem as a policy/config design:
            - expose real home to subprocesses (HERMES_REAL_HOME) for scripts/helpers,
            - add docs/tooling guidance,
            - add targeted escape hatches for known shared-auth CLIs, likely config-driven rather than global,
            - for macOS, consider symlinking ~/Library/Keychains into profile home when enabled.
          - Close/merge #25151 only if we label it as partial and follow up for CLI-auth passthrough. Otherwise it will look like we “fixed” the issue while Claude/Azure/GitHub auth remains broken.```

[2026-06-14T10:12:19.190000+00:00] maxfreedompollard (id=1515660149271036005)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46067
    
    When I saw OpenRouter's post about Fusion being as good as Fable at a lower cost, I checked it out and was impressed.  I noticed a bug in Hermes while trying to add multi-turn capabilities for Fusion, so here is my PR to fix the bug, and add native multi-turn Fusion support on Hermes. 
    
    OpenRouter's post btw: https://x.com/OpenRouter/status/2065856853989270011
    
    Long description (from PR):
    
    Hermes is currently the #1 app on OpenRouter by token usage (18.2T tokens, ~3.5x the next app; #1 in Most Popular, Trending, and Top Coding Agents as of 2026-06-14). OpenRouter is the largest single surface Hermes runs on, so a fix here reaches a very large share of real usage - both the silent pareto-code regression that affects users today and the new Fusion option.
    
    Fusion itself is a genuinely new capability for that audience. OpenRouter exposes multi-model Fusion deliberation through its API (the openrouter/fusion alias / openrouter:fusion server tool); single-shot use is straightforward, but multi-turn Fusion requires re-sending openrouter/fusion with full history every turn - which is exactly what Hermes' agent loop already does. Wiring Fusion in therefore gives Hermes users multi-turn Fusion deliberation through their existing OpenRouter connection. Capacity for multi-turn Fusion has only recently been added to the API only - the OpenRouter website currently only has single-turn Fusion, so this adds a brand new capability to both OpenRouter users and Hermes Agent users.
    [embed] fix(openrouter): surface router models in the picker and add Fusion... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46067
    [embed]  https://twitter.com/OpenRouter/status/2065856853989270011

[2026-06-14T10:37:01.921000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515666368295338104)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46091
    
    **Problem**: The /model command showed the config default model even when a fallback model was actively handling requests after a provider failure. The /model handler read model info from config and session overrides but never checked the cached agent's live model attribute. Added a check that reads the actual model from the running/cached agent and displays it when it differs from the config value.
    [embed] fix(gateway): /model shows active fallback instead of stale config ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46091

[2026-06-14T10:44:12.862000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515668175792898130)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46095
    
    **Problem**: When another process like hermes dashboard writes to the same session DB, the gateway's cached agent keeps stale in-memory transcript and replies with outdated context. The agent cache now stores the session message_count and checks it on reuse. If the count changed (another process wrote rows), the cache entry is discarded so a fresh agent reads the updated transcript from disk.
    [embed] fix(gateway): invalidate agent cache on cross-process session write... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46095

[2026-06-14T18:17:10.386000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1515782166590525611)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, wanted to share where we landed with Brave Search Pro.
    
    We ended up going the plugin route for our own Hermes installs, and for the other projects and clients where we run Hermes Agent:
    
    https://github.com/GodsBoy/hermes-brave-search-pro
    
    Hermes already has native Brave Free support, which is great. Our gap was specifically the paid Brave Search API surface: grounding context, richer web results, images, news, videos, discussions, suggestions, raw/debug modes, and a dedicated `brave_search` tool while keeping normal `web_search` stable.
    
    We still have the upstream PR open here:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/26286
    
    Until that PR, or something similar, lands in core, the plugin lets us run Brave Search Pro cleanly without maintaining local Hermes patches. It keeps `web_search` as the standard Hermes contract, routes discovery through Brave Pro, keeps extraction separate with Tavily, and exposes the richer Brave-specific modes only when explicitly needed.
    
    This actually made us appreciate the Hermes plugin architecture more. It gives us a clean opt-in path for a paid provider without disrupting users who are happy with the native free Brave provider. Thought I’d share in case it is useful as a plugin example or for thinking through how Brave Pro should eventually fit upstream.
    [attachment] brave-hermes-hero.png https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1515782166905356429/brave-hermes-hero.png?ex=6a3041a6&is=6a2ef026&hm=2afc60002fe72ae9a84d10ff4a189683a3cbe8665b40d8a9e01e5ce67a78ba57&

[2026-06-14T19:05:52.697000+00:00] wojtek.majnkraft (id=1515794423651373096)
    Hey everyone, wanted to share something I've been building.
    
    I started using Hermes Agent recently and the thing that bugged me most was that I couldn't really inspect what my agent was "remembering." Memory was either a black box or it disappeared when the session ended.
    
    So I built hermes-okf — a memory system that stores everything as plain markdown files on disk. Every decision, tool call, plan step... you can literally cat the file and read it. No database, no vector store, just .md + YAML.
    
    I based it on Google's Open Knowledge Format (OKF) which is basically a spec for structured knowledge as markdown files. It gives you:
    
    Persistent memory across sessions (agent restarts, you don't lose anything)
    Knowledge graph from markdown links (no RDF, no Cypher — just [[links]])
    Two-memory model: hot buffer in memory + cold archive on disk, auto-flushes
    Crash recovery  snapshot and restore from any point
    Optional RAG via ChromaDB if you want semantic search
    Full CLI to inspect the bundle from terminal
    v0.3.0 just dropped on PyPI. It has a universal HermesOKFProvider that works with any Hermes agent, not just one specific setup. 
    
    I'm looking for feedback from people actually building with Hermes. What's missing? What's annoying? Should I add async I/O? Multi-agent bundle merging? Git-backed history? I'd love to hear from people who have run into the same memory issues I did.
    
    🔗 GitHub: https://github.com/EliaszDev/hermes-okf 📦 PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/hermes-okf/ 📖 Wiki: https://github.com/EliaszDev/hermes-okf/wiki
    
    If you try it out, drop me an issue or DM me. I'd genuinely love to know if this is useful or if I'm solving a problem no one else has.
    It's like Obisidian with Hermes but better, built into the agent core

[2026-06-15T04:07:02.574000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515930612186484897)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46407
    
    **Problem**: Matrix send_message media path was creating a new adapter per message, exhausting recipient one-time keys under burst sends.
    Each fresh MatrixAdapter does full E2EE init (login + olm/megolm key share), consuming OTKs that can't replenish fast enough, causing silent message drops.
    Cached the standalone adapter by (homeserver, token) and reuse it across calls, only recreating on error or config change.

[2026-06-15T04:09:34.853000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515931250890899526)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46452
    
    **Problem**: Desktop installer fails on Windows with npm exit code 1 at the desktop stage.
    @icons-pack/react-simple-icons@^13.13.0 declares engine node>=24, but Hermes only needs ^20, so npm hard-fails on Node 22.
    Added --ignore-engines flag to npm ci and npm install in the desktop build stage.

[2026-06-15T04:11:59.692000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1515931858389700712)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46315
    
    **Problem**: Named profile gateway units showed permanent outdated warning after every restart or update.
    shutil.which('node') resolved differently depending on whether gateway or CLI ran, producing different PATH entries in the unit.
    Removed the environment-dependent shutil.which block; _build_service_path_dirs() already handles node path deterministically.

[2026-06-15T08:35:05.425000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1515998068552765600)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, could you please review this Hermes PR when you get a gap?
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40341
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40287
    
    ## Short version
    
    `hermes update` can currently remove packages that are only declared as plugin `pip_dependencies`, because they are not in the core `pyproject.toml` / lockfile.
    
    We hit this with Mem0:
    
    * `mem0ai` disappeared after update
    * Memory silently failed for days
    * `hermes memory setup` had to reinstall it
    
    This PR looks like the right update-path fix.
    
    After the core dependency refresh, it scans active plugins, including the selected `memory.provider`, and reinstalls missing plugin dependencies.
    
    ## Why it matters
    
    It protects Hindsight, Mem0, and other plugin-backed features from silently breaking after normal updates.
    
    I also left a Mem0-specific evidence comment here:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40341#issuecomment-4705991258
    
    Thank you.
    [embed] fix(update): reinstall active plugin dependencies after update (#40... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/40341
    [embed] hermes update removes plugin pip_dependencies (e.g., hindsight-clie... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/40287

[2026-06-15T10:09:09.975000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1516021743511273564)
    Hey <@387972437901312000>, would appreciate a review on a small Telegram gateway fix when you get a sec:
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46569
    Issue: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/46568
    
    ## Why it is needed
    
    Ever since rich messages got turned on via `telegram.extra.rich_messages`, the Bot API 10.1 `sendRichMessage` path from #44829, replies lost their context.
    
    When you reply to a bot message that went out as a rich message, Telegram gives the reply pointer but leaves `reply_to_message.text` / `.caption` empty.
    
    Hermes then drops the `[Replying to: "..."]` injection, and the agent has no idea what you are replying to.
    
    This is most obvious when replying to a cron announcement.
    
    It worked fine before rich messages because the old `sendMessage` path always carried a real text field that round-tripped.
    
    ## Fix
    
    A small bounded, TTL-expiring outbound cache on the adapter keyed by:
    
    ```text
    (chat_id, message_id)
    ```
    
    It is populated on both send paths, with a fallback in `_build_message_event` that only fires when there is no native quote and no text / caption.
    
    This restores parity with legacy replies, so there is no new stale-context surface. It stays clean versus #26714.
    
    The cache is in-memory only and capped three ways:
    
    * 1024 entries
    * 2000 chars
    * 48h TTL
    
    Cron gets this for free since delivery already routes through `adapter.send`.
    
    ## Validation
    
    Green across the board on the first run:
    
    * 6 test shards
    * Both nix checks
    * Both builds
    * e2e
    * check-attribution
    * ruff + ty
    
    Also included:
    
    * 21 new tests
    * RED / GREEN verified
    * Two minor P3 follow-ups noted in the PR body
    
    Happy to split or reshape if you would prefer something narrower.
    [embed] fix(telegram): restore reply context for replies to rich messages b... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46569
    [embed] [Bug]: Telegram rich cron announcements lose reply context because ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/46568

    [embed] fix: approve listed pairing requests by itsflownium · Pull Request... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46584

[2026-06-15T12:01:56.980000+00:00] scottermonkey (id=1516050126387544095)
    Help me understand the way Hermes agents/profiles load skill front-matter (or entire skills?) when an agent/profile initializes in the context of me working with you to maximize for each profile which skills are visible and made use of at what times. For example, I thought it would help to create a sort of index of skills I want to be sure the profile was aware of them via a section in the AGENTS.md file (see example below) for each profile but that has worked inconsistently. [2026-06-15T19:29:45.025000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1516162819135901779)
    I hope you have time to look into this. I think it's an important bug <@387972437901312000> 
    
    🔧 PR #46843 — fix(tools): resolve raw-task-id cwd overrides in code_execution_tool
    
    code_execution_tool._get_or_create_env() was the one call site that never got migrated to the resolve_task_overrides() helper added in #46552. Result: a CWD override registered under a raw session id (by the ACP/Desktop adapter) got silently dropped once it collapsed to "default" — so execute_code spun up its sandbox with the wrong cwd (terminal/file tools were already fine, only execute_code was affected).
    
    ✅ 2-line fix + regression tests (fail before the fix, pass after)
    🔗 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46843
    [embed] fix(tools): resolve raw-task-id cwd overrides in code_execution_too... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46843

[2026-06-15T19:54:28.732000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1516169042254233872)
    PR #46848 — Fix gateway runtime detection on Fly.io Firecracker VMs
    Gateway status reporting fell through to "manual process" on Fly-hosted s6-supervised gateways, because the check relied on is_container() (Docker/Podman/lxc only — false on Firecracker microVMs). Now gates on detect_service_manager() == "s6" instead, so Fly deployments correctly report s6 (container supervisor) with accurate service status. Added 3 new tests, all 47 in the file pass.
    🔗 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46848
    
    PR #46854 — Add IPv4 fallback for IMAP connections (IPv6 hang fix)
    #46084 added an IPv4-only fallback for SMTP when IPv6 is unreachable, but the two IMAP4_SSL connections (connect() and _fetch_new_messages()) had no equivalent — they could hang until socket timeout on IPv6-broken networks. Added _IPv4IMAP4_SSL + _connect_imap() mirroring the existing SMTP fix, applied at both call sites. 4 new tests added, full email suite (72 tests) passes.
    🔗 https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46854
    [embed] fix(gateway): gate s6 runtime snapshot on detect_service_manager, n... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46848
    [embed] fix(gateway): add IPv4 fallback for IMAP connections on unreachable... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46854
    [reply to srojk34 msg=1516162819135901779]

[2026-06-15T21:22:01.701000+00:00] sworntech (id=1516191074802991104)
    **Small Hermes Docker docs PR**:
    
    **Problem**: Gateway/server deployments can run unattended, so warning-only tool-loop guardrails may not be enough if an agent gets stuck in repeated tool calls.
    
    **Fix**: Added a Docker/gateway operator note explaining that `tool_loop_guardrails.hard_stop_enabled` defaults to `false`, why that is reasonable for watched CLI/TUI sessions, and how operators can explicitly enable hard stops for unattended deployments.
    
    **Impact**: Docs-only change. No runtime behavior changed.
    
    References #46708
    
    PR:
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46873

[2026-06-16T07:52:05.690000+00:00] sellnotbuy (id=1516349636225536182)
    Hey, I opened a PR for an ntfy delivery bug I found while checking Hermes routing consistency:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47102
    
    The issue is that `send_message` already handles explicit ntfy topics correctly, but cron live delivery and `DeliveryRouter` could still publish to the configured default topic instead of the resolved target topic.
    
    That means a cron alert or scheduled notification could look successfully delivered while actually going to the wrong ntfy topic.
    
    The PR aligns cron live delivery and `DeliveryRouter` with the existing `send_message` behavior, and adds regression tests for the affected paths.
    
    CI is currently waiting for maintainer approval to run workflows on the fork PR, but I listed the local validation in the PR description.
    [embed] fix(ntfy): honor live delivery topics by Kxazar · Pull Request #47... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47102

[2026-06-16T08:35:57.878000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1516360676422058036)

    **Problem**: Custom providers defined in config.yaml are hidden in the desktop model picker with 0 models.

    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47141
    
    **Problem**: Agent cannot write to its own ~/.hermes/.env file, getting 'Write denied: protected system/credential file' error.
    The hermes .env paths were in the write deny list alongside SSH keys and system files.
    Removed ~/.hermes/.env from the deny list so the agent can manage its own environment variables.

[2026-06-16T09:19:39.852000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1516371673778094081)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47144
    
    **Problem**: On k8s/k3s with containerd runtime and cgroup v2, is_container() returns False because the cgroup file has no docker/podman markers.
    Added detection for containerd and kubepods markers in /proc/1/cgroup so is_container() returns True on these systems.
    This prevents detect_service_manager() from returning 'none' which caused gateway supervision to be silently disabled and containers to crash-loop.

[2026-06-16T13:42:25.451000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1516437799492911114)
    #47242 — TUI gateway doesn't interrupt async delegations on shutdown
    CLI and gateway both call interrupt_all() on exit to stop background subagents. TUI skipped this entirely, leaving daemon threads burning tokens with no one listening. 5-line fix.
    → https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47242
    
    #47269 — Standalone email send missing port 465 support and IPv4 fallback
    _send_email() in tools/send_message_tool.py only did SMTP+STARTTLS — providers requiring implicit TLS on port 465 always failed. Also no IPv4 fallback, so dual-stack hosts with unreachable IPv6 timed out. The gateway had both fixes (_IPv4SMTP/_IPv4SMTP_SSL) but they were never ported to the standalone path. Added _smtp_connect() helper covering both.
    → https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47269
    [embed] fix(tui_gateway): interrupt async delegations on TUI shutdown by sr... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47242
    [embed] fix(tools): add IPv4 fallback and port-465 support to standalone em... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47269

[2026-06-16T16:57:39.693000+00:00] dskwe. (id=1516486932584988854)
    Ran into an annoying bug where Hermes rejected my `glm-5.2` model because it wasn't in Z.AI's live `/models` listing — even though it's right there in our own `_PROVIDER_MODELS` catalog 😅
    
    Opened a PR to fix this CLI validator edge case: [#47209](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47209)
    
    The validator never consulted the catalog when the API was reachable — it now checks `_PROVIDER_MODELS` before rejecting. Models in the catalog get accepted with a warning; unknown models still get rejected, so the fake-model guard stays intact.
    
    Best of both worlds: you can use models the catalog knows about but the API hasn't listed yet, while `claude-nonexistent`-style typos are still blocked. 
    
    All tests are green. Would love some eyes or a review on this! 🙌

[2026-06-16T17:50:44.500000+00:00] reverendissimo00 (id=1516500290633994432)
    https://github.com/Reverendissimo/ESP32-Voice
    
      ESP32-S3-BOX-3 voice terminal: firmware plus a Python bridge backend.
    
      • firmware/ — ESP-IDF app for the BOX-3 + sensor dock. Captures speech (VAD), streams audio to a
        server, plays TTS back over HTTP, drives the LVGL display, reads sensors/radar, handles IR,
        config over NVS, OTA updates, and a USB serial CLI.
      • tools/hermes_voice_bridge/ — Backend that ties it together: ASR, Hermes agent (/v1/responses),
        Chatterbox TTS, ESP playback streaming, multi-device admin UI, and a versioned firmware catalog
         for OTA.
      • tools/ — Helper scripts (flash, smoke tests, WAV playback).
      • Docs — Architecture, API contract, and project layout markdown files at the repo root.
    
      The device is the hands/ears/mouth; the bridge is the brain.

[2026-06-16T19:02:00.405000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1516518225079304383)
    PR #47412 — fix(compressor): strip _multimodal tool-result images after compress()
    
    browser_vision and computer_use return screenshots as _multimodal envelopes (base64, can be several MB each). The compressor was only stripping user-attached images, so these tool-result screenshots survived every compression pass and kept inflating the request payload — causing HTTP 413 errors even after 101 → 13 message compression, because providers limit byte size, not token count.
    
    Fix: added _strip_all_multimodal_tool_results() called inside compress() after the message list is assembled. Replaces every _multimodal envelope with its text_summary. Safe to strip all of them (including the most recent) because compression only runs between turns — by the time it triggers, the model has already seen and responded to every screenshot.
    
    6 new tests, all 102 pass. Fixes #47339.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47412
    [embed] fix(compressor): strip _multimodal tool-result images after compres... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47412

[2026-06-16T20:00:56.545000+00:00] innadedova (id=1516533056725323787)
    Hi! Contributing for the first time, so would appreciate the feedback! 🤗 
    I connected Gemini as a provider and tried using the Gemma free models for auxiliary tasks, and ran into a bug on the native Gemini path. 
    
    While digging into it, I found this sits in the same general Gemma-on-Gemini area as related issue #27121, but the failure here turned out to be different: when Hermes didn’t explicitly set max_tokens, it was still synthesizing generationConfig.maxOutputTokens, and Gemma rejects that shape unless the cap was actually requested.
    
    Gemma being hidden from the normal Gemini model picker seems intentional, which makes sense given the less steady runtime on Google’s side. But if you run them non-concurrently they’re still pretty attractive for auxiliary work because the limits are generous, and they’re useful for things like vision too.
    So this PR fixes that path by omitting the synthetic maxOutputTokens only for native Gemini Gemma requests when no explicit cap was asked for, while still preserving explicit caps and leaving the generic OpenAI-compatible / Anthropic-compatible routing alone. I also included a small monitor fix for same-order outputs like [{ "score": 10 }] when the model omits index, because that showed up in the same Gemma flow while validating auxiliary tasks.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47426
    [embed] fix(gemini): omit synthetic maxOutputTokens for Gemma auxiliary cal... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47426

[2026-06-16T20:25:36.214000+00:00] aialliai (id=1516539262907191347)
    <@417886593937047552> Can confirm that I had this problem on an outdated version of TG on my mac(barely use TG on mac)...updated and voila. So maybe in a few weeks it could be the default 🙂

[2026-06-16T20:44:02.478000+00:00] kntlbdak (id=1516543902914707507)
    Hey, flagging a persistent issue I hit with profile switching on Win 10 Desktop 
    
    Switching the profile dropdown in the GUI to any non-default profile mostly just shows "Gateway Disconnected", kills input, or crashes the backend. desktop.log shows exit code 3221225477 (0xC0000005) pretty often.
    
    I dug through the logs and cleaned up my side,  killed duplicate Python processes from leftover automation scripts + old Scheduled Tasks, made sure dependencies like python-telegram-bot are installed. The startup issues got better but the GUI profile switch still randomly crashes even with everything clean.
    
    Feels like a race condition in the Electron launcher when it spawns non-default profile backends, since the access violation happens at process init. Not user config.
    
    Workaround that works 100%: keep GUI on default, and for other profiles just do hermes profile use <name> && hermes from terminal. Loads the right model every time, no crashes.
    
    Anyone else hitting the 3221225477 on profile switch? Or is there a fix in nightly builds I haven't caught yet?

[2026-06-17T01:30:44.362000+00:00] bsquared0pen (id=1516616052845580480)
    DEMO VIDEO: https://streamable.com/qmqeei
    
    PR #46971: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46971
    
    Concept inspiration: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows
    
    Greetings Community! Humbly asking for a maintainer's feedback and test my PR for `Dynamic Workflows` which is a take on the new feature that Claude Code released a few weeks ago. If you use Claude Code as well as Hermes Agent, you know what this is. I find this feature to be incredibly valuable and a great way to make ambitious long-running tasks work ... ones that require many subagent sessions for successful completion. I've especially found it useful for things like literature search where your queries require multi-hop retrieval to do a systematic review, or for understanding or troubleshooting behaviors of an external system via its APIs.
    
    For the most part, Hermes provides most of the primitives for this feature, but implementing dynamic workflows as a skill is too brittle. To reliably get a long-running workflow to complete requires a DAG and deterministic dispatching and reconciliation. Hence why this PR implements as a tool.
    [attachment] 608911000-eb110977-96ab-4174-9b5a-f5907ac199da.png https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1491258766648283238/1516616053344436365/608911000-eb110977-96ab-4174-9b5a-f5907ac199da.png?ex=6a334a44&is=6a31f8c4&hm=3fb6201663aa26dbf9222d21ff953083c1c2e380aed70f3ee3360d696ec18daf&
    [embed] Watch Dynamic Workflows Hermes Agent Demo | Streamable https://streamable.com/qmqeei
    [embed] FEAT: DYNAMIC WORKFLOWS, native workflow coordination by bbopen · ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/46971
    [embed] Orchestrate subagents at scale with dynamic workflows - Claude Code... https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows

    Third and final one for tonight:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47648
    
    Pull request [#47648](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47648) fixes a bug where `hermes curator restore <name>` could silently pull an unrelated archived skill out of `.archive/` and rename it, because the fallback match was a bare `startswith("<name>-")` that grabbed sibling skills like `git-helpers` when you asked for `git`. The fix tightens that fallback to only accept the exact 14 digit timestamp suffix `archive_skill` writes on a real name collision, so siblings no longer match and the requested skill either restores correctly or returns "not found".

[2026-06-17T09:15:46.472000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1516733082776899764)
    PR #47732 — fix(agent): unwrap pre-wrapped tool schemas from context engines and memory providers <@387972437901312000> 
    
    Context engines and memory providers can return tool schemas already in OpenAI's {"type":"function","function":{...}} form. Every consumer site was blindly wrapping them a second time, producing a nameless tool entry. Strict providers like DeepSeek reject the entire request with HTTP 400 (tools[N].function: missing field name) — one bad schema silently kills all model calls for the session.
    
    Fixed in 4 locations across agent_init.py and memory_manager.py. Also added a guard to skip any schema that still has no name after unwrapping, rather than appending it as dead weight. 5 new regression tests, 96/96 passing.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47732
    [embed] fix(agent): unwrap pre-wrapped tool schemas from context engines an... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47732

[2026-06-17T13:36:37.566000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1516798728223199454)
    <@387972437901312000> small but high-impact gateway bug, with fixes already pending but still unmerged:
    
    Every image sent to a Telegram forum topic bound to a session is silently dropped before the model call.
    
    The agent replies as if no image arrived. The same image in a private DM works fine.
    
    Root cause:
    
    `_apply_persist_user_message_override` in `run_agent.py` collapses the multimodal `[text, image_url]` user turn into a plain text string, the clean persist override, at turn start via `_persist_session`, before the API request is built.
    
    Bound topics set that override. DMs do not.
    
    That matches the exact split:
    
    * DM works
    * Telegram bound topic fails
    
    Tracked in #44242, which was filed as ACP-only, but this hits Telegram bound topics just as hard, for every provider:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/44242
    
    Two one-line fixes are already up:
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44247
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44249
    
    I traced the Telegram repro end to end with per-stage logging and confirmed the drop is that exact line:
    
    * Image is consumed and attached inline
    * Message enters as a list with the image
    * Then it becomes a plain string before the API call
    * No message-repair path is involved
    
    Flagging the Telegram impact because it is broad and silent: every image sent to a bound topic, for every provider, regardless of vision support.
    
    Patching the line locally fixed it immediately. The agent then described the image in the bound topic.
    
    Could you take a look at merging one of the pending fixes?
    
    It is broad and silent, so it is easy to miss.
    [embed] ACP image content blocks dropped before API call (persist_user_mess... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/44242
    [embed] fix(agent): skip persist_user_message override when content is a mu... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44247
    [embed] fix: preserve ACP multimodal content during persistence by Rivuza ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/44249

[2026-06-17T18:28:30.962000+00:00] lordbartok (id=1516872184730091670)
    fix(egress): address @weklund review — pin SHA, guard CA mount, 2yr CA, host-svc doc — #47969
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47969
    
    Problem: Review pass on the iron-proxy firewall (#30179) surfaced 5 real egress-isolation seams — an implicit HTTPS_PROXY passthrough overriding the container proxy, the binary SHA being a no-op against a compromised download channel, docker_extra_args not checking -v/--mount (rogue CA could win), a 10yr CA, and an undocumented raw-TCP path to host services via host.docker.internal.
    
    Fix: Filter passthrough proxy vars in the init-session env; pin per-platform SHA-256 in source as the authoritative trust anchor (checksums.txt demoted to a cross-check); scan -v/--volume/--mount and refuse mounts onto the egress CA path; drop CA validity to 2yr; document the host-service exposure + mitigation.
    
    Targets feat/iron-proxy (#30179). +7 regression tests · iron_proxy (92) + docker (76) green · ruff clean. Thanks @weklund for the thorough review 🙏
    [embed] fix(egress): filter implicit passthrough proxy vars in init-session... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47969

[2026-06-17T22:38:57.576000+00:00] lordbartok (id=1516935210917298368)
    fix(egress): maxpetrusenko P1/P2 — fail-closed secrets, NODE_OPTIONS conflict, GPG verify — #48076
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48076
    
    Follow-up to @maxpetrusenko's security review on the iron-proxy egress firewall (#30179). The 3 P0s are already handled by @kuangmi-bit's fix/iron-proxy-p0s, so this PR owns the 3 P1s + the P2 that were still open. Stacked on feat/iron-proxy.
    
    P1 — fail-closed secrets (the important one). Secret rules now emit replace.require: true. Without it, a real provider key that a sandbox process sent directly to an allowlisted host — bypassing the minted proxy token — would still sail through the proxy boundary. With require, iron-proxy returns ActionReject when a mapped host is hit and no token swap fired. The whole token-swap firewall could otherwise be silently no-op'd by a process that just used the real key.
    
    ⚠️ The subtle part: Max cited the v0.45 upstream docs, but we're pinned to v0.39, and iron-proxy decodes config with KnownFields(true) (strict) — an unknown field hard-fails every proxy startup. So I pulled the actual v0.39.0 source and confirmed replaceConfig.Require (yaml:"require,omitempty") exists and is enforced in TransformRequest before adding it. Safe on our pin, no version bump required.
    
    P1 — NODE_OPTIONS CA-mode conflict. The append-merge only de-duped exact tokens, so an operator's --use-bundled-ca could survive next to the egress-required --use-openssl-ca — leaving Node's final trust behavior dependent on flag order. Egress flag now wins deterministically (conflicting CA-mode flag stripped + warned); unrelated tuning like --max-old-space-size is preserved.
    
    P1 — release-channel tamper. install_iron_proxy now GPG-verifies checksums.txt against checksums.txt.asc + the bundled public-key.asc in an ephemeral keyring. SHA-256 only protects the archive if checksums.txt itself came from a clean channel. Best-effort: degrades with a warning when gpg/sig assets are absent (SHA-256 stays enforced), but a present-but-invalid signature hard-fails the install.
    
    P2 — threat-model wording. Scoped "tokens only work from behind the proxy" → "behind the configured trusted proxy boundary" (CA private key + proxy endpoint integrity are part of that boundary) across the docstring, config, and egress docs, with a MITRE ATT&CK T1588.004 AiTM note.
    
    +259/−10 · iron_proxy 94 + docker 74 green · ruff clean. Left the v0.39→v0.45 bump as a separate follow-up since it's a behavior-affecting binary change that deserves its own validation. cc @maxpetrusenko — thanks for the well-cited review 🙏
    [embed] fix(egress): maxpetrusenko P1/P2 — fail-closed secrets, NODE_OPTI... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48076

[2026-06-17T23:17:03.988000+00:00] aleksandr_1 (id=1516944800824164482)
    Sir, two PRs that harden prefix cache preservation.
    
    **1. PR #47890** guards `_restore_or_build_system_prompt` against concurrent rebuilds on the gateway path. Two simultaneous turns for the same session could both trigger a full system-prompt rebuild, fire `on_session_start` twice, and waste the cache. Fixed with a per-session lock and double-checked locking guard.
    **P2** - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47890
    
    **2. PR #47915** extends `_stored_prompt_matches_runtime` to detect cwd and runtime surface drift. A stored prompt could silently be reused as a valid cache hit even after a compression chain changed the working directory or desktop/terminal surface. Both checks are no-ops when the relevant marker is absent, so legacy prompts remain fully compatible.
    **P2** - https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/47915
    [reply to teknium msg=1516774553135616123]

[2026-06-18T00:27:40.045000+00:00] lordbartok (id=1516962568134983875)
    <@387972437901312000> on "why is it failing to download" — it mostly isn't a download failure. The recurring "The specified electronDist does not exist" is a **static-path vs npm-workspace-hoisting** mismatch: `build.electronDist` was a hardcoded relative path, but npm non-deterministically puts the workspace-only `electron` devDep either under `apps/desktop/node_modules/electron` (nested) or hoists it to the repo-root `node_modules/electron`. A static path only matches one layout, so a clean install — and especially a re-install during update — points electronDist at an empty path half the time. That's why it "fails after update" and why a manual symlink fixes it until the next install wipes it.
    
    Two lesser faults wore the same error: (a) genuinely region-blocked GitHub release downloads (already mitigated by the npmmirror fallback), and (b) update rebuilds inheriting `NODE_ENV=production` / `NPM_CONFIG_OMIT=dev`, stripping TypeScript/Vite.
    
    Good news: already fixed on main by #48091 (merged earlier today — supersedes #48081/#48082). It drops the static electronDist and resolves it at build time via `require.resolve("electron/package.json")` in `scripts/run-electron-builder.cjs`, so it's hoist-proof for both layouts + self-heals a blocked install. #48003 is superseded by it. Issue #47917 should be safe to close once folks confirm on the next build. 🎻

[2026-06-18T00:48:59.786000+00:00] maxfreedompollard (id=1516967935758045184)
    <@387972437901312000> 
    
    On Windows with no timezone configured, the gateway writes message timestamps with a multi-word zone name like "Pacific Daylight Time", but the regular expression that reads them back forbids spaces, so the code cannot recognize a timestamp it wrote itself and leaves those prefixes unstripped and undeduplicated. The fix widens that one regular expression to accept space separated zone names, so the reader and the writer agree again. 
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48134
    [embed] fix(gateway): strip timestamp prefixes with multi-word timezone nam... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48134

[2026-06-18T01:32:15.898000+00:00] maxfreedompollard (id=1516978824640729259)
    Although this one affects every system:
    
    It fixes the shared strip_markdown helper used by five plain text gateway adapters (SMS, iMessage, Feishu, QQ Bot, Photon), where the asterisk emphasis patterns silently paired up and ate * bullet markers and any text holding two or more literal asterisks (like a * b * c), mangling ordinary agent output on every operating system by default. The fix gives the two star patterns the same inside edge guards the underscore patterns already had, so bullet lists and literal asterisks now pass through untouched while real *italic* and **bold** still get stripped.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48151
    [embed] fix(gateway): keep markdown bullet lists and literal asterisks in p... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48151

[2026-06-18T09:36:37.785000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1517100719029878894)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48347
    
    **Problem**: Users get stuck in permanent deadlock when concurrent commands swap the session guard during message processing.
    Old task's finally block deleted _session_tasks before _release_session_guard, leaving an orphaned guard with no detectable owner.
    Fixed by only deleting _session_tasks when the guard can also be released; done tasks are left for staleness detection.
    [embed] fix(gateway): prevent stale session lock from orphaned guard swap (... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48347

[2026-06-18T11:51:17.830000+00:00] kyssta69 (id=1517134609194684556)
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48391
    
    **Problem**: TUI model picker was rewriting the entire config.yaml via yaml.dump when persisting a global model switch, destroying comments and potentially clobbering unrelated config sections like auxiliary slots and fallback tiers. The root cause was _persist_model_switch using save_config() (full rewrite) instead of save_config_value() (targeted key update). Changed to use save_config_value() with atomic_roundtrip_yaml_update, matching the CLI approach.
    [embed] fix(tui): use targeted save_config_value for model persistence (#48... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48391

    **Problem**: Dashboard crashes on Chinese Windows (GBK locale) with UnicodeDecodeError when serving index.html or CSS. Two read_text() calls in web_server.py were missing encoding='utf-8', so Python used the system default GBK codec. Added explicit UTF-8 encoding to both bare read_text() calls.
    [embed] fix(dashboard): add encoding='utf-8' to bare read_text() calls (#48... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48392

[2026-06-18T11:59:49.464000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1517136755143213078)
    PR #48393 — fix(desktop): dispose open PTY sessions in before-quit handler <@387972437901312000> 
    
    The before-quit handler kills the Python backend and cleans up watchers but never disposes live PTY sessions. When Electron tears down the environment, node-pty's ThreadSafeFunction fires on a half-destroyed runtime → SIGABRT crash (node-pty#904). Happens on every quit/update-restart if a terminal tab is open.
    
    Fix: sweep terminalSessions and call the existing disposeTerminalSession() before environment teardown. 6 lines, single file.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48393
    [embed] fix(desktop): dispose open PTY sessions in before-quit handler by s... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48393

[2026-06-18T14:37:53.059000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1517176532223922178)
    PR #48461 — fix(bootstrap-installer): prevent panic when truncating multi-byte UTF-8 output
    
    truncate() slices by byte offset (&s[..max]), which panics if max lands inside a multi-byte UTF-8 character. The install scripts emit ⚠, ✓, → throughout, so a failed manifest with >4000 bytes of output can crash the installer silently — the user never sees the actual error. Walks back to the nearest char boundary using is_char_boundary() (stable since Rust 1.0, MSRV 1.77 compatible).
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48461
    [embed] fix(bootstrap-installer): prevent panic when truncating multi-byte ... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48461

[2026-06-18T16:44:14.750000+00:00] yap.bi (id=1517208332140548196)
    Opened a fix for #48510 — on Windows, codex-runtime reported "codex CLI not found" 
    even when installed, because npm installs codex.CMD and subprocess.run() doesn't 
    resolve .CMD without shell=True. Fix resolves the binary via shutil.which() at both 
    the version check and the actual spawn.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48522
    
    
    Opened a fix for #48504 — Desktop remote mode proved the backend ready, then failed 
    with "Timed out after 15000ms" while loading profiles. Profile-heavy installs take 
    ~35s but every authenticated call fell back to a hardcoded 15s timeout. Bumped it to 
    60s; the readiness/auth probes keep their own short timeouts so a down backend is 
    still caught fast.
    
    PR: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48526

[2026-06-19T02:21:03.098000+00:00] srojk34 (id=1517353490072998079)
    Found that /resume and /branch call end_session() without flushing un-persisted messages first — same bug that #47202 fixed in compress_context() and /new. When a turn is interrupted mid-flight and the user runs /resume or /branch, messages from that turn are silently lost. Added the same best-effort flush before end_session() in both commands + regression tests.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48764
    [embed] fix(cli): flush un-persisted messages before /resume and /branch en... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/48764

[2026-06-19T07:07:45.420000+00:00] godsboy7777 (id=1517425641841098872)
    Hey <@690519146701783042>, when you have a gap, could you please give PR #15624 a fresh review? I have been working with the mem0 team so they update PR and we get this merged as it affects everyone using their plugin on hermes.
    
    https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15624
    
    This is the Mem0 v3 / OSS mode PR from Kartik. It matters because the current Hermes Mem0 plugin is still on Mem0 v2 semantics, where memories written with both `user_id` and `agent_id` are not returned by a plain `user_id` read. Mem0 support confirmed this is expected v2 behaviour, and that v3 fixes it by making `agent_id` attribution rather than a narrower recall scope.
    
    So this PR should let Hermes drop the local OR-filter workaround and rely on `{"user_id": ...}` reads again once merged.
    
    The cleanup items from your review look addressed now:
    - `mem0ai>=2.0.4,<3` upper bound is in place
    - telemetry opt-out docs are now in both the plugin README and `website/docs/user-guide/features/memory-providers.md`
    - qdrant path test is now root-safe
    - PostHog key is behind `MEM0_POSTHOG_KEY`
    - async exit flush is in place
    - OSS double-telemetry handling is in place
    - AUTHOR_MAP entries and version bump are in
    - branch is mergeable and no longer behind main
    
    I also checked the latest PR head locally and ran:
    
    `uv run pytest tests/plugins/memory -q`
    
    Result:
    
    `346 passed in 98.85s`
    
    Would appreciate a re-review so this can get unstuck and merged. It removes a real practical recall issue for Hermes users on Mem0.
    [embed] feat(mem0): v3 API, OSS mode, update/delete tools, telemetry & revi... https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15624
